[1] David convened every official in Israel to Jerusalem — the tribal chiefs, the commanders of the rotating divisions serving the king, the officers over thousands and hundreds, the stewards of all the king's property and livestock, along with his sons, the palace officials, the elite warriors, every man of standing.

[2] King David rose to his feet and spoke: "Listen to me, my brothers, my people. It was in my heart to build a resting place for the ark of the LORD's covenant — a footstool for our God. I made all the preparations to build.

[3] But God said to me, 'You will not build a house for my name. You are a man of war. You have shed blood.'

[4] Yet the LORD, the God of Israel, chose me out of my whole father's house to be king over Israel forever. He chose Judah as the ruling tribe, and within Judah my father's house, and from my father's sons he was pleased to make me king over all Israel.

[5] And from all my sons — the LORD has given me many — he chose Solomon, my son, to sit on the throne of the LORD's kingdom over Israel.

[6] He told me: 'Solomon your son will build my house and my courts. I have chosen him as my son, and I will be his father.

[7] I will establish his kingdom forever — if he holds firm to my commands and my rulings, as he does today.'

[8] So now, before the eyes of all Israel — the assembly of the LORD — and within hearing of our God: keep and seek out every command of the LORD your God, so that you may possess this good land and leave it as an inheritance to your children after you, forever.

[9] And you, Solomon my son — know the God of your father. Serve him with a whole heart and a willing mind, because the LORD searches every heart and discerns every intention behind every thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you. If you abandon him, he will cast you off forever.

[10] So consider this carefully: the LORD has chosen you to build a house for his sanctuary. Be strong. Do it."

[11] Then David handed Solomon the blueprints — the portico, the buildings, the storerooms, the upper chambers, the inner rooms, the room for the atonement cover —

[12] every plan the Spirit had placed in his mind: the courts of the LORD's house, all the surrounding rooms, the treasuries of God's house, the treasuries for the sacred dedications,

[13] the arrangements of priests and Levites in their divisions, all the work of service in the LORD's house, every vessel for that service.

[14] He specified the weight of gold for each gold vessel, the weight of silver for each silver vessel —

[15] the weight for the gold lampstands and their gold lamps, weight for each lampstand and its lamps; for the silver lampstands the weight for each, lampstand by lampstand, according to its use;

[16] the gold by weight for each table of the showbread, and silver for the silver tables;

[17] pure gold for the forks, basins, and pitchers; weighed amounts of gold for each gold dish and silver for each silver dish;

[18] refined gold by weight for the altar of incense. And he gave him the plan for the chariot — the golden cherubim, wings spread, covering the ark of the LORD's covenant.

[19] "All of this," David said, "the LORD made me understand in writing — his hand upon me. Every detail of the design."

[20] Then David said to Solomon his son: "Be strong. Be courageous. Do it. Do not fear. Do not lose your nerve. The LORD God — my God — is with you. He will not fail you. He will not abandon you. Not until every piece of the work for the service of the LORD's house is finished.

[21] The divisions of priests and Levites are ready for all the service of God's house. Every willing craftsman of every skill stands with you for every task. The officials and the people — every word you speak, they will obey."

[1] Then King David addressed the whole assembly: "My son Solomon — the one God has chosen — is young and untested, and the work is massive. This palace isn't for a man; it's for the LORD God.

[2] With everything in me I've stockpiled for the house of my God: gold for the gold pieces, silver for the silver, bronze for the bronze, iron for the iron, wood for the wood, onyx stones and settings, turquoise, multicolored stones, every kind of precious gem, and marble in abundance.

[3] On top of all that — because I love the house of my God — I'm giving from my own personal treasury, my private gold and silver, over and above everything I've already provided for the holy house:

[4] three thousand talents of gold from Ophir, seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the buildings,

[5] gold for the gold work, silver for the silver, and for every craft of every artisan. Now — who is ready to consecrate himself today to the LORD?"

[6] And they came forward — the heads of the families, the leaders of Israel's tribes, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, the overseers of the king's work — and they gave willingly.

[7] For the service of God's house they gave: five thousand talents of gold and ten thousand darics, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze, one hundred thousand talents of iron.

[8] Whoever had precious stones handed them over to the treasury of the LORD's house, into the keeping of Jehiel the Gershonite.

[9] The people celebrated their own generosity — because they had given freely, wholeheartedly, to the LORD. And King David rejoiced — overflowed with joy.

[10] Then David blessed the LORD in front of the entire assembly. He said: "Blessed are you, LORD, God of our father Israel, from forever to forever.

[11] Yours, LORD — the greatness, the power, the glory, the victory, the majesty. Everything in the heavens and on the earth is yours. Yours, LORD, is the kingdom; you are lifted high above all.

[12] Wealth and honor come from you. You rule everything. Strength and power are in your hand — you decide who rises, who holds together.

[13] So now, our God, we thank you. We praise your magnificent name.

[14] But who am I, and who are these people, that we could possibly give like this? Everything comes from you. We've only handed back to you what was already yours.

[15] We're foreigners in your sight, drifters — like every ancestor before us. Our days on earth are a shadow, and there's no holding on.

[16] LORD our God — all this abundance we've gathered to build a house for your holy name — it came from your hand. All of it is yours.

[17] I know, my God, that you test the heart and you love integrity. With an honest heart I've given all this freely, and now I've watched your people here, gathered, giving to you with the same joy.

[18] LORD — God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers — keep this forever in the hearts of your people. Anchor their hearts to you.

[19] And give my son Solomon a whole heart to keep your commands, your statutes, your decrees — to do all of it, to build the palace I have prepared for."

[20] Then David said to the entire assembly, "Bless the LORD your God." So the whole assembly blessed the LORD, the God of their fathers — bowed low, dropped to the ground before the LORD and the king.

[21] The next day they sacrificed to the LORD and offered burnt offerings: a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings — sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.

[22] They ate and drank in the LORD's presence that day with enormous joy. Then they made Solomon, son of David, king a second time, anointed him before the LORD as ruler, and Zadok as priest.

[23] So Solomon took the throne of the LORD as king in place of his father David. He prospered. All Israel obeyed him.

[24] Every official, every warrior, every one of King David's sons pledged loyalty to King Solomon.

[25] The LORD lifted Solomon high in the eyes of all Israel and gave him royal majesty unlike any king of Israel before him.

[26] David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.

[27] His reign over Israel lasted forty years — seven in Hebron, thirty-three in Jerusalem.

[28] He died at a good old age, full of years, wealth, and honor. His son Solomon reigned after him.

[29] The acts of King David, beginning to end, are recorded in the chronicles of Samuel the seer, the chronicles of Nathan the prophet, and the chronicles of Gad the seer —

[30] together with the full account of his reign, his might, and everything that swept over him, Israel, and all the kingdoms of the surrounding lands.

[1] Paul — summoned by God's will to be an apostle of Christ Jesus — and Sosthenes, our brother,

[2] to God's church in Corinth: to those made holy in Christ Jesus, called to be his set-apart people, alongside everyone everywhere who calls on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ — their Lord and ours.

[3] Grace to you. Peace to you. From God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

[4] I thank my God constantly for you, for the grace he poured out on you in Christ Jesus.

[5] In him you have been made rich — in every kind of speech, in every kind of knowledge.

[6] The testimony about Christ took root in you and held.

[7] So now you lack nothing — not a single gift — as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be unveiled.

[8] And he himself will hold you steady to the end, so that on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ, no accusation will stick.

[9] God is faithful — the God who called you into partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.

[10] Now I'm begging you, brothers and sisters, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: speak with one voice. No fractures among you. Be knit back together — same mind, same purpose.

[11] Because Chloe's people have told me plainly: there are quarrels breaking out among you.

[12] Here's what I mean. One of you says, "I follow Paul." Another, "I follow Apollos." Another, "I follow Cephas." Another, "I follow Christ."

[13] Has Christ been carved into pieces? Was Paul crucified for you? Were you baptized into the name of Paul?

[14] I thank God I baptized none of you — except Crispus and Gaius —

[15] so nobody can claim they were baptized into my name.

[16] (Oh — I did baptize the household of Stephanas. Beyond that, I can't recall baptizing anyone else.)

[17] Christ didn't send me to baptize. He sent me to announce the good news — and not with clever rhetoric, or the cross of Christ would be drained of its power.

[18] Because the message of the cross sounds like nonsense to those headed for ruin. But to us being rescued, it is the power of God himself.

[19] As it stands written: *I will demolish the wisdom of the wise. I will discard the intelligence of the intelligent.*

[20] So where's the philosopher now? Where's the scholar? Where's the great debater of this age? Hasn't God exposed the world's wisdom as foolishness?

[21] Here's the design: since the world, with all its wisdom, couldn't find God through wisdom, God decided to save those who believe through what looks like foolishness — the message we proclaim.

[22] Jews demand miraculous signs. Greeks chase wisdom.

[23] We proclaim a crucified Messiah — a scandal to Jews, lunacy to Gentiles —

[24] but to those who are called, both Jew and Greek, this same Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.

[25] Because God's "foolishness" is wiser than human wisdom. God's "weakness" is stronger than human strength.

[26] Look at yourselves, brothers and sisters — look at who you were when he called you. Not many of you were intellectuals by worldly standards. Not many were powerful. Not many came from prestigious families.

[27] But God deliberately chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise. He chose the weak things to shame the strong.

[28] He chose what the world dismisses, what it despises, what it counts as nothing — to nullify what the world counts as something —

[29] so that no human being can strut in his presence.

[30] You are in Christ Jesus because of him. And Christ has become for us wisdom from God — our righteousness, our holiness, our liberation —

[31] so that, as it stands written, *whoever boasts, let them boast in the Lord.*

[1] When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didn't show up armed with eloquence or impressive philosophy to announce God's mystery. [2] I made one decision and stuck to it: while I was with you, I would know nothing except Jesus Christ — and him crucified. [3] I came to you weak. Afraid. Trembling, even. [4] My message and my preaching weren't dressed up in clever, persuasive arguments. They came with a demonstration — Spirit and power — [5] so that your faith would rest on God's power, not on human wisdom.

[6] Now, we do speak wisdom among the mature — but not the wisdom of this age or of the powers running this age, powers headed for oblivion. [7] No, we speak God's wisdom: hidden, mysterious, the wisdom God set aside before time began for our glory. [8] None of the rulers of this age caught on. If they had, they wouldn't have crucified the Lord of glory. [9] As it's written:

What no eye has seen,
what no ear has heard,
what no human mind has imagined —
this is what God has prepared for those who love him.

[10] And God has unveiled it to us through the Spirit. The Spirit plumbs everything — even the depths of God himself. [11] Think about it: who knows what's going on inside a person except that person's own spirit? In the same way, no one grasps the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God. [12] And we haven't received the spirit of this world — we've received the Spirit who comes from God, so we can actually understand what God has freely given us. [13] This is what we speak: not in words taught by human wisdom, but in words taught by the Spirit — matching spiritual realities to spiritual words.

[14] The person without the Spirit can't receive what comes from God's Spirit. To them it sounds like nonsense, and they can't understand it, because it has to be discerned spiritually. [15] The spiritual person, on the other hand, evaluates everything but is not subject to anyone else's verdict. [16] For who has ever known the mind of the Lord well enough to instruct him?

But we — we have the mind of Christ.

[1] Brothers and sisters, I couldn't talk to you as spiritual people. I had to talk to you as people still wired to the flesh — as infants in Christ.

[2] I fed you milk, not solid food. You weren't ready for it. And honestly? You're still not ready.

[3] You're still operating on raw human impulse. When jealousy and infighting run the room, what does that tell you? That you're still running on the same fuel as everyone else.

[4] One of you says, "I'm with Paul." Another says, "I'm with Apollos." Doesn't that prove you're just being ordinary?

[5] So what is Apollos, really? What is Paul? Servants — that's it. Servants through whom you came to believe, each of us doing the work the Lord handed us.

[6] I planted. Apollos watered. But God — God made it grow.

[7] So the planter is nothing. The waterer is nothing. Only God matters — the One making things grow.

[8] Planter and waterer are on the same team, and each will be paid according to what they actually did.

[9] We're God's coworkers. You're God's field. You're God's building.

[10] By the grace God gave me, I laid the foundation like a master builder, and someone else is building on it. But every builder had better watch how they build.

[11] Because no one can lay any other foundation than the one already laid — and that foundation is Jesus Christ.

[12] Now if anyone builds on that foundation using gold, silver, precious stones — or wood, hay, straw —

[13] the work will show itself for what it is. The Day will expose it. Fire will reveal it. Fire will test what every person's work is actually made of.

[14] If what someone built survives the flames, they get the reward.

[15] If it burns up, they take the loss. They themselves will still be saved — but barely, like someone running out of a burning house.

[16] Don't you get it? You are God's temple. God's Spirit lives in you.

[17] Anyone who destroys God's temple — God will destroy them. Because God's temple is holy, and that temple is you.

[18] Stop fooling yourselves. If any of you thinks you're wise by the world's standards, become a fool — that's how you become actually wise.

[19] The wisdom of this world is sheer foolishness to God. As Scripture says: "He catches the wise in their own cleverness."

[20] And again: "The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise — knows they amount to nothing."

[21] So stop bragging about which human leader you follow. Everything already belongs to you —

[22] Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, the present, the future — all of it is yours,

[23] and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.

[1] So here's how to think of us: servants of the Messiah, stewards entrusted with the mysteries of God. [2] And the one thing required of a steward is this — that they prove trustworthy. [3] As for me, it matters very little how you assess me, or what any human court decides. I don't even pass judgment on myself. [4] My conscience is clear, but that doesn't make me innocent. The Lord is the one who judges me. [5] So stop rendering verdicts before the time is up. Wait until the Lord comes. He will floodlight what darkness has hidden and expose the motives buried in every heart. Then — and only then — each person will receive their commendation from God.

[6] I've applied all this to Apollos and myself, brothers and sisters, as an example for your sake — so you can learn through us what it means to "not go beyond what is written," so none of you will swell up with pride, championing one leader against another. [7] Who made you so special? What do you have that you didn't receive? And if you received it, why do you strut around as if you earned it?

[8] Already you're full! Already you're rich! You've ascended to your thrones without us! How I wish you really had — then we could reign alongside you! [9] Because the way I see it, God has paraded us apostles at the end of the procession, like men sentenced to die in the arena. We've become a spectacle — to the cosmos, to angels, to humanity. [10] We're fools for the Messiah; you're so wise in him. We're weak; you're strong. You bask in honor; we eat dishonor. [11] To this very hour we go hungry and thirsty, dressed in rags, beaten, homeless. [12] We work ourselves raw with our own hands. Cursed, we bless. Persecuted, we endure. [13] Slandered, we speak kindly in return. We've become the world's garbage, the scrapings off the bottom of everything — and that's still our status today.

[14] I'm not writing this to humiliate you. I'm warning you as my beloved children. [15] Because even if you had ten thousand tutors in the Messiah, you don't have many fathers. I'm the one who fathered you in the Messiah Jesus through the gospel. [16] So I'm begging you — imitate me. [17] That's why I sent Timothy to you. He's my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, and he'll remind you of my ways in the Messiah Jesus — the same things I teach everywhere in every church.

[18] Some of you have gotten arrogant, assuming I'm not coming back. [19] But I am coming — soon, if the Lord wills. And when I do, I won't be impressed by the words of these inflated talkers. I'll see what power they actually carry. [20] Because the kingdom of God isn't about talk. It's about power. [21] So what do you want? Should I come to you with a rod — or with love and a gentle spirit?

[1] Word is going around about sexual immorality among you — and not just any kind, but the sort that even the pagans don't stomach: a man sleeping with his father's wife.

[2] And you're proud of yourselves? You ought to be wrecked. You ought to be in mourning until the man doing this is removed from your midst.

[3] As for me — absent in body, present in spirit — I've already passed judgment on the one who did this, as if I were standing right there.

[4] When you gather in the name of the Lord Jesus, and my spirit is with you, and the power of our Lord Jesus is present —

[5] hand this man over to Satan. Let the flesh be destroyed so the spirit can be saved on the day of the Lord.

[6] Your boasting is grotesque. Don't you understand? A pinch of yeast works its way through the whole batch.

[7] Clean out the old yeast. Become what you already are — a fresh batch, unleavened. Because Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.

[8] So let's keep the feast — not with the old yeast, the yeast of malice and evil, but with bread that has no yeast in it at all: sincerity, truth.

[9] In my earlier letter I told you not to associate with the sexually immoral.

[10] I didn't mean the immoral of this world — or the greedy, or swindlers, or idolaters. To avoid them you'd have to leave the planet.

[11] What I meant was this: don't associate with anyone who claims the name "brother" and yet lives as sexually immoral, greedy, idolatrous, abusive, drunk, a swindler. Don't even share a meal with someone like that.

[12] What business do I have judging outsiders? You judge those inside.

[13] God will judge the outsiders. "Drive the evil one out from among you."

[1] When one of you has a grievance against another, how dare you drag it before pagan judges instead of bringing it to God's people? [2] Don't you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world itself comes under your verdict, are you really unqualified to handle these trivial disputes? [3] Don't you know we will judge angels? How much more, then, the affairs of this life? [4] So when you have ordinary cases to settle, why hand them over to people the church doesn't even respect? [5] I'm saying this to shame you. Is there really not one wise person among you capable of arbitrating between brothers? [6] Instead, one believer takes another to court—and does it in front of unbelievers.

[7] The fact that you have lawsuits among you at all is already a defeat. Why not just accept the wrong? Why not let yourself be cheated? [8] Instead, you're the ones doing the wronging, the cheating—and to your own brothers.

[9] Don't you know that the unjust will not inherit the kingdom of God? Stop deceiving yourselves. No one who lives in sexual sin, no idolater, no adulterer, no man who gives himself to other men or takes other men into himself, [10] no thief, no greedy person, no drunk, no slanderer, no swindler—none of them will inherit the kingdom of God. [11] And some of you were exactly that. But you were washed clean. You were set apart. You were declared righteous—in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.

[12] "Everything is permitted to me"—but not everything is good for me. "Everything is permitted to me"—but I will not be enslaved by anything. [13] "Food for the stomach and the stomach for food"—and God will do away with both. But the body is not made for sexual immorality. It's made for the Lord, and the Lord for the body. [14] God raised the Lord, and by his power he will raise us too.

[15] Don't you realize your bodies are members of Christ himself? So shall I take what belongs to Christ and join it to a prostitute? Never. [16] Don't you know that whoever joins himself to a prostitute becomes one body with her? Scripture says it: "The two will become one flesh." [17] But whoever joins himself to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.

[18] Run from sexual sin. Every other sin a person commits is outside the body—but the sexually immoral person sins against his own body. [19] Don't you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit, who lives in you, whom you received from God? You are not your own. [20] You were bought, and the price was steep. So honor God with your body.

[1] Now, about what you wrote me: yes, it's good for a man to stay unattached to a woman. [2] But because sexual immorality is everywhere, each man should have his own wife, and each woman her own husband. [3] The husband owes his wife what's hers; the wife owes her husband the same. [4] A wife's body isn't hers alone—it belongs to her husband too. And a husband's body isn't his alone—it belongs to his wife. [5] Don't withhold yourselves from each other, unless you've both agreed to it for a season, to give yourselves to prayer. Then come back together, so Satan can't exploit your lack of self-control. [6] I'm saying this as a concession, not a command. [7] Honestly, I wish everyone could be like me. But each person has their own gift from God—one this way, one that.

[8] To the unmarried and the widows I say: it's good to stay single, like me. [9] But if you can't restrain yourselves, marry. Better to marry than to burn.

[10] To the married I give this charge—not from me, but from the Lord: a wife must not separate from her husband. [11] If she does, she's to stay unmarried or be reconciled. And a husband must not divorce his wife.

[12] To the rest, this is from me, not the Lord: if a believer has a wife who doesn't believe and she's willing to stay, he shouldn't divorce her. [13] And if a woman has an unbelieving husband who's willing to stay, she shouldn't divorce him. [14] Because the unbelieving husband is set apart through his wife, and the unbelieving wife through her husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean—but as it is, they're holy. [15] But if the unbeliever leaves, let them go. The brother or sister isn't bound in such cases. God has called us to peace. [16] After all, wife—how do you know? Maybe you'll save your husband. Husband—how do you know? Maybe you'll save your wife.

[17] Beyond that: everyone should live the life the Lord assigned them, the situation God called them into. This is what I lay down in every church. [18] Were you circumcised when called? Don't undo it. Were you uncircumcised? Don't get circumcised. [19] Circumcision is nothing. Uncircumcision is nothing. Keeping God's commands—that's everything. [20] Each person should remain in the condition they were in when called. [21] Were you a slave when called? Don't let it eat at you—though if you can gain your freedom, take it. [22] Because the slave called in the Lord is the Lord's freed person; and the free person called is Christ's slave. [23] You were bought at a price—don't become slaves of men. [24] Brothers and sisters, whatever condition you were in when called, stay there with God.

[25] About the unmarried: I have no command from the Lord, but I'll offer my judgment as one whom the Lord has made trustworthy. [26] Given the pressure of the present crisis, I think it's good for a person to stay as they are. [27] Are you bound to a wife? Don't seek release. Are you free of a wife? Don't seek one. [28] But if you do marry, you haven't sinned. And if a virgin marries, she hasn't sinned. It's just that those who marry will face hardships in this life, and I want to spare you.

[29] Here's what I'm saying, brothers and sisters: the time has been compressed. From now on, those who have wives should live as if they didn't; [30] those who mourn, as if they weren't mourning; those who celebrate, as if they weren't celebrating; those who buy, as if they owned nothing; [31] those who deal with the world, as if they had no stake in it. Because the shape of this world is passing away.

[32] I want you free of anxiety. The unmarried man concerns himself with the Lord's affairs—how to please the Lord. [33] The married man concerns himself with worldly things—how to please his wife— [34] and his interests are divided. The unmarried woman or virgin concerns herself with the Lord's affairs, devoted in body and spirit. The married woman concerns herself with worldly things—how to please her husband. [35] I'm telling you this for your own good, not to tie you up, but so you can live with dignity and undistracted devotion to the Lord.

[36] If a man thinks he's not treating his fiancée honorably—if his passions are strong and it has to happen—let him do what he wants. He isn't sinning. Let them marry. [37] But the man who stands firm in his heart, under no compulsion, with full control over his own desire, who has resolved in his heart to keep her as she is—he does well. [38] So the one who marries his fiancée does well, and the one who doesn't does even better.

[39] A wife is bound as long as her husband lives. If he dies, she's free to marry whoever she wants—only in the Lord. [40] But in my judgment, she's happier if she stays as she is. And I think I too have the Spirit of God.

[1] Now about food sacrificed to idols. We all have knowledge on this, sure. But knowledge inflates; love builds. [2] Anyone who thinks they've got something figured out doesn't yet know the way they need to know. [3] But the person who loves God — that one is known by him.

[4] So, on eating food offered to idols: we know an idol is nothing in this world, and there is no God but one. [5] Yes, there are so-called gods scattered through heaven and earth — plenty of "gods," plenty of "lords" — [6] but for us there is one God, the Father, the source of everything and the reason we exist; and one Lord, Jesus the Anointed, through whom everything came to be and through whom we live.

[7] But not everyone has caught up to this knowledge. Some are so accustomed to idols that when they eat such food, they still feel it as something offered to a real god — and their conscience, being fragile, gets stained. [8] Food won't bring us closer to God. We're no worse off if we don't eat, no better off if we do.

[9] Just be careful: this freedom of yours must not become a trap for someone still finding their footing. [10] Imagine someone with a weak conscience sees you — you, with all your knowledge — reclining at a meal in an idol's temple. Won't they be emboldened to eat the same, against their own conscience? [11] And that brother or sister — the one Christ died for — gets shipwrecked by your "knowledge." [12] When you sin against them like that, hammering on their fragile conscience, you sin against Christ himself.

[13] So here's where I land: if food trips up my brother or sister, I'll never eat meat again. Not ever. I will not be the one who knocks them down.

[1] Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven't I seen Jesus our Lord with my own eyes? Aren't you yourselves the proof of my work in him?

[2] Even if others refuse to recognize me as an apostle, I am one to you — because you exist. You are the seal stamped on my apostleship.

[3] So here is my defense to those who put me on trial:

[4] Don't we have the right to eat and drink at the church's expense?

[5] Don't we have the right to bring a believing wife along with us, the way the other apostles do, and the Lord's brothers, and Cephas?

[6] Or is it just Barnabas and me who are required to work a day job to fund our ministry?

[7] Who ever served as a soldier and paid his own way? Who plants a vineyard and never tastes the grapes? Who tends a flock and never drinks the milk?

[8] And I'm not just arguing from common sense — the Law says the same thing.

[9] It is written in the Law of Moses: *Don't muzzle an ox while it's threshing the grain.* Is God really preoccupied with oxen,

[10] or is he speaking for our sake? Of course it's for our sake. Whoever plows should plow in hope. Whoever threshes should thresh expecting a share of the harvest.

[11] If we sowed spiritual seed in you, is it really too much for us to reap a material harvest from you?

[12] If others have a claim on your support, don't we have a stronger one? But we have never used that right. We endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the path of the gospel of Christ.

[13] You know how this works: those who serve in the temple eat from the temple. Those who attend the altar share in what's offered on it.

[14] In the same way, the Lord directed that those who proclaim the gospel should make their living from the gospel.

[15] But I have used none of these rights. And I'm not writing this now to start collecting. I'd rather die than have anyone strip me of this boast.

[16] Because preaching the gospel is no badge of honor for me — I'm under compulsion. Disaster will fall on me if I don't preach it.

[17] If I were doing this by my own choice, I'd have earned wages. But I'm not — I've been entrusted with a commission.

[18] So what is my pay? This: that I preach the gospel free of charge, refusing to lean on the rights the gospel gives me.

[19] I'm free — beholden to no one — and yet I have made myself a slave to everyone, to win as many as I can.

[20] To the Jews I became a Jew, to win Jews. To those under the Law I became as one under the Law — though I'm not under it myself — to win those under the Law.

[21] To those outside the Law I became as one outside the Law — not that I'm outside God's law, I am under Christ's law — to win those outside the Law.

[22] To the weak I became weak, to win the weak. I have become every kind of thing to every kind of person, so that by every means possible I might save some.

[23] I do all of this for the gospel, so that I might share in its blessing.

[24] You know how it works at the stadium — every runner runs, but only one takes the prize. So run. Run to win.

[25] Every athlete in training disciplines himself in everything. They do it for a wreath that withers. We do it for one that never will.

[26] So I don't run aimlessly. I don't box like a man punching the air.

[27] I pound my body into submission and make it my servant. Otherwise, after preaching to everyone else, I myself could be disqualified.

[1] I don't want you to miss this, family: our ancestors all walked under that cloud, all passed through that sea. [2] Every one of them was baptized into Moses—drenched in cloud, immersed in sea. [3] They all ate the same supernatural food. [4] They all drank the same supernatural drink, because they were drinking from a spiritual rock that traveled with them—and that rock was Christ. [5] But here's the thing: most of them didn't make it. God was not pleased. Their bodies were scattered across the wilderness.

[6] These stories are warnings stamped for us—so we don't crave evil the way they did. [7] Don't be idolaters, like some of them were. Scripture says it plain: "The people sat down to eat and drink, then got up to party." [8] Don't sleep around, like some of them did—twenty-three thousand dropped dead in a single day. [9] Don't put Christ to the test, like some of them did—and the snakes finished them. [10] Don't grumble, like some of them did—and the destroyer came for them.

[11] All of this happened to them as a pattern, and it was written down to wake us up—we who are living at the climax of the ages. [12] So if you think you're standing firm, watch your step. You're closer to falling than you know.

[13] No temptation has come at you that isn't common to being human. And God is faithful. He won't let you be pushed past what you can handle. When the pressure comes, He'll carve out a way through—so you can stand under it.

[14] So, my beloved—run from idolatry. Run hard.

[15] I'm talking to people with sense. Weigh what I'm saying. [16] The cup of blessing we bless—isn't that a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread we break—isn't that a sharing in the body of Christ? [17] One loaf, one body—because we all eat from the same bread. [18] Look at Israel in the flesh: the ones who eat the sacrifices share in the altar, don't they?

[19] So what am I saying? That an idol is something real? That food sacrificed to an idol means anything? [20] No—what I'm saying is that what pagans sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, not to God. And I don't want you in partnership with demons. [21] You cannot drink the Lord's cup and the cup of demons. You cannot share the Lord's table and the table of demons. [22] Or are we trying to provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He is?

[23] "Everything is permitted"—but not everything is helpful. "Everything is permitted"—but not everything builds anything up. [24] Stop chasing your own good. Chase someone else's.

[25] Eat anything sold in the meat market. Don't interrogate it on grounds of conscience— [26] because the earth is the Lord's, and everything in it.

[27] If an unbeliever invites you to dinner and you want to go, eat whatever's set in front of you. Don't grill the host about it. [28] But if someone tells you, "This was offered in sacrifice"—then don't eat it. For their sake. For conscience's sake. [29] I'm not talking about your conscience—I'm talking about theirs. Because why should my freedom be put on trial by someone else's conscience? [30] If I eat with gratitude, why am I being slandered for what I thanked God for?

[31] So whether you eat, whether you drink, whether you do anything at all—do it all for the glory of God. [32] Don't be the reason anyone stumbles—not Jews, not Greeks, not the church of God. [33] That's how I live: trying to please everyone in every way, not hunting my own advantage but theirs—so that they might be saved.

[1] Imitate me, the way I imitate the Messiah.

[2] I'm proud of you for remembering me in everything and holding fast to the traditions exactly as I handed them to you.

[3] But here's what I want you to grasp: the Messiah is the head of every man, the man is the head of the woman, and God is the head of the Messiah.

[4] A man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.

[5] A woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered shames her head — it's no different than if she'd shaved it bald.

[6] If she won't cover up, then let her go all the way and cut her hair off. But if cutting or shaving is disgraceful for a woman, then let her cover her head.

[7] A man shouldn't cover his head, because he is the image and glory of God — and the woman is the glory of the man.

[8] Man didn't come out of woman; woman came out of man.

[9] Man wasn't created for woman's sake; woman was created for man's sake.

[10] That's why a woman ought to have authority on her head — because of the angels.

[11] Still, in the Lord, neither is woman independent of man nor man of woman.

[12] As woman came from man, so now man comes through woman — and all of it comes from God.

[13] Judge for yourselves: is it fitting for a woman to pray to God uncovered?

[14] Doesn't nature itself teach you that long hair on a man dishonors him,

[15] but long hair on a woman is her glory? Her hair was given to her as a covering.

[16] If anyone wants to fight about this — we don't have such a practice, and neither do God's assemblies.

[17] Now to the next matter — and here I cannot praise you, because your gatherings are doing more harm than good.

[18] First off, I hear that when you assemble as a church, there are factions among you — and I half believe it.

[19] There have to be divisions, I suppose, so that those who pass the test become recognizable.

[20] So when you come together, it isn't really the Lord's Supper you're eating.

[21] Because each of you dives into your own meal first — one goes home hungry, another gets drunk.

[22] Don't you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise God's church and humiliate those who have nothing? What do you want me to say? Praise you? Not for this.

[23] What I handed on to you, I received from the Lord himself: on the night he was handed over, the Lord Jesus took bread,

[24] gave thanks, broke it, and said, "This is my body, given for you. Do this to remember me."

[25] In the same way, after supper, he took the cup and said, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Whenever you drink it, do it to remember me."

[26] So every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you're proclaiming the Lord's death — until he comes.

[27] Which means anyone who eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord carelessly is answerable for the body and blood of the Lord.

[28] So examine yourself. Then eat the bread and drink the cup.

[29] Because anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.

[30] That's why so many of you are weak and sick, and a number have died.

[31] If we judged ourselves honestly, we wouldn't be judged.

[32] When the Lord does judge us, he's disciplining us — so we won't be condemned along with the world.

[33] So, my brothers and sisters: when you come together to eat, wait for each other.

[34] If you're starving, eat at home — so your gatherings don't bring judgment down on you. I'll sort out the rest when I get there.

[1] Now about the gifts the Spirit gives, brothers and sisters — I don't want you in the dark.

[2] Remember what you used to be? Pagans, swept along toward idols that couldn't say a word, pulled around like you had no will of your own.

[3] So let me make this clear: nobody speaking by the Spirit of God says "Jesus is cursed," and nobody can say "Jesus is Lord" except by the Holy Spirit.

[4] There's a whole range of gifts — one Spirit behind all of them.

[5] A whole range of ways to serve — one Lord.

[6] A whole range of things being done — one God, and he's the one doing all of it in everyone.

[7] When the Spirit shows up in someone, it's for the good of everyone.

[8] To one, the Spirit gives a word loaded with wisdom. To another, through that same Spirit, a word charged with knowledge.

[9] To another, faith — the kind that moves things — by the same Spirit. To another, gifts of healing, by that one Spirit.

[10] To another, power to do the miraculous. To another, prophecy. To another, the ability to read spirits and know what's really going on. To another, tongues of various kinds. To another, the ability to interpret them.

[11] All of this — all of it — is the work of one and the same Spirit, who hands out gifts to each person exactly as he decides.

[12] Think of the body. It's one thing, but it's made of many parts — and all those parts, however many, still make up one body. That's exactly how Christ is.

[13] Because we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body — Jew, Greek, slave, free, makes no difference — and we were all given the one Spirit to drink.

[14] The body isn't a single part. It's many.

[15] If the foot says, "I'm not a hand, so I don't belong" — does that make it any less part of the body?

[16] If the ear says, "I'm not an eye, so I don't belong" — does that make it any less part of the body?

[17] If the whole body were an eyeball, where would hearing come from? If the whole thing were an ear, where would smell come from?

[18] But God arranged the parts — every one of them — in the body, exactly the way he wanted.

[19] If they were all one part, there'd be no body at all.

[20] As it is, many parts — one body.

[21] The eye can't tell the hand, "I don't need you." The head can't tell the feet, "I don't need you."

[22] In fact, the parts of the body that look weakest? Indispensable.

[23] The parts we think are less honorable, we treat with extra honor. The parts we'd rather not show off, we cover with the most care.

[24] The presentable parts don't need that kind of attention. But God put the body together this way on purpose, giving extra honor to the parts that lacked it,

[25] so there'd be no division in the body — so every part would care about every other part.

[26] If one part suffers, all of them suffer with it. If one part gets honored, all of them celebrate with it.

[27] You are the body of Christ. Each of you, a part of it.

[28] And God placed people in the church in this order: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers — then miracles, then gifts of healing, then helping, then leading, then various kinds of tongues.

[29] Is everyone an apostle? Everyone a prophet? Everyone a teacher? Does everyone do miracles?

[30] Does everyone have healing gifts? Does everyone speak in tongues? Does everyone interpret?

[31] Chase the greater gifts. And let me show you a way that goes beyond all of them.

[1] I could speak every language ever spoken — human tongues, angelic tongues — but without love, I'm just noise. A gong banging. A cymbal crashing. [2] I could have the gift of prophecy, unlock every mystery, master every field of knowledge. I could have faith strong enough to relocate mountains. Without love, I'm nothing. [3] I could give away everything I own, hand over my body to the flames — and if love isn't driving it, none of it counts for anything.

[4] Love is patient. Love is kind. Love doesn't burn with envy, doesn't strut, doesn't swell with self-importance. [5] It isn't rude. It doesn't grasp for itself. It doesn't fly off the handle. It doesn't keep a ledger of wrongs. [6] It takes no pleasure in injustice — it celebrates the truth. [7] It carries everything, trusts through everything, hopes through everything, endures everything.

[8] Love never collapses. Prophecies? They'll be retired. Tongues? They'll fall silent. Knowledge? It'll be eclipsed. [9] Because what we know is partial, what we prophesy is partial — [10] and when the whole arrives, the partial gets folded away.

[11] When I was a child, I spoke like a child, thought like a child, reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I left the child behind. [12] Right now we're squinting at a blurry reflection in a polished metal mirror — but then, face to face. Right now I know in pieces — but then I'll know fully, the way I've been fully known.

[13] Three things stand: faith, hope, love. And the greatest of them is love.

[1] Chase love. And yes, hunger for the gifts of the Spirit — especially the gift of speaking God's word to people.

[2] When you speak in a tongue, you're not addressing people — you're addressing God. Nobody understands you. Your spirit is pouring out mysteries, but they're locked away.

[3] But when someone speaks God's word into a room, they're building people up, encouraging them, holding them together.

[4] Tongues build up the one speaking. Prophecy builds up the whole community.

[5] I'd be thrilled if every one of you spoke in tongues — but more thrilled if you all spoke God's word. The one who prophesies outweighs the one who speaks in tongues, unless someone's there to interpret so the church actually gets something from it.

[6] Think it through, friends. If I show up speaking in tongues, what good am I to you? I'd only help if I brought you something you could grasp — a revelation, knowledge, a word from God, teaching.

[7] Even lifeless instruments — a flute, a harp — if they don't play distinct notes, how does anyone recognize the tune?

[8] If a trumpet just blares noise, who's going to grab their weapon and run to battle?

[9] Same with you. If your tongue produces sounds nobody can decode, how will anyone know what you said? You'll be talking into the wind.

[10] There are countless languages in the world, and every one of them carries meaning.

[11] But if I don't know the language, I'm a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker's a foreigner to me.

[12] So here's the point: since you're hungry for spiritual gifts, hunger for the ones that build up the church. Aim there.

[13] Anyone who speaks in a tongue should pray for the ability to interpret it.

[14] Because if I'm praying in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind is sitting on the bench, doing nothing.

[15] So what do I do? I'll pray with my spirit — and pray with my mind. I'll sing with my spirit — and sing with my mind.

[16] Otherwise, if you're blessing God only in the spirit, how is the outsider sitting there supposed to say "Amen" to your thanks? They have no idea what you said.

[17] You may be giving thanks beautifully — but the other person isn't being built up at all.

[18] I thank God I speak in tongues more than any of you.

[19] But in the gathered church, I'd rather speak five intelligible words to teach others than ten thousand words in a tongue.

[20] Brothers and sisters, stop thinking like children. Be infants when it comes to evil — but grown-ups in how you think.

[21] It's written in the Law: *Through strangers' tongues and foreigners' lips I will speak to this people, and even then they won't listen to me, says the Lord.*

[22] So tongues are a sign — not for believers, but for unbelievers. Prophecy is the opposite — it's for believers, not unbelievers.

[23] Picture it: the whole church packed in, everyone speaking in tongues. Outsiders walk in, people who don't believe — they'll think you've lost your minds.

[24] But if everyone is speaking God's word and an unbeliever or outsider walks in, they're cut to the quick. They're exposed. Every word lands on them.

[25] The secrets of their heart come out into the open. They fall on their face, worship God, and admit: *God is really here among you.*

[26] So what's the takeaway, friends? When you gather, each of you brings something — a song, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, an interpretation. Let all of it build the body up.

[27] If anyone speaks in a tongue, keep it to two — three at the most — and one at a time. And someone needs to interpret.

[28] If no interpreter is there, the speaker should keep silent in the assembly and speak to themselves and to God.

[29] Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what's said.

[30] If a revelation comes to someone sitting nearby, the first speaker should sit down.

[31] You can all prophesy — just one at a time — so everyone learns and everyone is encouraged.

[32] The spirits of the prophets are under the prophets' control.

[33] God isn't a God of chaos but of peace. This holds in every gathering of God's people.

[34] Women should stay quiet in the assemblies. They aren't permitted to speak up there — they should be in submission, as the Law also says.

[35] If they want to learn something, they can ask their own husbands at home. It's a disgrace for a woman to be speaking out in the assembly.

[36] Or did the word of God originate with you? Are you the only ones it reached?

[37] If anyone thinks they're a prophet or spiritually gifted, they need to recognize that what I'm writing to you is the Lord's command.

[38] If anyone ignores this, they themselves will be ignored.

[39] So, my brothers and sisters, hunger to speak God's word, and don't shut down speaking in tongues.

[40] Just let everything happen in a worthy and orderly way.

[1] Brothers and sisters, let me remind you of the gospel I preached to you — the one you received, the one you're standing on, [2] the one saving you right now, if you hold tight to the message I delivered. Otherwise you believed for nothing.

[3] I handed over to you what was handed to me — and this is first-order, non-negotiable: Christ died for our sins, exactly as the Scriptures said he would. [4] He was buried. He was raised on the third day, exactly as the Scriptures said he would be. [5] He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve. [6] After that he appeared to more than five hundred brothers all at once — most of them still alive, though some have fallen asleep. [7] Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles. [8] Last of all, he appeared to me too — the freak birth in the family.

[9] I'm the least of the apostles. I don't even deserve the title — I hunted down God's church. [10] But by God's grace I am what I am, and his grace toward me wasn't wasted. I outworked them all — not me, really, but the grace of God with me. [11] So whether it was me or them, this is what we preach, and this is what you believed.

[12] Now if our message is that Christ has been raised from the dead, how can some of you say there's no resurrection of the dead? [13] If there's no resurrection, then Christ wasn't raised either. [14] And if Christ wasn't raised, our preaching is empty and your faith is empty. [15] Worse — we come out as liars about God, because we testified that he raised Christ. He didn't, if the dead aren't raised. [16] Because if the dead aren't raised, Christ wasn't raised. [17] And if Christ wasn't raised, your faith is a fraud and you're still buried in your sins. [18] Which means everyone who died trusting Christ is gone — finished. [19] If our hope in Christ runs out at the grave, we're the most pathetic people on earth.

[20] But Christ has been raised from the dead — the firstfruits of everyone who's fallen asleep. [21] Since death came through a human being, resurrection comes through a human being too. [22] As everyone dies in Adam, everyone will be made alive in Christ. [23] Each in turn: Christ first, then his people when he comes. [24] Then the end — when he hands the kingdom over to God the Father, after he's dismantled every ruler, every authority, every power. [25] He has to reign until he's put every enemy under his feet. [26] The last enemy to be destroyed is death. [27] Because *he has put everything under his feet*. Now when it says everything is under him, obviously that doesn't include the One who put everything under him. [28] And when everything is finally under him, then the Son himself will be placed under the One who placed everything under him — so that God will be all in all.

[29] Otherwise, what are people doing getting baptized for the dead? If the dead aren't raised at all, why baptize for them? [30] Why are we putting ourselves in harm's way every hour of every day? [31] I die daily — I swear it by the pride I take in you, brothers and sisters, in Christ Jesus our Lord. [32] If my fight with wild beasts in Ephesus was just human theater, what did I gain? If the dead aren't raised, *let's eat and drink, because tomorrow we die.* [33] Don't be fooled: bad company corrupts good character. [34] Sober up. Stop sinning. Some of you don't even know God — I say this to shame you.

[35] But someone will ask: "How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?" [36] Fool. What you sow doesn't come to life unless it dies. [37] And what you sow is not the body that will be — just a bare seed, wheat or whatever. [38] God gives it a body as he chooses, and to each seed its own body. [39] Not all flesh is the same flesh: humans have one kind, animals another, birds another, fish another. [40] There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies — but the splendor of the heavenly is one thing, the splendor of the earthly another. [41] The sun has its own glory, the moon another, the stars another — and star differs from star in glory.

[42] So it is with the resurrection of the dead. Sown perishable; raised imperishable. [43] Sown in dishonor; raised in glory. Sown in weakness; raised in power. [44] Sown a natural body; raised a spiritual body. If there's a natural body, there's a spiritual one. [45] As it stands written: *The first man Adam became a living soul.* The last Adam became a life-giving Spirit. [46] But the spiritual didn't come first — the natural did, then the spiritual. [47] The first man was from the earth — dust. The second man is from heaven. [48] As the dust-man was, so are the dust-people. As the heavenly man is, so are the heavenly people. [49] And just as we have borne the image of the dust-man, we will also bear the image of the heavenly man.

[50] Here's what I'm telling you, brothers and sisters: flesh and blood can't inherit the kingdom of God. The perishable can't inherit the imperishable. [51] Listen — I'm telling you a mystery: we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed — [52] in a flash, in the blink of an eye, at the last trumpet. The trumpet will sound, the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed. [53] Because this perishable thing must put on imperishability, and this mortal thing must put on immortality. [54] And when the perishable puts on imperishability, and the mortal puts on immortality, then the word that was written will come true:

*Death has been swallowed up in victory.*
[55] *Death — where's your win?*
*Death — where's your sting?*

[56] Death's sting is sin, and sin's power is the law. [57] But thank God — he gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

[58] So then, my dear brothers and sisters: stand your ground. Don't be moved. Pour yourselves into the work of the Lord without holding back, because you know — in the Lord, your labor is not wasted. Not one drop.

[1] Now about the collection for God's people: do exactly what I told the Galatian churches to do. [2] On the first day of every week, each of you should set aside something at home, saving up in proportion to what you've earned, so that no last-minute collections happen when I arrive. [3] When I get there, I'll send whoever you approve, with letters of introduction, to carry your gift to Jerusalem. [4] If it makes sense for me to go too, they'll travel with me.

[5] I'll come to you after I pass through Macedonia — that's the route I'm taking. [6] I may stay with you a while, even spend the winter, so you can send me on my way wherever I head next. [7] I don't want to just drop in for a quick visit this time. I'm hoping to stay longer, if the Lord permits. [8] But I'll remain in Ephesus until Pentecost, [9] because a wide-open door for real work has swung open here — and the opposition is fierce.

[10] If Timothy arrives, make sure he's at ease among you. He's doing the Lord's work, just like I am. [11] Don't let anyone look down on him. Send him off in peace so he can come back to me — I'm waiting for him with the brothers.

[12] About our brother Apollos: I strongly urged him to visit you with the others, but he wasn't willing to come right now. He'll come when the time is right.

[13] Stay alert. Stand firm in the faith. Be courageous. Be strong. [14] Let everything you do be done in love.

[15] One more thing, brothers and sisters. You know Stephanas and his household — the first converts in Achaia, who've devoted themselves to serving God's people. [16] Submit to people like them, and to everyone who labors and works alongside them. [17] I'm thrilled that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived. They've made up for your absence — [18] they refreshed my spirit, and yours too. Honor people like this.

[19] The churches in the province of Asia send their greetings. Aquila and Priscilla, along with the church that meets at their house, send you warm greetings in the Lord. [20] All the brothers and sisters here greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

[21] This greeting is in my own hand — Paul.

[22] If anyone has no love for the Lord — let them be cursed. *Marana tha* — Come, Lord!

[23] The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.

[24] My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.

[1] What existed from the start — what we heard, what we saw with our own eyes, what we studied, what our hands actually touched — this is what we're telling you about: the Word who is Life.

[2] That Life stepped into the open. We saw it. We're witnesses. We're announcing to you the Life that has always belonged to the Father and then showed up among us.

[3] What we saw, what we heard — we're passing it on to you, so you can be in this with us. And what we're in is communion with the Father and with his Son, Jesus the Anointed.

[4] We're writing all this so the joy can be complete — yours and ours together.

[5] Here is the message we picked up from him and now hand to you: God is light. In him there is no darkness at all. Not a trace. Not a shadow.

[6] If we claim to share life with him while still walking around in the dark, we're lying. We're not living the truth — we're performing it.

[7] But if we walk in the light, the way he is in the light, then we're truly connected — to each other, to him — and the blood of Jesus his Son keeps washing us clean of every sin.

[8] If we tell ourselves we have no sin, we're running a con on ourselves. The truth isn't in us.

[9] But if we name our sins honestly, he is faithful, he is just — he forgives the sins and scrubs away every shred of the wrong.

[10] If we claim we haven't sinned, we're calling God a liar — and his word has no place in us.

[1] My children, I'm writing this so you won't sin. But if anyone does sin, we have someone speaking up for us in the Father's presence—Jesus the Messiah, the righteous one.

[2] He himself is the atoning sacrifice for our sins—and not only ours, but for the sins of the whole world.

[3] Here's how we know we've come to know him: we keep his commands.

[4] Anyone who says "I know him" but ignores his commands is a liar. The truth isn't in him.

[5] But whoever actually holds to his word—in that person God's love has reached its full weight. This is how we know we're in him:

[6] anyone claiming to live in him has to walk the way Jesus walked.

[7] Friends, I'm not handing you some new command. It's the old one—the one you've had from the start. The ancient command is the message you already heard.

[8] And yet, in another sense, I am writing you a new command—one that's true in him and in you, because the darkness is dissolving and the real light is already shining.

[9] Anyone who claims to be in the light while hating his brother is still buried in the dark.

[10] The one who loves his brother stays in the light, and there's nothing in him to trip over.

[11] But the one who hates his brother is in the dark, walks in the dark, and has no idea where he's going—because the darkness has blinded his eyes.

[12] I'm writing to you, children, because your sins have been wiped clean on account of his name.

[13] I'm writing to you, fathers, because you've known the one who has been there from the beginning. I'm writing to you, young men, because you've conquered the evil one.

[14] I wrote to you, children, because you've known the Father. I wrote to you, fathers, because you've known the one from the beginning. I wrote to you, young men, because you are strong—God's word lives in you and you've conquered the evil one.

[15] Don't fall in love with the world or anything in it. If anyone loves the world, the Father's love isn't in him.

[16] Because everything the world peddles—the craving of the flesh, the craving of the eyes, the swagger of self-made life—none of it comes from the Father. It all comes from the world.

[17] And the world is passing away, along with everything it wants. But the one who does what God wants lives forever.

[18] Children, this is the final hour. You heard that an antichrist is coming—and sure enough, many antichrists are already here. That's how we know it's the final hour.

[19] They left us, but they were never really one of us. If they had been, they would have stayed. Their leaving exposed them: none of them ever belonged.

[20] But you—you have an anointing from the Holy One, and all of you know the truth.

[21] I'm not writing because you don't know the truth. I'm writing because you do—and because no lie ever came out of the truth.

[22] So who is the liar? The one who denies that Jesus is the Messiah. That's the antichrist—the one who denies the Father and the Son.

[23] Anyone who denies the Son doesn't have the Father either. The one who confesses the Son has the Father too.

[24] Whatever you heard from the beginning—let it stay in you. If what you heard from the beginning stays in you, then you'll stay in the Son and in the Father.

[25] And this is what he promised us: eternal life.

[26] I'm writing this about the people trying to lead you astray.

[27] As for you—the anointing you received from him stays in you, and you don't need anyone to teach you. His anointing teaches you about everything, and what it teaches is true, not a lie. So stay in him, just as it taught you.

[28] And now, children, stay in him—so that when he appears, we'll have boldness and won't shrink back from him in shame at his coming.

[29] If you know he is righteous, then you know that everyone who does what's right has been born of him.

[1] Look at the love the Father has lavished on us — that we should be called children of God. And that's what we are. The world doesn't recognize us because it never recognized him.

[2] Friends, right now we are God's children. What we will become hasn't been revealed yet. But this much we know: when he appears, we will be like him, because we will see him as he actually is.

[3] Everyone who carries this hope inside them purifies themselves, just as he is pure.

[4] Anyone who keeps sinning is breaking the law — sin is lawlessness, plain and simple.

[5] You know he appeared to lift sin away. And in him there is no sin at all.

[6] No one who lives in him keeps on sinning. Anyone who keeps sinning hasn't seen him and doesn't know him.

[7] Children, don't let anyone deceive you. The one who does what is right is righteous, just as he is righteous.

[8] The one who keeps sinning belongs to the devil — because the devil has been sinning from the start. This is why the Son of God appeared: to dismantle the devil's work.

[9] No one born of God keeps living in sin, because God's seed is planted in them. They can't keep sinning, because they've been born of God.

[10] Here is how you tell God's children from the devil's children: anyone who doesn't do what is right is not from God — and neither is anyone who doesn't love their brother.

[11] This is the message you heard from the beginning: love one another.

[12] Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and slaughtered his own brother. And why did he kill him? Because his own deeds were evil, and his brother's were righteous.

[13] So don't be shocked, brothers and sisters, when the world hates you.

[14] We know we've crossed over from death into life because we love our brothers and sisters. Anyone who doesn't love is still sitting in death.

[15] Anyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer — and you know no murderer has eternal life living inside him.

[16] This is how we know what love is: he laid down his life for us. So we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.

[17] If someone has the world's resources and sees a brother in need and slams the door of his heart against him — how can the love of God be in him?

[18] Children, let's not love with words and slogans. Let's love with action. With truth.

[19] This is how we'll know we belong to the truth, and how we'll quiet our hearts in his presence

[20] whenever our hearts condemn us — because God is greater than our hearts, and he knows everything.

[21] Friends, if our hearts don't condemn us, we stand before God with confidence.

[22] And whatever we ask, we receive from him, because we keep his commands and do what pleases him.

[23] And this is his command: believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, just as he commanded us.

[24] Whoever keeps his commands lives in him, and he in them. And this is how we know he lives in us: by the Spirit he gave us.

[1] Friends, don't trust every spirit. Test them. Find out if they're really from God, because the world is crawling with false prophets.

[2] Here's how you recognize the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God.

[3] Every spirit that refuses to confess Jesus is not from God — that's the spirit of the antichrist. You heard it was coming. It's already here.

[4] You, children, belong to God, and you've already beaten them. The One in you is greater than the one running loose in the world.

[5] They're of the world. That's why they speak the world's language and the world listens.

[6] We're of God. Anyone who knows God listens to us. Anyone who doesn't, doesn't. That's how you tell the Spirit of truth from the spirit of deception.

[7] Beloved, let's love each other — because love is from God, and everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God.

[8] Whoever doesn't love has never known God. Because God is love.

[9] Here's how God's love broke into the open: he sent his only Son into the world so we could live through him.

[10] This is love — not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that absorbs our sin.

[11] Beloved, if God loved us like that, we owe each other the same.

[12] No one has ever laid eyes on God. But when we love each other, God lives in us — and his love completes itself in us.

[13] Here's how we know we're at home in him and he in us: he's given us his Spirit.

[14] And we've seen it. We testify: the Father sent the Son to be the Savior of the world.

[15] Whoever confesses Jesus is the Son of God — God lives in them, and they in God.

[16] We've come to know and trust the love God has for us. God is love. Whoever lives in love lives in God, and God lives in them.

[17] This is love brought to its full weight in us: we can face the day of judgment with our heads up — because as he is, so are we in this world.

[18] Fear has no room in love. Real love drives fear out the door. Fear has to do with punishment, and anyone still afraid hasn't yet been perfected in love.

[19] We love because he loved us first.

[20] Anyone who says "I love God" while hating his brother is a liar. If you can't love the brother you've seen, you can't love the God you haven't.

[21] And this is the command we have from him: whoever loves God must also love his brother.

[1] Anyone who believes Jesus is the Anointed One has been born from God. And everyone who loves the Father loves the child He fathered. [2] Here's how we know we love God's children: when we love God and do what He says. [3] Because loving God means this — keeping His commands. And His commands aren't a crushing weight. [4] Everything born from God overpowers the world. And the weapon that wins — the victory that has already conquered the world — is our faith. [5] Who beats the world? Only the one who believes Jesus is the Son of God.

[6] This is the one who came through water and blood — Jesus, the Anointed One. Not just water. Water and blood. And the Spirit testifies to this, because the Spirit is truth itself. [7] So three witnesses speak: [8] the Spirit, the water, and the blood. And all three say the same thing.

[9] If we accept human testimony, God's testimony is greater — and this is God's testimony: He has testified about His Son. [10] Whoever believes in the Son of God carries that testimony inside them. Whoever refuses to believe God has called Him a liar — because they've rejected what God said about His own Son. [11] And here's the testimony: God gave us eternal life, and this life is in His Son. [12] If you have the Son, you have life. If you don't have the Son of God, you don't have life. Period.

[13] I'm writing this to you who believe in the name of the Son of God so you'll know — actually know — that you have eternal life.

[14] And here's the confidence we have with Him: when we ask anything in line with His will, He hears us. [15] And if we know He hears us — whatever we ask — then we know we already have what we asked Him for.

[16] If you see your brother committing a sin that doesn't lead to death, ask, and God will give him life — for those whose sin doesn't lead to death. There is a sin that leads to death. I'm not saying to pray about that one. [17] Every wrongdoing is sin, but not all sin leads to death.

[18] We know this: anyone born from God doesn't keep on sinning. The One born from God guards them, and the evil one can't lay a hand on them. [19] We know we belong to God, and the whole world lies in the grip of the evil one. [20] And we know the Son of God has come and has given us understanding so we can know the One who is real. And we are in the One who is real — in His Son, Jesus the Anointed. This is the true God. This is eternal life.

[21] Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

[1] King David was old now, weighed down with years. They piled blankets on him, but the cold never left his bones.

[2] His servants said, "Let us find a young virgin for our lord the king — someone to wait on him, attend him, lie against his chest. She will warm him."

[3] So they swept through every corner of Israel searching for the most beautiful young woman they could find, and they brought back Abishag of Shunem.

[4] She was stunning. She nursed the king, served him — but he never knew her.

[5] Meanwhile Adonijah, Haggith's son, was puffing himself up. "I will be king," he announced. He set up chariots, cavalry, fifty men to run ahead of him.

[6] His father had never once crossed him, never asked, "Why are you doing this?" Adonijah was striking to look at, born right after Absalom.

[7] He cut deals with Joab son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest — both threw in with him.

[8] But Zadok the priest, Benaiah son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei, Rei, and David's elite warriors — none of them sided with Adonijah.

[9] Adonijah staged a feast at the Stone of Zoheleth, near En Rogel — sheep, oxen, fatted calves slaughtered for the occasion. He invited every one of his royal brothers and all the king's officials from Judah.

[10] But Nathan the prophet, Benaiah, the elite warriors, his brother Solomon — them, he did not invite.

[11] Nathan went to Bathsheba, Solomon's mother. "Have you heard? Adonijah son of Haggith has made himself king — and our lord David doesn't even know.

[12] Listen to me. I am about to save your life and your son Solomon's life.

[13] Go to King David. Now. Say this: 'My lord the king, didn't you swear to me, your servant, that Solomon my son would reign after you, that he would sit on your throne? So why is Adonijah king?'

[14] While you're still speaking, I will come in behind you and confirm every word."

[15] Bathsheba went into the king's chamber. He was very old. Abishag the Shunammite was tending him.

[16] Bathsheba bowed low, dropped to her knees. "What is it?" the king asked.

[17] "My lord," she said, "you yourself swore by the Lord your God to your servant: 'Solomon your son will reign after me, he will sit on my throne.'

[18] And now — look — Adonijah has crowned himself king, and you, my lord, you have no idea.

[19] He has slaughtered oxen and fatted calves and sheep — a massive feast — and invited all your sons, Abiathar the priest, Joab the commander. But Solomon, your servant? Not invited.

[20] My lord the king, all Israel is watching, waiting for you to say who will sit on your throne after you.

[21] If you don't speak, the moment my lord lies down with his ancestors, Solomon and I will be branded traitors."

[22] She was still speaking when Nathan the prophet arrived.

[23] They told the king, "Nathan the prophet is here." He entered, bowed face to the floor.

[24] Nathan said, "My lord the king — did you decree that Adonijah would reign after you, that he would sit on your throne?

[25] Because today he has gone down and slaughtered oxen and fatted calves and sheep without count, and he has invited every one of the king's sons, the army commanders, Abiathar the priest. Right now they are eating and drinking with him, shouting, 'Long live King Adonijah!'

[26] But me — your servant — and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and your servant Solomon — he did not invite us.

[27] Did this come from my lord the king? Did you really not tell your servants who would sit on your throne after you?"

[28] King David said, "Bring Bathsheba back to me." She came in and stood before him.

[29] He swore: "As surely as the Lord lives — the One who has rescued me from every trouble —

[30] just as I swore to you by the Lord, the God of Israel, that Solomon your son would reign after me, that he would sit on my throne in my place — I will do exactly that. Today."

[31] Bathsheba bowed face to the ground, knelt before the king, and said, "May my lord King David live forever."

[32] Then David said, "Call in Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada." They came before the king.

[33] "Take my own servants with you," he ordered. "Put Solomon my son on my own mule. Bring him down to Gihon.

[34] There, Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet are to anoint him king over Israel. Sound the ram's horn and shout, 'Long live King Solomon!'

[35] Then escort him back up. He will come and sit on my throne and reign in my place. I have appointed him ruler over Israel and Judah."

[36] Benaiah son of Jehoiada answered the king: "Amen. May the Lord, the God of my lord the king, declare it so.

[37] As the Lord was with my lord the king, so may he be with Solomon — and may he make his throne even greater than the throne of my lord King David."

[38] So Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah son of Jehoiada, the Kerethites and the Pelethites went down. They put Solomon on King David's mule and escorted him to Gihon.

[39] Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the sacred tent and anointed Solomon. They blew the ram's horn, and the people roared: "Long live King Solomon!"

[40] The whole crowd went up after him, playing flutes, rejoicing with such force the earth itself split open with the sound.

[41] Adonijah and all his guests heard it just as they were finishing the feast. When Joab caught the sound of the ram's horn, he said, "Why is the city in an uproar?"

[42] He was still speaking when Jonathan son of Abiathar the priest arrived. Adonijah said, "Come in. You're a man of standing — you must bring good news."

[43] "No," Jonathan answered. "Our lord King David has made Solomon king.

[44] The king sent Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah son of Jehoiada, the Kerethites and the Pelethites with him. They put him on the king's own mule.

[45] Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed him king at Gihon, and they came up from there cheering — the city is shaking with it. That's the sound you hear.

[46] Solomon is on the royal throne now.

[47] The king's officials have already gone in to congratulate our lord King David, saying, 'May your God make Solomon's name greater than yours and his throne greater than your throne.' And the king bowed in worship on his bed,

[48] saying, 'Blessed is the Lord, the God of Israel, who has set someone on my throne today while my own eyes can see it.'"

[49] At that, every guest of Adonijah leapt up in panic and scattered.

[50] Adonijah was terrified of Solomon. He bolted for the sanctuary and seized the horns of the altar.

[51] Word came to Solomon: "Adonijah is afraid of you. Look — he's gripping the horns of the altar, saying, 'Let King Solomon swear to me today that he will not put his servant to the sword.'"

[52] Solomon said, "If he proves himself a worthy man, not a hair of his head will fall to the ground. But if evil is found in him, he dies."

[53] So King Solomon sent for him. They brought him down from the altar. He came and bowed before King Solomon, and Solomon said, "Go home."

[1] David's days were running out. He called his son Solomon close and gave him his final charge:

[2] "I'm going where everyone goes. Stand strong. Be a man.

[3] Keep watch over what the LORD your God requires of you. Walk in his ways. Hold his statutes, his commands, his judgments, his testimonies — every one of them written in the Law of Moses. Do this, and you'll succeed in everything you touch, everywhere you turn.

[4] Do this, and the LORD will make good on what he swore to me: 'If your sons guard their path and walk faithfully before me with all their heart and all their soul, you will never lack a man on Israel's throne.'

[5] Now — you know what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me. What he did to the two commanders of Israel's armies, Abner son of Ner and Amasa son of Jether. He killed them. Spilled the blood of war in a time of peace. Put war's blood on the belt around his waist and on the sandals on his feet.

[6] Use your wisdom. Don't let his gray head go down to the grave in peace.

[7] But show kindness to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite. Let them eat at your table. They were there for me when I ran from your brother Absalom.

[8] And there's Shimei son of Gera, the Benjamite from Bahurim. He cursed me — a vicious curse — the day I went to Mahanaim. Then he came down to meet me at the Jordan, and I swore to him by the LORD I wouldn't kill him by the sword.
