[8] While they struck, I was left alone. I fell on my face and cried out: "Sovereign LORD! Will you destroy every last remnant of Israel as you pour out your fury on Jerusalem?"

[9] He answered: "The guilt of Israel and Judah is monstrous—beyond measure. The land is drowning in blood, the city choked with corruption. They tell themselves: 'The LORD has abandoned the land. The LORD does not see.'

[10] So I will not look on them with pity. I will not spare. I will bring down on their own heads exactly what they have done."

[11] Then the man in linen with the writing kit at his side came back to report: "I have done what you commanded."

[1] I looked, and there above the dome over the heads of the cherubim — something like sapphire, the shape of a throne, blazing into view.

[2] He spoke to the man in linen: "Go in. Step between the wheels, beneath the cherubim. Fill both hands with burning coals from among them. Scatter them over the city." And I watched him go.

[3] The cherubim were standing on the south side of the temple when the man went in, and a cloud filled the inner court.

[4] Then the glory of the LORD lifted off the cherub and moved to the threshold of the house. The cloud filled the temple. The court blazed with the radiance of his glory.

[5] The sound of the cherubim's wings carried all the way to the outer court — like the voice of God Almighty when he speaks.

[6] When he ordered the man in linen, "Take fire from between the wheels, from between the cherubim," the man went and stood beside one of the wheels.

[7] A cherub reached his hand into the fire that burned among them, lifted some out, and placed it in the hands of the man in linen, who took it and left.

[8] Under the wings of the cherubim — the form of human hands was visible.

[9] I looked: four wheels beside the cherubim, one beside each one. The wheels gleamed like beryl, like chrysolite stone.

[10] All four looked the same — as if a wheel were running through the middle of a wheel.

[11] When they moved, they could go in any of the four directions without turning. Wherever the head faced, they followed — no pivoting, no hesitation.

[12] Their entire bodies — backs, hands, wings, and the wheels — were covered with eyes. All four of them. All over.

[13] I heard the wheels being called "the whirling ones."

[14] Each cherub had four faces: the first the face of a cherub, the second a human face, the third a lion, the fourth an eagle.

[15] The cherubim rose. These were the living creatures I had seen by the Kebar River.

[16] When the cherubim moved, the wheels moved with them. When the cherubim lifted their wings to rise above the earth, the wheels did not leave their side.

[17] When the cherubim stopped, the wheels stopped. When they rose, the wheels rose with them — because the spirit of the living creatures was in them.

[18] Then the glory of the LORD departed from the threshold of the temple and came to rest above the cherubim.

[19] Before my eyes, the cherubim lifted their wings and rose from the ground, the wheels beside them. They paused at the east gate of the LORD's house — and the glory of the God of Israel hovered above them.

[20] These were the same living creatures I had seen beneath the God of Israel by the Kebar River. Now I knew: they were cherubim.

[21] Each had four faces, each had four wings, and beneath their wings — the form of human hands.

[22] Their faces were the very faces I had seen by the Kebar — the same appearance, the same beings. Each one moved straight ahead.

[1] Then the Spirit lifted me up and carried me to the east gate of the LORD's house — the gate facing sunrise. And there at the entrance stood twenty-five men. Among them I saw Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, princes of the people.

[2] He said to me: "Son of man, these are the men plotting evil, peddling poisoned counsel inside this city.

[3] They say, 'No need to build houses yet — the danger isn't close. This city is the pot, and we are the meat inside it. Safe. Protected.'

[4] So prophesy against them. Prophesy, son of man."

[5] Then the Spirit of the LORD fell on me, and he told me to speak: "This is what the LORD says — I know what you're saying, House of Israel. I know every thought rising in your minds.

[6] You've filled this city's streets with corpses. Your slaughter has piled up everywhere.

[7] So this is what the Lord GOD says: The bodies you've dumped in this city — they're the meat. The city is the pot. But you? I'm dragging you out of it.

[8] You're terrified of the sword? I'll bring you the sword. That's the word of the Lord GOD.

[9] I'll haul you out of this city, hand you over to foreigners, and execute judgment on you.

[10] You'll fall by the sword. I'll judge you at the border of Israel itself — and then you'll know I am the LORD.

[11] This city will not be your safe pot, and you will not be the protected meat inside it. I will judge you at the border of Israel.

[12] Then you'll know I am the LORD — whose statutes you ignored, whose laws you refused to keep, while imitating every nation around you."

[13] And while I was still prophesying, Pelatiah son of Benaiah dropped dead. I collapsed face-down and cried out: "Lord GOD! Are you wiping out everything that's left of Israel?"

[14] Then the word of the LORD came to me:

[15] "Son of man — your brothers, your own blood, your kin, the whole House of Israel — every one of them. The people still in Jerusalem are saying about them: 'They went far from the LORD. This land — it was handed to us. It's ours now.'

[16] So tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says — Yes, I sent them far off among the nations. Yes, I scattered them across foreign lands. But I myself have been a sanctuary for them — a small one, in the countries where they've gone.

[17] So tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says — I will gather you from the peoples. I will collect you out of the countries where you were scattered. And I will give you back the soil of Israel.

[18] They'll come home and strip this land of every vile thing, every abomination they find.

[19] I will give them one heart. I will put a new spirit inside them. I will tear the heart of stone out of their chests and give them a heart of flesh —

[20] so they'll walk in my statutes, guard my laws, do them. They will be my people. I will be their God.

[21] But those whose hearts still chase after vile things and abominations — I will bring their conduct down on their own heads. That's the word of the Lord GOD."

[22] Then the cherubim lifted their wings, the wheels rose beside them, and the glory of the God of Israel hovered above them.

[23] The glory of the LORD lifted off from the middle of the city and came to rest on the mountain east of it.

[24] Then the Spirit lifted me up and carried me — in the vision, by the Spirit of God — back to Chaldea, to the exiles. And the vision I had seen lifted from me.

[25] And I told the exiles everything the LORD had shown me.

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] "Son of man, you are living inside a rebellious house. They have eyes that could see and refuse to. They have ears that could hear and refuse to. Because they are a rebellious house.

[3] So, son of man — pack your bags. Pack like an exile, in broad daylight, where they can watch you. Then march out, in front of their eyes, from your place to another, as if you were being deported. Maybe — just maybe — they'll see it and understand. Though they are a rebellious house.

[4] Drag your gear out by day, in plain sight — the luggage of a refugee. Then in the evening, walk out yourself, in front of them, the way someone leaves who is never coming back.

[5] While they watch, dig through the wall. Carry your belongings out through the hole.

[6] Hoist the bag onto your shoulder in the dusk. Walk out with your face covered, so you cannot see the land. Because I have made you a sign to the house of Israel."

[7] So I did exactly what I was commanded. By day I carried out my baggage like an exile. By evening I dug through the wall with my own hands. In the gathering dark, I shouldered my pack and walked out — while they watched.

[8] In the morning the word of the LORD came to me:

[9] "Son of man, didn't the house of Israel — that rebellious house — ask you, 'What are you doing?'

[10] Tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says. This oracle is for the prince in Jerusalem and for the whole house of Israel inside that city.

[11] Tell them: I am your sign. What I have just done — that is what will be done to them. Into exile, into captivity, they will go.

[12] And the prince among them — he will hoist his bag on his shoulder in the dark and slip out. They'll dig through the wall to smuggle him through. He will cover his face, because his own eyes will never see this land again.

[13] I will spread my net over him. He will be caught in my snare. I will drag him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans — but he will not see it. And there he will die.

[14] Everyone around him — his guard, his troops, every last unit — I will scatter to every wind. And I will unsheathe the sword and chase them down.

[15] Then they will know that I am the LORD — when I have flung them among the nations, scattered them across the lands.

[16] But I will spare a few from sword and famine and plague — enough survivors to tell the nations where they go about every disgusting thing they did. Then they will know that I am the LORD."

[17] The word of the LORD came to me:

[18] "Son of man, eat your bread with trembling. Drink your water with shaking and dread.

[19] Then say to the people of the land: This is what the Lord GOD says about those still in Jerusalem, in the soil of Israel — they will eat their bread in anxiety and drink their water in horror. Because their land will be stripped bare of everything in it, on account of the violence of everyone living there.

[20] The inhabited cities will be ruins. The land will be wasteland. Then you will know that I am the LORD."

[21] The word of the LORD came to me:

[22] "Son of man — what is this proverb you people repeat in the land of Israel? 'The days drag on and every vision comes to nothing.'

[23] Tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says. I am putting an end to that proverb. They will not chant it in Israel anymore. Instead, tell them: The days are almost here — and every vision is about to land.

[24] No more empty visions. No more flattering divinations inside the house of Israel.

[25] Because I, the LORD, will speak. Whatever word I speak will happen. No more delays. In your own days, rebellious house, I will speak the word and do it. This is the declaration of the Lord GOD."

[26] The word of the LORD came to me:

[27] "Son of man — listen to what the house of Israel is saying: 'The vision he sees is for years from now. He's prophesying about the distant future.'

[28] So tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says. Not one of my words will be delayed any longer. Whatever I say, I will do. This is the declaration of the Lord GOD."

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] "Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Israel — the ones currently prophesying. Tell those who spin visions from their own gut: Hear the word of the LORD.

[3] This is what the Lord GOD says: Doom on the fools who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing.

[4] Israel, your prophets are jackals nosing through the ruins.

[5] They never climbed up to the breaches. They never rebuilt the wall for the house of Israel so it could stand its ground when the day of the LORD arrives.

[6] Their visions are empty. Their divinations are lies. They say, 'The LORD declares —' when the LORD never sent them. And still they expect their words to come true.

[7] Was the vision you saw not a sham? Was the divination you spoke not a lie? You say, 'The LORD declares' — when I never said a word.

[8] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Because you have spoken emptiness and seen lies — I am against you. That's the word of the Lord GOD.

[9] My hand will come down on the prophets who see lies and divine deceit. They will not sit in the council of my people. They will not be enrolled in the register of the house of Israel. They will not set foot in the land of Israel. Then you will know that I am the Lord GOD.

[10] Because — yes, because — they have led my people astray, saying 'Peace' when there is no peace. When someone throws up a flimsy wall, they come along and slap whitewash over it.

[11] So tell the whitewashers: that wall is coming down. A flooding rain is coming. Hailstones — I'm sending them. A hurricane wind will tear through.

[12] And when the wall collapses, won't people ask you: 'Where's the whitewash you smeared on it now?'

[13] This is what the Lord GOD says: In my fury I will unleash a hurricane wind. In my anger, a flooding rain. In my wrath, hailstones to finish it off.

[14] I will tear down the wall you whitewashed. I will level it to the ground. Its foundation will be exposed. When it falls, you will be crushed inside it. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

[15] I will spend my fury on the wall and on those who whitewashed it. And I will tell you: the wall is gone, and so are the ones who plastered it —

[16] the prophets of Israel who prophesied to Jerusalem and saw visions of peace for her when there was no peace. That's the word of the Lord GOD.

[17] And you, son of man — set your face against the daughters of your people who prophesy out of their own imagination. Prophesy against them.

[18] Say: This is what the Lord GOD says: Doom on the women who stitch magic bands for every wrist and make veils for heads of every size to hunt down souls. Will you hunt the lives of my people and keep your own alive?

[19] You profane me before my people for a few handfuls of barley and crusts of bread — killing souls that should not die, sparing souls that should not live, peddling lies to a people that loves to listen.

[20] So this is what the Lord GOD says: I am against your magic bands — the ones you use to snare souls like birds. I will rip them off your arms. I will release the souls you've been hunting — let them fly.

[21] I will tear off your veils and rescue my people from your hands. They will never again be prey in your grip. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

[22] Because you have demoralized the righteous with lies — when I never grieved them — and propped up the wicked so he will not turn from his evil and save his life —

[23] therefore: no more empty visions. No more divination. I will tear my people out of your hands. Then you will know that I am the LORD."

[1] Then some of Israel's elders came to me and sat down in front of me.

[2] And the word of the LORD came to me:

[3] "Son of man, these men have set up their idols in their own hearts. They've planted the stumbling block of their guilt right in front of their faces. Should I let myself be consulted by them at all?

[4] So speak to them. Tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says — Any Israelite who enshrines idols in his heart, plants his guilt like a trap before his own eyes, and then waltzes in to question the prophet — I, the LORD, will answer him. I'll answer him according to the swarm of his idols.

[5] I'll seize Israel's heart with that answer — every last one of them who has wandered from me chasing their idols.

[6] So tell the house of Israel: This is what the Lord GOD says — Turn. Turn back from your idols. Turn your faces away from every disgusting thing you've made.

[7] Because any Israelite — or any foreigner living in Israel — who breaks away from me, enshrines his idols in his heart, sets his guilt like a snare before his own eyes, and then comes to a prophet to inquire of me through him: I, the LORD, will answer him myself.

[8] I'll set my face against that man. I'll make him a sign, a warning, a proverb on people's lips. I'll cut him off from the middle of my people. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

[9] And if a prophet is seduced into giving an answer — it was I, the LORD, who seduced him. I will stretch out my hand against him and erase him from my people Israel.

[10] They'll both carry their guilt — the prophet and the one who came asking. They'll answer the same.

[11] So the house of Israel will never wander from me again, never defile themselves again with all their rebellions. They will be my people, and I will be their God. Declaration of the Lord GOD."

[12] The word of the LORD came to me:

[13] "Son of man, if a land sins against me by breaking faith, and I stretch out my hand against it — shatter its supply of bread, send famine into it, cut down both human and beast —

[14] even if these three were in it — Noah, Daniel, and Job — they would save only themselves by their righteousness. Declaration of the Lord GOD.

[15] Or say I unleash wild animals through the land and they leave it childless, a wasteland nobody dares cross because of the beasts —

[16] even if these three men were in it, as I live — declaration of the Lord GOD — they wouldn't save a single son or daughter. They alone would be saved, and the land would be left desolate.

[17] Or say I bring a sword against that land and say, 'Sword, sweep through this land,' and I cut down both human and beast —

[18] even if these three men were in it, as I live — declaration of the Lord GOD — they wouldn't save a single son or daughter. They alone would be saved.

[19] Or say I send plague into that land and pour out my fury on it in blood, cutting down both human and beast —

[20] even if Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, as I live — declaration of the Lord GOD — they wouldn't save a son or a daughter. They would save only themselves by their righteousness.

[21] Because this is what the Lord GOD says: How much worse, then, when I send my four devastating judgments against Jerusalem — sword, famine, wild beasts, and plague — to cut down both human and beast.

[22] And yet — survivors will be left in it, sons and daughters being led out. They'll come to you. You'll see how they lived, what they did. And you'll be consoled about the disaster I've brought on Jerusalem, every blow I struck.

[23] They will console you when you see how they lived, what they did. Then you'll know I did nothing in that city without reason. Declaration of the Lord GOD."

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man—what makes the wood of a vine worth anything more than the wood of any tree in the forest, any branch among the trees?

[3] Can you carve anything useful from it? Can you even shape a peg sturdy enough to hang a pot on?

[4] No—it gets thrown to the fire as fuel. The flames eat both ends and char the middle. Now what is it good for?

[5] If it was useless before the fire touched it, how much more useless now that it's burned and blackened—what could anyone make of it?

[6] So here is what the Lord GOD says: Just as I hand over the vine wood among the forest trees to the fire, so I hand over the people of Jerusalem.

[7] I have set my face against them. They escaped one fire—another will devour them. And you will know I am the LORD when I turn my face against them.

[8] I will leave the land a wasteland, because they have been faithless. This is the declaration of the Lord GOD.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me again:

[2] Son of man, make Jerusalem face what she is. Show her every disgusting thing she's done.

[3] Tell her: This is what the Lord GOD says to Jerusalem — Your roots, your birth, are Canaanite soil. Your father was an Amorite. Your mother, a Hittite.

[4] The day you were born, no one cut your cord. No one washed you clean. No one rubbed you with salt or wrapped you in cloth.

[5] No eye looked at you with pity. No hand reached for you to do even one of these things. They threw you out in an open field, repulsed by the sight of you, on the day you were born.

[6] Then I came by. I saw you kicking in your own blood. And I said to you, lying there in that blood — Live. I said it again — Live.

[7] I made you flourish like a wildflower. You grew. You bloomed. You became a woman — breasts formed, hair grew long — but you were still naked, still bare.

[8] I came by again. I looked at you. You were ready for love. So I spread the corner of my cloak over you and covered your nakedness. I gave you my oath. I entered a covenant with you — the declaration of the Lord GOD — and you became mine.

[9] I washed you. I rinsed the blood off your skin. I anointed you with oil.

[10] I dressed you in embroidered linen, gave you leather sandals, wrapped you in fine cloth, draped you in silk.

[11] I covered you in jewelry — bracelets on your wrists, a chain around your neck,

[12] a ring in your nose, earrings in your ears, a crown of beauty on your head.

[13] Gold and silver were your ornaments. Your clothes were linen and silk and embroidery. You ate the finest flour, honey, oil. You became staggeringly beautiful — fit to be queen.

[14] Your name went out among the nations because of your beauty. It was perfect — because the splendor I had placed on you made it so. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[15] But you trusted in your beauty. You traded on your fame and turned whore. You poured your seductions out on every passerby — anyone who walked by could have you.

[16] You took your clothes and made bright-colored shrines out of them, and there you played the whore. Nothing like this has ever happened — nothing like this should ever happen.

[17] You took the beautiful jewelry I gave you — my gold, my silver — and you molded yourself male images and prostituted yourself to them.

[18] You took your embroidered robes and dressed those idols in them. My oil, my incense — you set them before those things.

[19] The food I provided you — the fine flour, the oil, the honey I put in your mouth — you set before them as a sweet aroma. That's what happened. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[20] You took your sons and your daughters — the children you bore to me — and sacrificed them to those things to be devoured. As if your prostitution wasn't enough,

[21] you butchered my children and handed them over, burning them up as offerings.

[22] In all your obscenities and whoring, you never once remembered the days of your youth — how you were naked and bare, kicking in your own blood.

[23] And then — disaster on disaster, woe to you, the declaration of the Lord GOD —

[24] you built yourself a brothel-mound. You raised your platform in every public square.

[25] On every street corner you built your platform. You turned your beauty into a disgrace. You spread your legs for every passerby. Your whoring multiplied.

[26] You whored with the Egyptians — your big-bodied neighbors. You doubled your prostitution to provoke me.

[27] So I stretched out my hand against you. I cut back your portion. I handed you over to the lust of those who hated you — the daughters of the Philistines — and even they were scandalized by your shameless behavior.

[28] You whored with Assyria too — because you couldn't get enough. You whored with them and still weren't satisfied.

[29] You took your whoring all the way to Chaldea, to the merchant land — and even that didn't fill you up.

[30] How sick is your heart? — the declaration of the Lord GOD — that you do all this, the work of a brazen prostitute,

[31] building your mound at every street corner, raising your platform in every square — and yet you weren't even like a prostitute, because you sneered at payment.

[32] An adulterous wife! Taking strangers instead of her husband!

[33] Every prostitute gets paid. But you — you handed out the gifts. You bribed your lovers to come from everywhere for your whoring.

[34] You're the opposite of every other prostitute. No one chases you — you chase them. No one pays you — you pay them. You are inside-out.

[35] So, whore — hear the word of the LORD.

[36] This is what the Lord GOD says: Because your lust was poured out, your nakedness exposed in your whoring with your lovers, and because of every disgusting idol, and the blood of your children that you gave them —

[37] watch. I'm gathering every lover you ever pleasured — every one you loved, every one you hated. I'm gathering them against you from every direction. I'm stripping you naked in front of them. They will see all of you.

[38] I'll sentence you the way adulterers and murderers are sentenced. I'll bring on you the blood of fury and jealousy.

[39] I'll hand you over to them. They'll tear down your mound. They'll smash your platform. They'll strip the clothes off you. They'll take your beautiful jewelry. They'll leave you naked and bare.

[40] They'll bring a mob against you. They'll stone you. They'll hack you to pieces with their swords.

[41] They'll burn your houses down and execute judgment on you while a crowd of women watches. I will end your whoring. There will be no more payments going out from you.

[42] Then my fury will rest. My jealousy will leave you. I will be quiet. I will not be angry anymore.

[43] Because you didn't remember the days of your youth — because you provoked me with all of this — I'm bringing your own path down on your head. The declaration of the Lord GOD. Did you not add this filth to all your other obscenities?

[44] Everyone who uses proverbs will use this one against you: Like mother, like daughter.

[45] You are your mother's daughter — a woman who despised her husband and her children. You are the sister of your sisters, who despised their husbands and their children. Your mother was a Hittite. Your father was an Amorite.

[46] Your older sister is Samaria, living with her daughters to the north of you. Your younger sister is Sodom, living with her daughters to the south.

[47] You didn't just walk in their ways and do their obscenities — that was too little for you. In every way, you went further into corruption than they did.

[48] As I live — the declaration of the Lord GOD — your sister Sodom and her daughters never did what you and your daughters have done.

[49] Listen — this was Sodom's guilt: she and her daughters had pride, full stomachs, comfortable peace — and they did not lift a finger for the poor and needy.

[50] They became arrogant. They did detestable things in front of me. So I removed them — as you have seen.

[51] Samaria didn't commit half your sins. You have done more disgusting things than they did. You make your sisters look righteous by comparison.

[52] So bear your disgrace. You're the one who argued your sisters' case — and your own sins have made them look better than you. You made them look righteous. So burn with shame. Carry your humiliation. You made your sisters look innocent.

[53] But I will restore them — Sodom and her daughters, Samaria and her daughters — and I will restore you along with them.

[54] So you'll bear your shame and be humiliated by everything you did — and your shame will be a comfort to them.

[55] Your sister Sodom and her daughters will return to what they were before. Samaria and her daughters will return to what they were before. And you and your daughters will return to what you were before.

[56] You wouldn't even speak Sodom's name in the day of your arrogance —

[57] before your own evil was exposed. Now you're the one being scorned — by the daughters of Aram and her neighbors, by the daughters of the Philistines, by everyone around you who despises you.

[58] You will carry the weight of your obscenities and your disgusting acts. The declaration of the LORD.

[59] This is what the Lord GOD says: I will do to you what you did — you despised the oath, you broke the covenant.

[60] But I — I will remember the covenant I made with you in the days of your youth. I will establish with you a covenant that lasts forever.

[61] Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your sisters — the older and the younger. I will give them to you as daughters, though not because of your covenant.

[62] I will establish my covenant with you. And you will know — I am the LORD.

[63] You will remember. You will be ashamed. You will never open your mouth again because of your humiliation — when I make atonement for you, for everything you have done. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, pose a riddle. Tell Israel a parable.

[3] Say this — the Lord GOD speaks: A massive eagle, vast of wing, long of pinion, dense with many-colored plumage, swept down on Lebanon and seized the crown of the cedar.

[4] He cracked off its topmost shoot, carried it to a land of merchants, and planted it in a city of traders.

[5] Then he took a seed from that land and set it in fertile soil — placed it by abundant waters, like a willow planted by a stream.

[6] It sprouted and became a vine — low to the ground, spreading wide, its branches turning toward him, its roots reaching down beneath him. So it grew into a vine, throwing out shoots, putting forth foliage.

[7] But there was another great eagle — vast of wing, thick with plumage. And this vine — from the bed where it was planted — twisted its roots toward this second eagle, stretched its branches his way, hoping he would water it.

[8] Yet it had already been planted in good soil by abundant waters, set to grow branches, bear fruit, become a magnificent vine.

[9] Say this — the Lord GOD speaks: Will it thrive? Won't the first eagle rip out its roots, strip its fruit, leave it to wither — every fresh leaf shriveling? It will take no mighty arm, no vast army, to tear it up at the roots.

[10] Look — even replanted, will it thrive? When the east wind hits, won't it wither dead away? In the very bed where it grew, it will wither.

[11] The word of the LORD came to me:

[12] Say to this rebellious house: Do you not understand what this means? Tell them — the king of Babylon came to Jerusalem, seized her king and her princes, hauled them off to Babylon with him.

[13] He took one of the royal bloodline, cut a covenant with him, put him under oath. He also carried off the leading men of the land —

[14] so that the kingdom would stay low, unable to lift itself, kept alive only by keeping that covenant.

[15] But the king rebelled. He sent envoys to Egypt — begging for horses, for a vast army. Will he succeed? Will the one who pulls such stunts escape? Will the one who breaks a covenant slip free?

[16] As I live — declares the Lord GOD — in the country of the king who crowned him, whose oath he despised, whose covenant he broke — there, in the heart of Babylon, he will die.

[17] Pharaoh and all his mighty army, all his vast horde, will be useless to him in the war — when Babylon throws up ramps and builds siege walls to cut down many lives.

[18] He despised the oath. He broke the covenant. He shook hands on the deal and then did all this. He will not escape.

[19] So this is what the Lord GOD says: As I live — my oath he despised, my covenant he broke — I will bring it down on his own head.

[20] I will throw my net over him. He will be caught in my snare. I will drag him to Babylon and put him on trial there for his treachery against me.

[21] All his finest troops, every one of them, will fall by the sword, and any survivors will be scattered to every wind. Then you will know that I, the LORD, have spoken.

[22] This is what the Lord GOD says: I myself will take a shoot from the top of the towering cedar and plant it. From the highest of its young branches I will pluck a tender sprig and plant it on a high and lofty mountain.

[23] On the mountain heights of Israel I will plant it. It will throw out branches, bear fruit, become a magnificent cedar. Every kind of bird will nest in it — find shelter in the shade of its boughs.

[24] Then every tree of the field will know that I am the LORD — I bring the tall tree low and lift the low tree high, I dry up the green tree and make the dry tree bloom. I, the LORD, have spoken. And I will do it.

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] "What is this proverb you keep repeating about the land of Israel — 'The fathers ate sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'?

[3] As I live, declares the Lord GOD, you will not quote that proverb in Israel again.

[4] Every soul belongs to me — the father's life and the son's life, both are mine. The soul that sins is the one who dies.

[5] Picture a man who is righteous, who does what is just and right:

[6] He does not eat at the mountain shrines. He does not lift his eyes to the idols of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor's wife. He does not approach a woman during her bleeding.

[7] He oppresses no one. He returns what was pledged for a loan. He robs no one. He gives bread to the hungry and clothes the naked.

[8] He lends without charging interest. He takes no profit at another's expense. He keeps his hands clean of injustice and judges honestly between people.

[9] He walks in my statutes and keeps my laws faithfully. That man is righteous. He will live, declares the Lord GOD.

[10] But suppose this man fathers a violent son — a man who sheds blood, who does any one of these things

[11] (though the father did none of them): he eats at the mountain shrines, defiles his neighbor's wife,

[12] crushes the poor and needy, robs, refuses to return what was pledged, lifts his eyes to idols, commits abominations,

[13] lends at interest and takes a cut. Will this son live? He will not. He has done every one of these detestable things — he will surely die. His blood is on his own head.

[14] Now suppose this violent man fathers a son who sees all his father's sins, watches them, and refuses to follow:

[15] He does not eat at the mountain shrines. He does not lift his eyes to the idols of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor's wife.

[16] He oppresses no one. He demands no pledge. He robs nothing. He gives bread to the hungry and clothes the naked.

[17] He keeps his hand from injustice, takes no interest or profit, follows my laws, walks in my statutes. He will not die for his father's sin. He will live.

[18] As for the father — because he extorted, robbed his brother, did what was wrong among his people — he dies for his own sin.

[19] Yet you ask, 'Why doesn't the son carry the father's guilt?' Because the son did what is just and right. He kept all my statutes and lived by them. He will live.

[20] The soul that sins is the one who dies. A son does not carry his father's guilt, and a father does not carry his son's. The righteousness of the righteous stays with the righteous; the wickedness of the wicked stays with the wicked.

[21] And if the wicked man turns — turns away from every sin he committed, keeps all my statutes, does what is just and right — he will live. He will not die.

[22] None of the offenses he committed will be held against him. Because of the righteousness he now practices, he will live.

[23] Do you think I take pleasure in the death of the wicked? declares the Lord GOD. Don't I want him to turn from his ways and live?

[24] But if a righteous man abandons his righteousness and does evil — every detestable thing the wicked do — will he live? Not one of his righteous deeds will be remembered. He dies in the treachery he has committed, in the sin he has chosen.

[25] And you say, 'The Lord's way is not fair.' Listen, house of Israel: Is my way unfair, or are yours?

[26] When a righteous man turns from his righteousness and does evil, he dies for it. He dies for the evil he has done.

[27] And when a wicked man turns from his wickedness and does what is just and right, he saves his own life.

[28] Because he looked, saw, and turned from every offense he committed, he will live. He will not die.

[29] Still the house of Israel says, 'The Lord's way is not fair.' Is my way unfair, house of Israel, or are yours?

[30] So I will judge each of you, house of Israel, according to your own ways, declares the Lord GOD. Turn. Turn from every offense, before guilt brings you down.

[31] Throw off every sin you have committed. Get yourselves a new heart and a new spirit. Why should you die, house of Israel?

[32] I take no pleasure in anyone's death, declares the Lord GOD. So turn — and live."

[1] Now lift up a lament for the princes of Israel.

[2] Say this:

What was your mother? A lioness among lions—
she stretched out among the young lions
and raised her cubs.

[3] One cub she brought up,
and he became a young lion.
He learned to tear his prey.
He devoured men.

[4] The nations heard about him.
They trapped him in their pit
and dragged him with hooks
down to the land of Egypt.

[5] When she saw her waiting was wasted,
her hope shattered,
she took another of her cubs
and made him a young lion.

[6] He prowled among the lions,
now a young lion himself.
He learned to tear his prey.
He devoured men.

[7] He battered their strongholds,
he laid their cities waste.
The land and everyone in it shuddered
at the thunder of his roar.

[8] Then the nations closed in on him
from every province around.
They spread their net over him,
and he was caught in their pit.

[9] They locked him in a cage with hooks
and hauled him to the king of Babylon.
They brought him into the strongholds
so his voice would never be heard again
on the mountains of Israel.

[10] Your mother was like a vine—
in your blood, planted by the water,
fruitful and full of branches
because the waters ran deep.

[11] She grew strong limbs,
fit for the scepters of rulers.
Her stature towered
among the thick boughs,
visible in her height,
in the mass of her branches.

[12] But she was uprooted in fury,
hurled to the ground.
The east wind dried her out.
Her fruit was stripped away.
Her strong limbs withered—
fire devoured them.

[13] Now she's planted in the wilderness,
in a dry and thirsty land.

[14] Fire has come out from her own branch,
consumed her fruit,
and left her with no strong limb,
no scepter fit to rule.

This is a lament. And it has become a lament.

[1] In the seventh year, on the tenth day of the fifth month, some of Israel's elders came to inquire of the LORD. They sat down in front of me.

[2] The word of the LORD hit me:

[3] "Son of man, speak to Israel's elders. Tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says — Have you come to consult me? As I live, I will not be consulted by you. Declaration of the Sovereign LORD.

[4] Will you judge them, son of man? Will you judge them? Then confront them with the atrocities of their ancestors.

[5] Tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says — On the day I chose Israel, I raised my hand in oath to the offspring of Jacob's house. I revealed myself to them in Egypt. I raised my hand and swore: I am the LORD your God.

[6] That same day I swore I would bring them out of Egypt into a land I had scouted for them — flowing with milk and honey, the jewel of every land.

[7] I told them: Each of you, throw away the obscenities you've been staring at. Don't defile yourselves with the gutter-gods of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.

[8] But they rebelled. They refused to listen. Not one of them threw away the obscenities they were staring at. None abandoned the gutter-gods of Egypt. So I said I would pour out my fury on them, spend my anger on them right there in the land of Egypt.

[9] But I acted for the sake of my name — so it wouldn't be dragged through the mud in front of the nations they lived among, the nations who had watched me reveal myself to bring Israel out of Egypt.

[10] So I brought them out of Egypt and led them into the wilderness.

[11] I gave them my statutes. I made my judgments known to them — the path by which a person lives, if they live by it.

[12] I also gave them my Sabbaths as a sign between us, so they would know that I, the LORD, am the One who sets them apart.

[13] But Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness. They didn't walk in my statutes. They threw away my judgments — the very path by which a person lives. They desecrated my Sabbaths, again and again. So I said I would pour out my fury on them in the wilderness and finish them.

[14] But I acted for the sake of my name — so it wouldn't be dragged through the mud in front of the nations who had watched me bring them out.

[15] Still, I raised my hand in the wilderness and swore I would not bring them into the land I had given them — flowing with milk and honey, the jewel of every land —

[16] because they had thrown away my judgments, refused to walk in my statutes, and desecrated my Sabbaths. Their hearts kept chasing their gutter-gods.

[17] Yet I looked on them with pity. I didn't destroy them. I didn't wipe them out in the wilderness.

[18] In the wilderness I said to their children: Don't walk in the ways of your parents. Don't keep their rulings. Don't defile yourselves with their gutter-gods.

[19] I am the LORD your God. Walk in my statutes. Keep my judgments. Live them.

[20] Set apart my Sabbaths. Let them be a sign between us, so you'll know I am the LORD your God.

[21] But the children rebelled too. They didn't walk in my statutes. They didn't keep my judgments — didn't live them, the very path by which a person lives. They desecrated my Sabbaths. So I said I would pour out my fury on them, spend my anger on them in the wilderness.

[22] But I held back my hand. I acted for the sake of my name — so it wouldn't be dragged through the mud in front of the nations who had watched me bring them out.

[23] Still, I raised my hand in the wilderness and swore I would scatter them among the nations, fling them across the lands —

[24] because they hadn't kept my judgments, had thrown away my statutes, had desecrated my Sabbaths, and their eyes were locked on the gutter-gods of their ancestors.

[25] So I gave them over to statutes that weren't good and judgments by which they could not live.

[26] I let them defile themselves with their own gifts — letting them pass every firstborn through the fire — so I could devastate them, so they would know I am the LORD.

[27] So speak to the house of Israel, son of man. Tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says — Even in this your ancestors blasphemed me by their treachery against me.

[28] When I brought them into the land I had sworn to give them, they looked around at every high hill, every leafy tree, and there they offered their sacrifices, presented their provocations, sent up their pleasing aromas, poured out their drink offerings.

[29] I asked them: What's this high place you keep running to? They still call it Bamah — the high place — to this day.

[30] So tell the house of Israel: This is what the Sovereign LORD says — Are you defiling yourselves the way your ancestors did? Whoring after their obscenities?

[31] When you lift up your gifts, when you pass your children through the fire, you are still defiling yourselves with every gutter-god of yours — to this day. And you expect me to be consulted by you, house of Israel? As I live, declaration of the Sovereign LORD, I will not be consulted by you.

[32] What you imagine will never happen. You keep saying, 'Let us be like the nations, like the tribes of the lands — serving wood and stone.'

[33] As I live, declaration of the Sovereign LORD, I will rule over you with a strong hand, an outstretched arm, and fury poured out.

[34] I will bring you out from the peoples. I will gather you from the lands where you've been scattered — with a strong hand, an outstretched arm, and fury poured out.

[35] I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples. And there, face to face, I will enter judgment with you.

[36] Just as I entered judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of Egypt, so I will enter judgment with you. Declaration of the Sovereign LORD.

[37] I will make you pass under the shepherd's rod. I will bring you into the bond of the covenant.

[38] I will purge from you the rebels — the ones who have broken faith with me. I will bring them out of the land where they're staying, but they will not enter the land of Israel. Then you will know I am the LORD.

[39] As for you, house of Israel — this is what the Sovereign LORD says — Go on, then. Each of you, serve your gutter-gods. But after this — you will listen to me. And you will no longer profane my holy name with your gifts and your idols.

[40] For on my holy mountain — the high mountain of Israel, declaration of the Sovereign LORD — there the whole house of Israel, all of them, will serve me in the land. There I will accept them. There I will require your contributions, the firstfruits of your offerings, all your holy gifts.

[41] I will accept you as a pleasing aroma when I bring you out from the peoples and gather you from the lands where you've been scattered. Through you I will show myself holy in the eyes of the nations.

[42] Then you will know I am the LORD — when I bring you into the land of Israel, the land I raised my hand and swore to give your ancestors.

[43] There you will remember your ways, every deed by which you defiled yourselves. You will loathe yourselves to your face for all the evils you committed.

[44] And you will know I am the LORD when I deal with you for the sake of my name — not according to your evil ways, not according to your corrupt deeds, house of Israel. Declaration of the Sovereign LORD.

[45] The word of the LORD hit me:

[46] "Son of man, set your face toward the south. Preach against the south. Prophesy against the forest of the Negev.

[47] Tell the forest of the Negev: Hear the word of the LORD. This is what the Sovereign LORD says — I am about to kindle a fire in you. It will devour every green tree in you and every dry tree. The blazing flame will not be put out. Every face from south to north will be scorched by it.

[48] Every living thing will see that I, the LORD, kindled it. It will not be put out."

[49] Then I said, "Sovereign LORD, they're saying about me, 'Isn't he just spinning riddles?'"

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, set your face toward Jerusalem. Preach against her sanctuaries. Prophesy against the soil of Israel.

[3] Tell her: This is what the LORD says — I am against you. I am drawing my sword from its sheath, and I will cut down the righteous and the wicked alike.

[4] Because I am cutting off righteous and wicked together, my sword will leave its sheath against every living thing from south to north.

[5] Then everyone alive will know that I, the LORD, have unsheathed my sword. It will not return.

[6] As for you, son of man — groan. Groan with a broken back, groan with bitter anguish, groan where they can see you.

[7] And when they ask, "Why are you groaning?" — tell them: Because of the news. It is coming. Every heart will dissolve, every hand go slack, every spirit faint, every knee turn to water. Look — it is on its way. It will happen. So says the Lord GOD.

[8] The word of the LORD came to me again:

[9] Son of man, prophesy. Say: This is what the Lord says —

A sword, a sword,
sharpened and polished —

[10] sharpened for slaughter,
polished to flash like lightning.

Shall we celebrate? The rod of my son despises every tree.

[11] The sword is given to be polished, gripped in the hand. Honed and burnished, ready for the killer's fist.

[12] Cry out, son of man. Wail. This sword is coming for my people, for every prince of Israel. They are thrown to the blade alongside my people. So beat your thigh in grief.

[13] The test is here. And what if even the despising rod proves nothing? declares the Lord GOD.

[14] So prophesy, son of man. Clap your hands. Let the sword strike twice — strike three times. The sword of slaughter. The sword of the great slaughter that surrounds them,

[15] so hearts melt and many fall at every gate. I have set the flashing edge — yes, wrapped for slaughter, honed for lightning.

[16] Swing right, sword. Cut left. Go wherever the edge is pointed.

[17] I too will clap my hands and let my fury settle. I, the LORD, have spoken.

[18] The word of the LORD came to me again:

[19] Son of man, draw two roads for the king of Babylon's sword to travel — both branching from the same country. Set up a signpost where the road forks toward the city.

[20] Mark one road for the sword to reach Rabbah of the Ammonites, the other to reach Judah and fortified Jerusalem.

[21] For the king of Babylon stands at the fork, at the head of the two roads, working his divinations: shaking arrows, consulting the household gods, reading the liver.

[22] In his right hand falls the lot for Jerusalem — to set up battering rams, to give the order to butcher, to raise the war shout, to plant rams against the gates, to throw up a siege ramp and build the walls of siege.

[23] To the people of Jerusalem this looks like a false omen — they swore loyalty oaths, they trust those. But he will remind them of their guilt, and they will be captured.

[24] Therefore the Lord GOD says: Because you have broadcast your guilt, exposing your rebellions so that every act lays your sins bare — because you have made yourselves remembered, you will be seized by the hand.

[25] And you — defiled, wicked prince of Israel, your day has arrived, your final reckoning has come —

[26] this is what the Lord GOD says: Tear off the turban. Lift off the crown. Nothing will stay as it was. Raise the low. Bring down the high.

[27] Ruin, ruin, ruin — I will make it ruin. And it will not be restored until he comes whose right it is. To him I will give it.

[28] And you, son of man, prophesy. Say: This is what the Lord GOD says about the Ammonites and their taunts —

Say: A sword, a sword!
Drawn for the slaughter,
polished to consume,
flashing like lightning —

[29] though they see false visions for you, though they read lying omens — the sword will fall on the necks of the defiled wicked, whose day has come, whose final reckoning has arrived.

[30] Return the blade to its sheath. In the place where you were created, in the land where you were born, I will judge you.

[31] I will pour out my fury on you. I will blast you with the fire of my wrath. I will hand you over to brutal men, master craftsmen of destruction.

[32] You will be fuel for the fire. Your blood will soak into the middle of the land. You will not be remembered. I, the LORD, have spoken.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] "Son of man—will you judge her? Will you judge the city of blood? Then make her face every last thing she's done.

[3] Tell her: This is what the Lord GOD says—City that spills blood inside her own walls so her hour will come, city that builds her idols to defile herself—

[4] You've made yourself guilty with the blood you've shed. You've made yourself filthy with the idols you've made. You've rushed your own ending. You've reached the end of your years. So I'm handing you over—a mockery to the nations, a joke in every land.

[5] Near and far, they'll laugh at you, you city of ruined name, city drowning in chaos.

[6] Look at them—every prince of Israel inside you, each one using his power to spill blood.

[7] Inside you, they treat father and mother like dirt. Inside you, they crush the immigrant. Inside you, they trample the orphan and the widow.

[8] My holy things—you despise them. My Sabbaths—you profane them.

[9] Inside you, slanderers hunt down lives. Inside you, they feast at the mountain shrines. Inside you, they commit obscenity.

[10] Inside you, men strip their fathers' beds bare. Inside you, they force themselves on women bleeding and unclean.

[11] One man does the unspeakable with his neighbor's wife. Another defiles his daughter-in-law in his own perversion. Another violates his sister, his own father's daughter—inside you.

[12] Inside you, they take bribes to shed blood. You charge interest. You gouge. You bleed your neighbor dry by extortion—and me, you've forgotten. Declaration of the Lord GOD.

[13] So watch—I'm slamming my fist down at the profit you've stolen and the blood that runs through your streets.

[14] Will your heart hold up? Will your hands stay steady on the day I deal with you? I, the LORD, have spoken, and I will act.

[15] I'll scatter you among the nations. I'll throw you across the lands. I'll burn the filth out of you.

[16] You'll be desecrated in the eyes of the nations—by your own doing. And then you'll know I am the LORD."

[17] The word of the LORD came to me:

[18] "Son of man, the house of Israel has turned into slag for me. All of them—copper, tin, iron, lead inside the furnace. Slag. Pure waste from the silver.

[19] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Because every one of you has become slag, I'm gathering you into the middle of Jerusalem.

[20] The way they pile silver and copper and iron and lead and tin into a furnace and crank the fire to melt it down—that's how I'll gather you in my anger and my wrath. I'll dump you in. I'll melt you down.

[23] The word of the LORD came to me:

[25] Her princes inside her are a pack of lions snarling over the kill. They devour lives. They seize wealth and treasure. They've made widows by the dozens inside her.

[26] Her priests do violence to my law and desecrate my holy things. They don't distinguish between holy and common. They don't teach the difference between unclean and clean. They look right past my Sabbaths—and I am profaned among them.

[27] Her officials inside her are wolves tearing apart prey—spilling blood, killing lives, all to make a profit.

[28] Her prophets paint over it all with whitewash—visions that are lies, divinations that are fraud. 'This is what the Lord GOD says,' they announce—when the LORD has said nothing.

[29] The people of the land run on extortion. They mug the poor and the needy. They abuse the immigrant. No justice for any of them.

[30] I went looking for someone—anyone—to build the wall, to stand in the gap before me on behalf of the land, so I wouldn't have to destroy it. I found no one.

[31] So I'm pouring out my rage on them. I'm finishing them with the fire of my fury. Their own way—I'm putting it back on their own heads. Declaration of the Lord GOD."

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] Son of man, two women — daughters of the same mother.

[3] They sold themselves in Egypt. Sold themselves young. There their breasts were handled, their virgin nipples fondled.

[4] The older was Oholah. Her sister, Oholibah. They became mine. They bore me sons and daughters. As for their names: Oholah is Samaria. Oholibah is Jerusalem.

[6] dressed in royal blue, governors and commanders, every one of them a beautiful young man, every one of them a horseman.

[7] She gave her body to all of them, the elite of Assyria. Every man she wanted, she defiled herself with him — and with all their filthy idols too.

[8] She never let go of the whoring she started in Egypt. Men had taken her in her youth, mauled her virgin breasts, poured their lust into her — and she stayed in it.

[9] So I handed her over. Handed her to the lovers she craved — to Assyria, the ones she burned for.

[10] They stripped her naked. Took her sons and daughters. Killed her with the sword. She became a cautionary tale among women — judgment had been carried out.

[11] Her sister Oholibah watched all this. And then went worse. Her lust was more depraved than her sister's, her whoring more reckless.

[12] She too burned for the Assyrians — governors and commanders, soldiers in full uniform, horsemen, every one of them a beautiful young man.

[13] I saw it. She had defiled herself the same way. The two sisters walked the same road.

[14] But she pushed further. She saw men carved on a wall — figures of Chaldeans etched in red,

[15] belts at their waists, flowing turbans on their heads, every one of them looking like an officer, looking like Babylon — Chaldea, their birthplace.

[16] One look and she was on fire for them. She sent messengers to them in Chaldea.

[17] So the Babylonians came to her, came to her bed of love, and defiled her with their lust. And after they had used her — she turned from them in disgust.

[18] When her whoring went public, when she exposed herself for all to see, I turned from her in disgust, the way I had turned from her sister.

[19] But she only multiplied her whoring, remembering her girlhood in Egypt, when she sold herself there.

[20] She lusted after their gigolos — men hung like donkeys, men who came like horses.

[21] You went back to the obscenity of your youth — back to Egypt, where they handled your young breasts, where they fondled the breasts of a girl.

[22] So this is what the Lord GOD says, Oholibah: I am going to wake up your lovers against you — the very ones you turned from in disgust. I will bring them against you from every side —

[23] the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans, Pekod and Shoa and Koa, and all the Assyrians with them — beautiful young men, governors and commanders, officers and warriors, every one of them riding.

[24] They will come at you with weapons, with chariots and wagons and a horde of nations. Shields and helmets and armor will close in on you from every direction. I will hand you over for judgment, and they will judge you by their own laws.

[26] They will strip the clothes off you and take your jewelry.

[27] That is how I will put an end to your obscenity, to the whoring you brought from Egypt. You will never look back at it again. You will never remember Egypt again.

[28] For this is what the Lord GOD says: I am about to hand you over to those you hate — to those you turned from in disgust.

[29] They will treat you with hatred. They will take everything you worked for and leave you naked and bare. The shame of your whoring will be exposed — the obscenity, the lust —
