[31] You walked your sister's road. So I am putting her cup in your hand.

[32] This is what the Lord GOD says:

You will drink your sister's cup —
deep and wide,
brimming with mockery and contempt,
holding more than you can hold.

[33] You will fill up with drunkenness and grief —
a cup of horror and devastation —
the cup of your sister Samaria.

[34] You will drink it. Drain it.
Chew on the broken shards.
Tear at your own breasts.

I have spoken. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[35] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Because you forgot me, because you threw me behind your back — you carry the weight of your obscenity, you carry the weight of your whoring.

[36] The LORD said to me: Son of man, will you judge Oholah and Oholibah? Then confront them with their atrocities.

[37] They have committed adultery. Blood is on their hands. They committed adultery with their idols. The children they bore me — they made them pass through the fire, food for those things.

[38] And there is more. They defiled my sanctuary on the same day. Trampled my Sabbaths.

[39] After slaughtering their children to their idols, they walked into my sanctuary the same day to desecrate it. Inside my own house — that is what they did.

[40] More than that — they sent for men to come from far away. Messengers were dispatched, and the men came. And for them, you bathed. You painted your eyes. You put on your jewelry.

[41] You stretched out on a luxurious couch with a table laid before it — and on that table you set my incense and my oil.

[42] Around her, the noise of a careless crowd — drunkards hauled in from the desert. They put bracelets on the sisters' wrists and crowns of beauty on their heads.

[43] And I said of the one worn out by adultery: now they will whore with her, and she with them.

[44] They went in to her — the way a man goes in to a prostitute. That is how they went in to Oholah and Oholibah, women of obscenity.

[45] But righteous men will pass sentence on them — the sentence given to adulteresses, the sentence given to women who shed blood. Because that is what they are. Adulteresses. Murderers. Blood on their hands.

[46] This is what the Lord GOD says: Bring up an army against them. Hand them over to terror and plunder.

[47] The army will stone them, cut them down with swords, slaughter their sons and daughters, burn their houses to the ground.

[48] That is how I will purge this obscenity from the land — a warning to every woman not to imitate it.

[49] They will pay for their obscenity. They will carry the consequences of their idol-worship. And then you will know that I am the Lord GOD.

[1] In the ninth year, tenth month, tenth day, the word of the LORD hit me:

[2] Son of man, write this date down. Etch it in. This very day — the king of Babylon has thrown himself against Jerusalem. Today.

[3] Now speak a parable to this house of rebels. Tell them: This is what the Lord GOD says — Set the pot on the fire. Set it. Pour the water in.

[4] Throw in the cuts of meat — every good piece, thigh and shoulder. Fill it with the choicest bones.

[5] Take the pick of the flock. Stack the wood beneath it. Bring it to a roiling boil. Let the bones themselves seethe inside it.

[6] So says the Lord GOD: Doom to the city of blood — that corroded pot, the rust still clinging to it, refusing to come clean. Empty it piece by piece. No drawing of lots. Just out.

[7] Because the blood she spilled is still inside her. She poured it out on bare rock — not on the ground where dust could cover it.

[8] To raise my fury, to bring the reckoning, I left that blood exposed on naked stone — uncovered, accusing.

[9] So says the Lord GOD: Doom to the city of blood. I myself will build the pyre high.

[10] Heap on the wood. Light the fire. Cook the meat down. Spice it. Let the bones char.

[11] Then set the empty pot on the coals until the bronze glows red, until the filth inside it melts, until the corrosion is burned away.

[12] But she has worn me out with her resistance. The thick rust refuses to leave her. Into the fire with the rust.

[13] Your filth is depravity. I tried to cleanse you and you would not be cleansed. So you will not be clean again — not until I have spent my fury on you.

[14] I, the LORD, have spoken. It is coming. I will do it. I will not pull back, will not spare, will not relent. By your ways and your deeds you will be judged. Word of the Lord GOD.

[15] Then the word of the LORD came to me:

[16] Son of man — listen. I am about to take from you, with one stroke, the delight of your eyes. Do not mourn. Do not weep. Do not let a single tear fall.

[17] Groan in silence. Make no funeral. Bind your turban on your head. Put your sandals on your feet. Do not cover your lip. Do not eat the bread of mourners.

[18] I spoke to the people in the morning. By evening my wife was dead. The next morning I did exactly what I had been told.

[19] And the people said: Are you not going to tell us what this means — what this performance of yours has to do with us?

[20] So I told them: The word of the LORD came to me, saying —

[21] Speak to the house of Israel: This is what the Lord GOD says — I am about to desecrate my sanctuary, that fortress you take such pride in, the delight of your eyes, the longing of your soul. And the sons and daughters you left behind — they will fall by the sword.

[22] Then you will do exactly what I have done. You will not cover your lip. You will not eat the bread of mourners.

[23] Your turbans will stay on your heads, your sandals on your feet. You will not wail. You will not weep. You will waste away in your own guilt and groan to one another in the dark.

[24] Ezekiel is your sign. Everything he did, you will do. When it happens — then you will know that I am the Lord GOD.

[25] And you, son of man — on the day I take from them their stronghold, their joy and their glory, the delight of their eyes, the lifting of their souls, their sons and their daughters —

[26] on that day a refugee will come to you with the news, to deliver it to your ears.

[27] On that day, when the fugitive arrives, your mouth will open. You will speak. You will not be silent any longer. You will be a sign to them — and they will know that I am the LORD.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] Son of man, set your face against Ammon. Prophesy against them.

[3] Tell the Ammonites: Hear the word of the Sovereign LORD. This is what the Sovereign LORD says — because you sneered "Ha!" over my sanctuary when it was desecrated, over the land of Israel when it was laid waste, over the house of Judah when they marched into exile —

[4] for that, I am handing you over to the people of the East. They will pitch their camps in you, plant their tents in you. They will eat your fruit. They will drink your milk.

[5] I will turn Rabbah into pasture for camels and the whole of Ammon into a sheep pen. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

[6] For this is what the Sovereign LORD says: Because you clapped your hands and stamped your feet and reveled in malice with every ounce of your soul against the land of Israel —

[7] for that, I am stretching out my hand against you. I will hand you over as plunder to the nations. I will cut you off from the peoples. I will erase you from the lands. I will destroy you. Then you will know that I am the LORD.

[8] This is what the Sovereign LORD says: Because Moab and Seir said, "Look — the house of Judah is just like every other nation" —

[9] for that, I will rip open Moab's flank, strip her of her cities, her frontier cities, the jewels of her land — Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, Kiriathaim.

[10] I am giving Moab, along with Ammon, to the people of the East as their possession, so that Ammon will not be remembered among the nations.

[11] On Moab I will execute judgment. Then they will know that I am the LORD.

[12] This is what the Sovereign LORD says: Because Edom took vengeance on the house of Judah and racked up bloodguilt by doing it —

[13] for that, this is what the Sovereign LORD says: I am stretching out my hand against Edom. I will cut down its people and its animals. I will leave it a wasteland from Teman to Dedan — they will fall by the sword.

[14] I will lay my vengeance on Edom by the hand of my people Israel. They will deal with Edom according to my fury, my burning anger. And Edom will know what my vengeance feels like, declares the Sovereign LORD.

[15] This is what the Sovereign LORD says: Because the Philistines acted in vengeance — took vengeance with venom in their soul, bent on destruction out of an ancient grudge —

[16] for that, this is what the Sovereign LORD says: I am stretching out my hand against the Philistines. I will cut down the Cherethites. I will wipe out the remnant of the coast.

[17] I will execute massive vengeance on them with furious rebukes. And when I lay my vengeance on them — they will know that I am the LORD.

[1] In the eleventh year, on the first of the month, the word of the LORD came to me:

[2] "Son of man, because Tyre crowed over Jerusalem—'Ha! The gateway of the nations is shattered! It swings open to me now. She lies in ruins—I'll feast on her remains'—

[3] therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: I am against you, Tyre. I will hurl nations against you the way the sea hurls its waves.

[4] They will smash the walls of Tyre and tear down her towers. I will scrape her soil away until nothing is left but bare rock.

[5] She will become a drying-rack for fishermen out in the open sea. I have spoken—declares the Lord GOD. The nations will pick her clean.

[6] Her daughter-villages on the mainland will be cut down by the sword. Then they will know that I am the LORD.

[7] For this is what the Lord GOD says: I am bringing Nebuchadnezzar against Tyre from the north—king of kings—with horses and chariots and cavalry, a vast army crashing down.

[8] Your mainland villages he will put to the sword. He will throw up siege-works against you, raise a ramp, lift a wall of shields.

[9] He will swing his battering rams against your walls and pry your towers apart with iron.

[10] His horses will be so many that their dust will bury you. Your walls will shake from the thunder of cavalry, wagons, and chariots when he marches through your gates like men entering a city already breached.

[11] His horses' hooves will trample every one of your streets. He will cut down your people with the sword, and your mighty pillars will crash to the ground.

[12] They will plunder your wealth and loot your goods. They will smash your walls and tear down your beautiful houses. Your stones, your timbers, your rubble—they will dump it all into the sea.

[13] I will silence the sound of your songs. The music of your harps will never be heard again.

[14] I will leave you bare rock, a place where fishermen spread their nets. You will never be rebuilt. I, the LORD, have spoken—declares the Lord GOD.

[15] This is what the Lord GOD says to Tyre: The coastlands will tremble at the sound of your fall, at the groans of the dying, the slaughter ripping through your streets.

[16] Every prince of the sea will step down from his throne, strip off his robes, peel away his embroidered clothes. Dressed in dread, they will sit on the ground, shuddering moment by moment, stunned at the sight of you.

[17] They will lift a lament over you and say: "How you have vanished from the seas, O famed city—you who ruled the waves, you and your people, who terrorized everyone on your shores!

[18] Now the coastlands shudder on the day you fall. The islands of the sea are shaken by your ending."

[19] For this is what the Lord GOD says: When I make you a ruined city, like the cities no one lives in, when I draw the deep up over you and the great waters cover you,

[20] then I will send you down with those who descend to the pit, down to the people of old. I will settle you in the underworld, in ruins ancient as time, with those who go down to the pit—so that you will never come back, never take your place in the land of the living.

[21] I will make you a horror. You will be no more. They will search for you and never find you again—declares the Lord GOD."

[1] The word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, raise a funeral song over Tyre.

[3] Tell her — you who sit at the gateway of the sea, merchant to the nations across countless coastlands, this is what the Lord GOD says:

Tyre, you have said it yourself — *I am beauty perfected.*

[4] Your borders ran out into the heart of the sea, and your builders made you stunning beyond words.

[5] They shaped your hull from cypress out of Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to raise your mast.

[6] Your oars were carved from the oaks of Bashan; your deck inlaid with ivory set in cypress from the coasts of Cyprus.

[7] Your sail — embroidered linen from Egypt, the kind that doubles as your flag. Your awnings — blue and purple from the shores of Elishah.

[8] Sidon and Arvad worked your oars; your own seasoned hands, Tyre, were the pilots aboard.

[9] The elders of Byblos, her veteran shipwrights, sealed your seams. Every ship on the sea and every sailor came to trade in your markets.

[10] Persians, Lydians, Libyans served in your army — your warriors. They hung shield and helmet inside you. They gave you your glory.

[11] The men of Arvad and your own troops manned your walls; men of Gammad watched your towers. They draped their shields all around your battlements — they completed your beauty.

[12] Tarshish traded with you because of your enormous wealth — silver, iron, tin, lead — they exchanged for your goods.

[13] Greece, Tubal, Meshech — they trafficked with you too. Human beings and bronze vessels were their currency.

[14] From Beth-togarmah came the horses, the war-horses, the mules.

[15] The men of Dedan were your traders; many coastlands were your customers, paying you in ivory tusks and ebony.

[16] Aram traded with you because your products were endless — turquoise, purple cloth, embroidery, fine linen, coral, rubies.

[17] Judah and the land of Israel traded with you — wheat from Minnith, millet, honey, oil, balm.

[18] Damascus bought your goods because of your massive output — wine from Helbon, wool from Zahar.

[19] Vedan and Javan from Uzal brought wrought iron, cassia, sweet cane for your market.

[20] Dedan traded saddle-blankets for riding.

[21] Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your customers — lambs, rams, goats.

[22] The merchants of Sheba and Raamah came with the finest spices, every kind of precious stone, gold.

[23] Haran, Canneh, Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, Chilmad — all of them traded with you,

[24] hauling in your markets the finest garments, blue cloth, embroidery, multicolored carpets bound with twisted cords.

[25] Ships of Tarshish were your caravans, hauling your cargo.

So you became gorged, weighed down with glory in the heart of the seas.

[26] Your oarsmen rowed you out into deep waters — and the east wind shattered you there, in the heart of the seas.

[27] Your wealth, your goods, your wares, your crew, your sailors and shipwrights, your traders and your soldiers, every soul on board — all of it sinks into the heart of the sea on the day you go down.

[28] At the sound of your pilots' wailing the open country shakes.

[29] Every oarsman, every sailor, every pilot of the sea steps off his ship and stands on the shore.

[30] They lift their voices over you, wailing bitterly. They throw dust on their heads. They roll in ashes.

[31] They shave themselves bald for you, dress in sackcloth, weep for you with bitter souls, with bitter mourning.

[32] In their grief they raise a song over you, a dirge:

*Who was ever like Tyre, silent now in the middle of the sea?*

[33] When your goods came up from the seas, you filled the bellies of many peoples. With your endless wealth and merchandise you made the kings of the earth rich.

[34] Now the sea has broken you. You are wrecked in the deep waters. Your cargo, your crew, sunk with you.

[35] Every coastland is appalled. Their kings shudder. Their faces twist.

[36] The traders of the peoples hiss at you. You have become a horror, and you are gone. Forever.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me again:

[2] Son of man, tell the ruler of Tyre — this is what the Lord GOD says:

Your heart swelled with pride. You said, "I am a god. I sit enthroned among the gods, surrounded by the sea." But you are a man, not a god — no matter how godlike you imagine your mind to be.

[3] Sure — you're wiser than Daniel. No secret eludes you.

[4] By your shrewdness, by your insight, you built yourself a fortune — gold and silver stacked in your vaults.

[5] Your genius for trade multiplied your wealth, and your wealth made your heart even more arrogant.

[6] So this is what the Lord GOD says: because you imagine your mind is godlike —

[7] watch. I am bringing foreigners against you, the most ruthless of the nations. They will draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom. They will gut your splendor.

[8] They will drag you down to the pit. You will die a violent death in the heart of the sea.

[9] Will you still say "I am a god" when you face your killers? You'll be a man, nothing more, in the hands of the ones running you through.

[10] You will die the death of the uncircumcised, killed by foreign hands. I have spoken. Declaration of the Lord GOD.

[11] The word of the LORD came to me:

[12] Son of man, take up a lament for the king of Tyre. Tell him — this is what the Lord GOD says:

You were the seal of perfection — brimming with wisdom, flawless in beauty.

[13] You walked in Eden, the garden of God. Every precious stone adorned you — ruby, topaz, diamond, beryl, onyx, jasper, sapphire, turquoise, emerald — all set in gold. The craftsmanship was prepared for you on the day you were created.

[14] I appointed you — a guardian cherub, wings spread. You stood on God's holy mountain. You walked among the stones of fire.

[15] You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created — until the day wickedness was found in you.

[16] Your vast commerce filled you with violence, and you sinned. So I drove you in disgrace from the mountain of God. I expelled you, guardian cherub, from among the stones of fire.

[17] Your beauty made your heart proud. Your splendor corrupted your wisdom. So I threw you to the ground. I made you a spectacle before kings.

[18] By the weight of your guilt, by your crooked trade, you defiled your sanctuaries. So I drew fire out of you — it consumed you. I reduced you to ash on the ground in the sight of every onlooker.

[19] All who knew you among the nations are stunned. You came to a horror of an end — and you are gone forever.

[20] The word of the LORD came to me:

[21] Son of man, set your face against Sidon. Prophesy against her.

[22] Say — this is what the Lord GOD says:

Sidon, I am against you. I will be glorified in your midst. They will know I am the LORD when I bring judgment down on her and reveal my holiness in her.

[23] I will send plague into her streets and blood through her alleys. The slain will fall inside her walls, struck by swords pressing in from every side. Then they will know I am the LORD.

[24] No more will Israel be cut by malicious thorns or torn by sharp briers from any of the neighbors who treat her with contempt. Then they will know I am the Lord GOD.

[25] This is what the Lord GOD says: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples where they have been scattered, I will show my holiness in them in the eyes of the nations. They will live on their own soil — the land I gave to my servant Jacob.

[26] They will live there in safety. They will build houses, plant vineyards, settle into security — once I have executed judgment on every neighbor who held them in contempt. Then they will know that I am the LORD their God.

[1] In the tenth year, the tenth month, on the twelfth day, the word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh, king of Egypt. Prophesy against him and against all of Egypt.

[3] Speak. Tell him: This is what the Lord GOD says — Look at me, Pharaoh, king of Egypt. I am coming for you, you massive sea-beast sprawled in the middle of your rivers, the one who says, "The Nile is mine. I made it for myself."

[4] I will drive hooks through your jaws. I will make the fish of your rivers cling to your scales. I will haul you up out of your waters with every fish of your rivers stuck fast to your hide.

[5] I will fling you out into the wilderness — you and every fish of your rivers. You will fall on the open field. No one will gather you, no one will bury you. I have given you as food to the wild animals and the birds of the sky.

[6] Then everyone living in Egypt will know that I am the LORD — because they were a reed-staff to the house of Israel.

[7] When they gripped you in their hand, you splintered and tore open their shoulder. When they leaned on you, you snapped and twisted their hips.

[8] So this is what the Lord GOD says: I am bringing a sword against you. I will cut off from you both human and beast.

[9] The land of Egypt will become wasteland and ruin. Then they will know that I am the LORD — because he said, "The Nile is mine. I made it."

[10] So look — I am against you and against your rivers. I will turn the land of Egypt into utter ruin, a desolation from Migdol to Syene, all the way to the border of Cush.

[11] No human foot will pass through it. No animal foot will pass through it. It will be uninhabited for forty years.

[12] I will make the land of Egypt a desolation among desolated lands. Its cities, among ruined cities, will lie waste for forty years. I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them through the countries.

[13] But this is what the Lord GOD says: At the end of forty years I will gather the Egyptians from the peoples where they were scattered.

[14] I will restore their fortunes and bring them back to the land of Pathros, the land of their origin. There they will be a lowly kingdom —

[15] the lowliest of kingdoms. Never again will it rise above the nations. I will make them so small they cannot rule the nations anymore.

[16] Never again will Egypt be the confidence of the house of Israel — a reminder of guilt every time Israel turned to them for help. Then they will know that I am the Lord GOD.

[17] In the twenty-seventh year, the first month, on the first day, the word of the LORD came to me:

[18] Son of man, Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, made his army labor hard against Tyre. Every head was rubbed bald, every shoulder rubbed raw. Yet neither he nor his army got any wages from Tyre for the work they did against it.

[19] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Look — I am handing the land of Egypt over to Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon. He will haul off her wealth, plunder her plunder, loot her loot. That will be the wages for his army.

[20] As payment for his labor, I have given him the land of Egypt — because they worked for me, declares the Lord GOD.

[21] On that day I will make a horn sprout for the house of Israel, and I will open your mouth among them. Then they will know that I am the LORD.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me again:

[2] Son of man, prophesy. Say this: The Lord GOD speaks—

Wail. Wail for the day.

[3] The day looms close.
The day of the LORD is close—
a day of cloud,
a day reserved for the nations.

[4] A sword is coming for Egypt.
Anguish will seize Cush
when the slain drop in Egypt,
when her wealth is hauled off,
when her foundations are torn out.

[5] Cush, Put, Lud, all of Arabia, Libya, and the children of the covenant land—they will fall with her by the sword.

[6] This is what the LORD says:

Egypt's props will collapse.
The pride of her power will crumble.
From Migdol to Syene
they will drop by the sword inside her—
declares the Lord GOD.

[7] She will lie desolate among desolate lands. Her cities will join the ruined cities.

[8] Then they will know I am the LORD—when I set Egypt on fire and every ally she leans on is shattered.

[9] On that day messengers will speed from my presence in ships to terrify Cush in her false security. Anguish will hit them on Egypt's day. It is coming. Watch.

[10] This is what the Lord GOD says:

I will end the noise of Egypt
by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.

[11] He and his army with him—
the most ruthless of the nations—
are being summoned to wreck the land.
They will draw their swords against Egypt
and fill the land with corpses.

[12] I will dry up the Nile.
I will sell the land to evil men.
I will lay the land waste and everything in it
by the hand of foreigners.

I, the LORD, have spoken.

[13] This is what the Lord GOD says:

I will smash the idols.
I will silence the worthless gods of Memphis.
No more princes will rise from the land of Egypt—
I will pour terror through Egypt.

[14] I will devastate Pathros,
set Zoan ablaze,
execute judgment on Thebes.

[15] I will pour my fury on Pelusium, Egypt's stronghold,
and cut off the roaring crowds of Thebes.

[16] I will set Egypt on fire.
Pelusium will writhe in agony.
Thebes will be split wide open.
Memphis—enemies in broad daylight.

[17] The young men of Heliopolis and Bubastis will fall by the sword. The cities themselves will march into captivity.

[18] At Tahpanhes the day will turn black when I shatter Egypt's yoke there and her arrogant strength comes to nothing. A cloud will cover her. Her daughters will go into exile.

[19] That is how I will judge Egypt. Then they will know I am the LORD.

[20] In the eleventh year, the first month, the seventh day, the word of the LORD came to me:

[21] Son of man, I have broken Pharaoh king of Egypt's arm. Look—it has not been bandaged for healing, not splinted, not wrapped, not made strong enough to grip a sword.

[22] So this is what the Lord GOD says: I am against Pharaoh king of Egypt. I will break both his arms—the strong one and the one already broken—and knock the sword right out of his hand.

[23] I will scatter Egypt through the nations. I will winnow them through the lands.

[24] But I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon. I will put my sword in his hand. And I will break Pharaoh's arms, and he will groan in front of him like a man bleeding out.

[25] I will hold up the arms of the king of Babylon, and Pharaoh's arms will drop limp. Then they will know I am the LORD—when I put my sword in the king of Babylon's hand and he stretches it out over the land of Egypt.

[26] I will scatter Egypt through the nations and winnow them through the lands. Then they will know I am the LORD.

[1] In the eleventh year, the third month, the first day, the word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, say to Pharaoh king of Egypt and to all his roaring hordes: Who do you think you resemble in your greatness?

[3] Picture Assyria — a cedar of Lebanon, beautiful branches, dense shade, towering height, its crown piercing the clouds.

[4] The waters fed it, the deep made it tall, rivers streaming around the place where it was planted, sending channels out to every other tree in the field.

[5] So it outgrew them all — branches multiplying, limbs stretching long, drinking from waters without end.

[6] Every bird of the sky nested in its branches. Every beast of the field gave birth beneath its boughs. Every great nation lived in its shadow.

[7] It was magnificent in its greatness, in the sweep of its branches — roots sunk deep into abundant water.

[8] No cedar in God's garden could match it. No cypress could rival its branches. No plane tree could compete with its limbs. Nothing in the garden of God matched its beauty.

[9] I made it beautiful with a wealth of branches. Every tree in Eden envied it — every tree in the garden of God.

[10] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Because it shot up tall, lifted its crown into the clouds, and its heart grew arrogant in its height —

[11] I handed it over to the mightiest of the nations, who dealt with it as its wickedness deserved. I drove it out.

[12] Foreigners — the most ruthless of the nations — cut it down and left it lying. Its branches collapsed onto the mountains and into every valley. Its limbs lay broken in every ravine of the land. The peoples who had sheltered in its shade walked away and left it.

[13] On its fallen trunk every bird of the sky perched. Among its limbs every beast of the field came to rest.

[14] All of this so that no tree by any waters will swell with its own height again, will lift its crown into the clouds, will let its well-watered greatness stand tall in its own arrogance. Because every one of them is appointed for death — for the underworld, down among mortals, with those who descend into the pit.

[15] This is what the Lord GOD says: On the day it went down to the grave, I made the deep mourn. I covered it. I held back its rivers, and the mighty waters were stopped. Because of it I clothed Lebanon in darkness, and every tree of the field withered in grief.

[16] At the crash of its falling I made the nations tremble — when I sent it down to the grave with those who descend into the pit. Then in the underworld every tree of Eden, the choicest and best of Lebanon, every well-watered one, was consoled.

[17] They went down with it to the grave, to those killed by the sword — those who had been its arm, who had lived in its shadow among the nations.

[18] So tell me — which of the trees of Eden matched you in glory and greatness? Yet you will be brought down with the trees of Eden to the underworld. You will lie among the uncircumcised, with those slaughtered by the sword. This is Pharaoh and all his roaring hordes. So says the Lord GOD.

[1] In the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, on the first of the month, the word of the LORD came to me:

[2] Son of man, take up a dirge over Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and tell him this: You fancied yourself a lion among nations, but you are a sea-monster thrashing in the rivers — churning the water with your feet, fouling every stream you touch.

[3] This is what the Lord GOD says: I will throw my net over you — a great host of peoples will haul you up in my dragnet.

[4] I will fling you onto the land, hurl you across the open field. Every bird of the sky will roost on you. I will gorge the wild beasts of the whole earth on your flesh.

[5] I will spread your meat across the mountains and fill the valleys with your carcass.

[6] I will drench the land with your blood — soak it into the mountains — and the ravines will run full of you.

[7] When I snuff you out, I will black out the sky and dim its stars. The sun — I will hide behind cloud. The moon will not give its light.

[8] Every shining thing in the heavens I will darken over you. I will lay darkness across your land. This is the word of the Lord GOD.

[9] I will trouble the hearts of many peoples when I parade your ruin among the nations — into lands you never knew.

[10] I will leave many peoples appalled at you. Their kings will shudder over you when I brandish my sword in their faces. They will tremble, every man for his own life, on the day you fall.

[11] For this is what the Lord GOD says: The sword of the king of Babylon is coming for you.

[12] By the swords of warriors I will drop your hordes — every one of them the terror of nations. They will smash the pride of Egypt, and all her hordes will be wiped out.

[13] I will erase her cattle from the great waters. No human foot will muddy them again, no hoof of beast will stir them.

[14] Then I will let their waters settle and let their rivers flow like oil, declares the Lord GOD.

[15] When I make Egypt a wasteland, when the land is stripped of everything that filled it, when I strike down everyone living in it — then they will know that I am the LORD.

[16] This is the dirge — they will chant it. The daughters of the nations will chant it. Over Egypt and all her hordes they will chant it, declares the Lord GOD.

[17] In the twelfth year, on the fifteenth of the month, the word of the LORD came to me:

[18] Son of man, wail over the hordes of Egypt. Send her down — her and the daughters of the mighty nations — to the underworld, with those who go down to the Pit.

[19] Whom do you surpass in beauty? Go down — lie with the uncircumcised.

[20] They will fall among the sword-slain. The sword is drawn. Drag her away, and all her hordes with her.

[21] From the heart of Sheol, the mighty chiefs will speak of him and his allies: They have come down. They lie still — the uncircumcised, the sword-slain.

[22] Assyria is there — her whole assembly. Their graves ring round her, all of them slain, fallen by the sword.

[23] Her graves are dug in the deepest pit, her assembly encircling her tomb — every one of them slain, cut down by the sword, those who once spread terror in the land of the living.

[24] Elam is there, all her hordes around her grave — all slain, fallen by the sword, gone down uncircumcised into the underworld, who once spread their terror in the land of the living. Now they carry their shame with those who descend to the Pit.

[25] A bed has been made for her among the slain, with all her hordes — graves all around. All of them uncircumcised, sword-slain, because their terror was loosed in the land of the living. They carry their shame with those who go down to the Pit. They are laid out among the slain.

[26] Meshech and Tubal are there — all their hordes, graves all around them. All uncircumcised, run through by the sword, because they spread their terror in the land of the living.

[27] They do not lie with the fallen heroes of old, who went down to Sheol in full armor — swords laid under their heads, iniquities resting on their bones — because those warriors were the terror of mighty men in the land of the living.

[28] But you — you will be broken among the uncircumcised. You will lie with the sword-slain.

[29] Edom is there — her kings and all her princes. For all their strength, they are laid with the sword-slain. They lie with the uncircumcised, with those who go down to the Pit.

[30] All the princes of the north are there — every one of them — and all the Sidonians. They went down with the slain. For all the terror of their might, they were put to shame. They lie uncircumcised with the sword-slain. They carry their disgrace with those who descend to the Pit.

[31] Pharaoh will see them — and he will be consoled over all his hordes. Pharaoh and all his army, sword-slain, declares the Lord GOD.

[32] For I let his terror loose in the land of the living — and now he will be laid out among the uncircumcised, among the sword-slain — Pharaoh and all his hordes, declares the Lord GOD.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] Son of man, speak to your people. Tell them this: When I bring a sword against a land, the people of that land pick one of their own and make him watchman.

[3] He sees the sword coming. He blows the horn. He warns them.

[4] If someone hears the horn and ignores it, and the sword comes and takes him out — his blood is on his own head.

[5] He heard the horn. He didn't act. His blood is on him. If he had listened, he would have saved his life.

[6] But if the watchman sees the sword coming and doesn't blow the horn, and the people aren't warned, and the sword comes and takes one of them — that man dies in his sin, but I will hold the watchman accountable for his blood.

[7] You, son of man — I have made you watchman over the house of Israel. You hear a word from my mouth, you warn them from me.

[8] When I tell the wicked, "Wicked man, you will die" — and you don't speak up to turn him from his road — that wicked man will die in his sin, but his blood is on your hands.

[9] But if you warn him to turn from his road, and he refuses — he dies in his sin, and you have saved your own soul.

[10] Now, son of man, say to the house of Israel: You're saying, "Our crimes and our sins are piled on top of us, we're rotting under their weight — how can we live?"

[11] Tell them: As I live — declares the Sovereign LORD — I take no pleasure in the death of the wicked. I want him to turn from his road and live. Turn back! Turn back from your evil roads! Why would you die, house of Israel?

[12] Son of man, say to your people: The righteousness of the righteous won't save him on the day he rebels. The wickedness of the wicked won't ruin him on the day he turns from it. And the righteous? He cannot lean on his old righteousness on the day he sins.

[13] When I tell the righteous he will surely live, and he trusts in his own righteousness and then commits evil — none of his righteous deeds will be remembered. He'll die for the evil he did.

[14] When I tell the wicked, "You will surely die," and he turns from his sin and does what is just and right —

[15] gives back the pledge, returns what he stole, walks in the statutes of life, refuses evil — he will surely live. He will not die.

[16] None of the sins he committed will be brought against him. He has done what is just and right. He will live.

[17] And still your people say, "The Lord's way isn't fair." It's their way that isn't fair.

[18] When a righteous man turns from his righteousness and commits evil, he dies for it.

[19] When a wicked man turns from his wickedness and does what is just and right, he lives by it.

[20] And you say, "The Lord's way isn't fair." I will judge each of you, house of Israel, by his own road.

[21] In the twelfth year of our exile, the tenth month, the fifth day — a refugee from Jerusalem came to me and said: "The city has fallen."

[22] The hand of the LORD had been on me the evening before the refugee arrived. He opened my mouth before the man came to me at dawn. My mouth was opened. I was no longer mute.

[23] Then the word of the LORD came to me:

[24] Son of man, the people living in those ruins in the land of Israel are saying, "Abraham was just one man, and he inherited the land. We are many — the land is surely given to us as a possession."

[25] So tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says — You eat meat with the blood still in it. You lift your eyes to your filthy gods. You shed blood. And you think you'll possess the land?

[26] You stand on your swords. You commit abominations. Every man defiles his neighbor's wife. And you think you'll possess the land?

[27] Tell them this: As I live — declares the Sovereign LORD — those in the ruins will fall by the sword. Those in the open country I will give to the wild animals to devour. Those in fortresses and caves will die of plague.

[28] I will make the land a wasteland, a desolation. The pride of her strength will end. The mountains of Israel will be deserted — no one passing through.

[29] Then they will know I am the LORD — when I make the land a wasteland, a desolation, because of all the abominations they committed.

[30] As for you, son of man — your people are talking about you, beside walls and in doorways, one to another, each to his brother: "Come on, let's go hear what word has come from the LORD."

[31] They come to you like a crowd. They sit in front of you as my people. They hear your words — but they don't do them. With their mouths they make love to you, but their hearts chase after dishonest gain.

[32] Look — to them you're nothing more than a love song, a beautiful voice and a skilled musician. They hear your words. They don't do them.

[33] But when it comes — and it is coming — then they will know a prophet has been in their midst.

[1] The word of the LORD hit me hard:

[2] "Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel. Prophesy. Tell them — this is what the Lord GOD says: Doom to the shepherds of Israel who only feed themselves. Shouldn't shepherds feed the flock?

[3] You eat the fat. You wear the wool. You butcher the prime animals. But the flock — you don't feed.

[4] The weak, you haven't strengthened. The sick, you haven't healed. The wounded, you haven't bandaged. The strays, you haven't brought back. The lost, you haven't searched for. You've ruled them with brute force and cruelty.

[5] So they scattered — no shepherd anywhere — and became food for every wild thing that prowls. Scattered.

[6] My sheep wandered across every mountain, every high hill. They were dispersed over the whole face of the earth, and no one looked for them. No one searched.

[7] So listen, shepherds. Listen to the word of the LORD:

[8] As I live — declares the Lord GOD — because my flock has been plundered, because my flock has become prey for every wild beast since there was no shepherd, because my shepherds didn't search for my flock but fed themselves instead and let the flock starve —

[9] therefore, shepherds, hear the word of the LORD.

[10] This is what the Lord GOD says: I am against the shepherds. I will demand my flock from their hand. I am ending their shepherding career. They will not feed themselves anymore. I will pull my sheep out of their mouths — they will not be food for them any longer.

[11] Because this is what the Lord GOD says: Look — I myself will search for my sheep. I will find them.

[12] As a shepherd looks for his scattered flock on the day he's among them, that's how I will look for my sheep. I will rescue them from every place they were scattered on a day of clouds and thick darkness.

[13] I will bring them out from the peoples, gather them from the countries, lead them home to their own soil. I will graze them on the mountains of Israel, by the streams, in every inhabited place of the land.

[14] I will feed them in good pasture. The high mountains of Israel will be their grazing ground. There they'll lie down in fine pasture, feeding on rich grass on the mountains of Israel.

[15] I will shepherd my flock myself. I will make them lie down. This is the word of the Lord GOD.

[16] I will search for the lost. I will bring back the strays. I will bandage the broken. I will strengthen the sick. But the sleek and the strong — I will destroy. I will shepherd with justice.

[17] As for you, my flock — this is what the Lord GOD says: I am about to judge between one sheep and another, between rams and goats.

[18] Isn't it enough that you feed in good pasture? Do you have to trample the rest with your feet? You drink clear water — do you have to muddy what's left with your hooves?

[19] So my flock has to feed on what your feet have trampled and drink what your feet have fouled?

[20] Therefore the Lord GOD says to them: Look — I myself will judge between the fat sheep and the lean.

[21] Because you shoulder and shove and ram the weak ones with your horns until you've scattered them everywhere —

[22] I will rescue my flock. They will not be prey anymore. I will judge between one sheep and another.

[23] I will raise up one shepherd over them — my servant David. He will feed them. He will be their shepherd.

[24] I, the LORD, will be their God, and my servant David will be prince among them. I, the LORD, have spoken.

[25] I will cut a covenant of peace with them. I will clear the wild beasts out of the land, so they can sleep safely in the wilderness and in the forests.

[26] I will make them and the places around my hill a blessing. I will send the rains in their season — showers of blessing falling.

[27] The trees of the field will yield their fruit, the land will yield its harvest, and they will live secure on their own soil. They will know I am the LORD when I shatter the bars of their yoke and rescue them from the hand of those who enslaved them.

[28] They will not be plunder for the nations anymore. Wild animals will not devour them. They will live in safety, and no one will make them afraid.

[29] I will raise up for them a garden of renown. No more will they be swept away by famine in the land. No more will they bear the contempt of the nations.

[30] They will know that I, the LORD their God, am with them, and that they — the house of Israel — are my people. This is the word of the Lord GOD.

[31] You are my flock, the flock of my pasture. You are human. I am your God. This is the word of the Lord GOD."

[1] The word of the LORD hit me again:

[2] Son of man, turn your face toward Mount Seir. Prophesy against it.

[3] Tell it: This is what the Lord GOD says — Listen, Seir, I am against you. I am stretching out my hand against you. I will make you a wasteland, a horror.

[4] Your cities — rubble. You — desolation. Then you will know I am the LORD.

[5] Because you nursed an ancient hatred. Because you handed Israel over to the sword at the moment of their collapse, in the hour their punishment reached its peak —

[6] therefore, as I live — declares the Lord GOD — I will hand you over to blood, and blood will hunt you. You did not hate bloodshed, so bloodshed will not let you go.

[7] I will turn Mount Seir into a horror, a wasteland, and I will cut off everyone who comes and goes from it.

[8] I will fill its mountains with the slain. Your hills, your valleys, your ravines — they will be choked with bodies cut down by the sword.

[9] I will leave you desolate forever. Your cities will never be inhabited again. Then you will know I am the LORD.

[10] Because you said, "These two nations, these two lands, are mine. We will take possession of them" — though the LORD was there —

[11] therefore, as I live — declares the Lord GOD — I will deal with you according to the fury and jealousy you unleashed in your hatred against them. And I will make myself known among them when I judge you.

[12] Then you will know that I, the LORD, heard every contemptuous word you spoke against the mountains of Israel. You said, "They are wasted. They have been handed over to us to devour."

[13] You boasted against me with your mouth. You piled up your words against me. I heard every one.

[14] This is what the Lord GOD says: While the whole earth celebrates, I will make you a desolation.

[15] You rejoiced when Israel's inheritance lay in ruins — so I will do the same to you. You will be a wasteland, Mount Seir, and all of Edom, every last bit of it. Then they will know I am the LORD.

[1] Now you, son of man, prophesy to the mountains of Israel. Say: Mountains of Israel, hear what the Lord God says.

[2] This is what the Lord God says: Because the enemy gloated over you—"Yes! Those ancient heights belong to us now!"—

[3] therefore prophesy. Say: This is what the Lord God says: Because they leveled you and devoured you from every side until you became the leftovers of the nations, the gossip on every tongue, the slander on every lip—

[4] therefore, mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God. This is what the Lord God says to the mountains and the hills, the ravines and the valleys, the desolate wastelands and the abandoned cities that became plunder and a punchline for the nations all around—

[5] this is what the Lord God says: In the fire of my jealousy I have spoken against the rest of the nations, and against Edom most of all, for they claimed my land as their own with hearts full of glee and contempt, hungry to strip it bare.

[6] So prophesy concerning the land of Israel. Say to the mountains and hills, the ravines and valleys: This is what the Lord God says: I am speaking now in jealousy and fury, because you have borne the insults of the nations.

[7] Therefore this is what the Lord God says: I have raised my hand and sworn it—the nations who surround you will eat their own shame.

[8] But you, mountains of Israel, will push out your branches and bear your fruit for my people Israel, because they are coming home soon.

[9] Look—I am for you. I am turning toward you. You will be plowed and planted.

[10] I will multiply people on you—the whole house of Israel, all of it. The cities will fill up again. The ruins will rise.

[11] I will multiply on you both human and animal. They will be fruitful, they will increase. I will settle you as you were in your beginning, and I will make you flourish beyond anything you knew before. Then you will know that I am the Lord.

[12] I will walk people across you again—my people Israel. They will possess you. You will be their inheritance. You will never again rob them of their children.

[13] This is what the Lord God says: Because they say to you, "You devour people, you steal the children of your own nation"—

[14] therefore you will devour people no more, and you will never again rob your nation of its children, declares the Lord God.

[15] I will not let you hear the mockery of the nations anymore. You will not carry the scorn of the peoples any longer. You will not cause your nation to stumble again, declares the Lord God.

[16] Then the word of the Lord came to me:

[17] Son of man, when the house of Israel lived on their own soil, they defiled it by the way they lived and what they did. Their conduct in my sight was like a woman's menstrual impurity.

[18] So I poured out my fury on them—for the blood they spilled on the land, for the idols with which they polluted it.

[19] I scattered them among the nations. I dispersed them through the lands. I judged them by their conduct and their deeds.

[20] But when they arrived among the nations, wherever they went, they profaned my holy name. People said about them, "These are the Lord's people? And they had to leave his land?"

[21] So I had concern for my holy name, which the house of Israel had profaned among the nations wherever they went.

[22] Therefore say to the house of Israel: This is what the Lord God says: I am not doing this for your sake, house of Israel. I am doing it for the sake of my holy name, which you profaned among the nations wherever you went.

[23] I will sanctify my great name—profaned among the nations, profaned by you in their midst. The nations will know that I am the Lord, declares the Lord God, when I prove myself holy through you before their eyes.

[24] I will take you from the nations. I will gather you from every land. I will bring you home to your own soil.

[25] I will sprinkle clean water over you, and you will be clean. I will wash you of every filth, every idol.

[26] I will give you a new heart. I will put a new spirit inside you. I will rip the heart of stone out of your chest and give you a heart of flesh.

[27] I will put my Spirit inside you and cause you to walk in my statutes—you will keep my laws and live them out.

[28] You will live in the land I gave your ancestors. You will be my people, and I will be your God.

[29] I will save you from all your uncleanness. I will summon the grain and multiply it. I will not lay famine on you again.

[30] I will multiply the fruit of the tree, the harvest of the field, so you will never again bear the disgrace of famine in front of the nations.

[31] Then you will remember your evil ways and the things you did that were not good, and you will loathe yourselves for your sins, for your detestable acts.

[32] Understand this—I am not doing this for your sake, declares the Lord God. Let it be known to you. Be ashamed. Be humiliated by your ways, house of Israel.

[33] This is what the Lord God says: On the day I cleanse you from all your sins, I will resettle the cities. The ruins will be rebuilt.

[34] The wasteland will be plowed, instead of lying desolate in the sight of everyone who passes by.

[35] And people will say, "This land that was wasted has become like the garden of Eden. The cities once ruined and abandoned and torn down stand fortified and full again."

[36] Then the nations left around you will know that I, the Lord, have rebuilt what was torn down, replanted what was wasted. I, the Lord, have spoken. I will do it.

[37] This is what the Lord God says: Once more I will let the house of Israel ask me for this, and I will do it for them: I will multiply their people like a flock—

[38] like the consecrated flock, like the flock of Jerusalem at her festivals. So the ruined cities will be packed with flocks of people. Then they will know that I am the Lord.

[1] The hand of the LORD came down on me. His Spirit carried me out and set me down in the middle of a valley—and it was full of bones.

[2] He walked me through them, around and around. There were so many, scattered across the valley floor. And they were bone dry.

[3] He said to me, "Son of man—can these bones live?"

I said, "Sovereign LORD, you alone know."

[4] Then he said, "Prophesy over these bones. Tell them: Dry bones, hear the word of the LORD.

[5] This is what the Sovereign LORD says to these bones: I am going to breathe into you. I am going to make you live.

[6] I will lay sinew on you, grow flesh over you, stretch skin across you, and put breath in you. You will live. And you will know that I am the LORD."

[7] So I prophesied, just as I was commanded. And while I was still speaking—a sound. A rattling. A rumble across the valley floor. The bones started moving, sliding toward each other, bone locking into bone.

[8] I watched as sinew threaded across them, muscle thickened, skin sealed them shut. But they were not breathing. There was no breath in them.

[9] Then he said to me, "Prophesy to the breath. Prophesy, son of man, and say to the breath: This is what the Sovereign LORD says—Come, breath. Come from the four winds. Breathe into these slain. Make them live."

[10] So I prophesied as he commanded me. And breath rushed into them. They lived. They stood up on their feet—an army. A vast army.

[11] Then he said to me: "Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel. Listen to what they say: 'Our bones are dried up. Our hope is gone. We are cut off—finished.'

[12] So prophesy. Tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says—I am opening your graves. I am bringing you up out of them, my people. I am leading you home to the soil of Israel.

[13] And when I split your graves open and haul you up out of them, my people—then you will know that I am the LORD.

[14] I will put my Spirit in you and you will live. I will plant you in your own land. Then you will know that I, the LORD, have spoken—and I have done it. This is the declaration of the LORD."

[15] The word of the LORD came to me:

[16] "Son of man, take a stick. Write on it: For Judah and the Israelites bound to him. Then take another stick and write on it: For Joseph—Ephraim's stick—and the whole house of Israel bound to him.

[17] Bring them together in your hand. Make them one stick. One in your grip.

[18] And when your people ask you, 'Aren't you going to tell us what you mean by this?'—

[19] tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says—I am taking Joseph's stick, the one in Ephraim's hand, along with the tribes of Israel bound to him, and I am joining it to Judah's stick. I am making them one stick. One in my hand.

[20] Hold those sticks up where they can see them.

[21] Then tell them: This is what the Sovereign LORD says—I am pulling the Israelites out of the nations where they've scattered. I am gathering them from every direction. I am bringing them home to their own soil.

[22] I will make them one nation in the land, on the mountains of Israel. One king will rule over all of them. Never again two nations. Never again two kingdoms.

[23] Never again will they defile themselves with their idols, their disgusting things, their endless rebellions. I will pull them out of every place where they sinned and I will wash them clean. They will be my people. I will be their God.

[24] My servant David will be king over them. One shepherd for all of them. They will walk in my laws. They will guard my statutes and live them out.

[25] They will live in the land I gave to my servant Jacob, the land your ancestors lived in. They will live there—they, their children, and their children's children—forever. And David my servant will be their prince forever.

[26] I will cut a covenant of peace with them. An everlasting covenant. I will settle them, multiply them, and place my sanctuary in the middle of them, forever.

[27] My dwelling will be with them. I will be their God. They will be my people.

[28] Then the nations will know that I am the LORD who sets Israel apart—when my sanctuary stands in the middle of them, forever."

[1] The word of the LORD hit me:

[2] Son of man, set your face toward Gog of the land of Magog—the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal—and prophesy against him.

[3] Tell him: This is what the Lord GOD says: I am coming for you, Gog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal.

[4] I will turn you around. I will put hooks in your jaws and drag you out—you and your whole army, horses and horsemen in full armor, a vast horde armed to the teeth with shields and swords—

[5] Persia, Cush, and Put with them, every soldier bristling with weapons,

[6] Gomer and all its troops, Beth-togarmah from the far north with all its troops—nations beyond counting marching with you.

[7] Get ready. Be ready, you and the whole crowd assembled around you. You are their commander.

[8] After a long time you will be called to arms. In the latter years you will march on a land that has come back from the sword, gathered out of many nations onto the mountains of Israel—mountains long deserted. Brought home from the peoples, they all live there now, secure.

[9] You will rise like a storm front. You will roll in like a cloud blotting out the land—you and all your troops, and all the peoples with you.

[10] This is what the Lord GOD says: On that day, things will start churning in your mind. You will hatch an evil plan.

[11] You will say: I will march against a land of unwalled villages. I will fall upon a quiet people living in safety—every one of them with no walls, no gates, no bars.

[12] To plunder. To loot. To turn your hand against ruins now resettled, against a people regathered from the nations, a people with livestock and goods, living at the center of the earth.

[13] Sheba and Dedan and the merchants of Tarshish and all their young lions will say to you: Have you come to plunder? Have you mustered your horde to loot—to carry off silver and gold, to seize livestock and goods, to haul away spoils on a massive scale?

[14] So prophesy, son of man, and tell Gog: This is what the Lord GOD says: On that day, when my people Israel live secure—will you not notice?

[15] You will come from your place in the far north, you and many peoples with you, all of them mounted on horses, a vast assembly, a mighty army.

[16] You will come up against my people Israel like a cloud covering the land. In the days to come I will bring you against my land, Gog—so that the nations may know me, when through you I show myself holy before their eyes.

[17] This is what the Lord GOD says: Are you the one I spoke of in former days through my servants the prophets of Israel, who prophesied in those days, year after year, that I would bring you against them?

[18] On the day Gog sets foot on the soil of Israel—declares the Lord GOD—my fury will erupt in my face.

[19] In my jealousy, in the fire of my wrath, I have spoken: On that day a great quake will rip through the land of Israel.

[20] The fish in the sea, the birds in the sky, the beasts of the field, every creeping thing on the ground, every human being on the face of the earth—all will tremble before me. Mountains will collapse. Cliffs will crumble. Every wall will fall to the ground.

[21] I will summon a sword against him on all my mountains, declares the Lord GOD. Every man's sword will turn on his brother.

[22] I will bring him to trial with plague and bloodshed. I will pour down on him and his troops and all the peoples with him torrential rain, hailstones, fire, and sulfur.

[23] I will show my greatness. I will show my holiness. I will make myself known before the eyes of many nations. Then they will know that I am the LORD.

[1] So you, son of man—prophesy against Gog. Tell him: This is what the Lord GOD says. Look at me, Gog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal—I am coming for you.

[2] I will spin you around and drag you forward. I will haul you up from the far reaches of the north and march you straight onto the mountains of Israel.

[3] There I will knock the bow from your left hand and send the arrows flying from your right.

[4] On those mountains you will fall—you and every battalion behind you, every nation marching at your side. I am handing you over to the birds of prey and the wild beasts. You will be carrion.

[5] You will drop in the open field. I have spoken. This is the declaration of the Lord GOD.

[6] I will hurl fire on Magog and on those who lounge in safety along the coastlands—and they will know I am the LORD.

[7] I will plant my holy name in the middle of my people Israel. Never again will I let that name be dragged through the dirt. And the nations will know—the LORD, the Holy One in Israel.

[8] It is coming. It will happen. The declaration of the Lord GOD. This is the day I spoke of.

[9] Then the people in the towns of Israel will go out and build bonfires with the weapons—shields and bucklers, bows and arrows, war clubs and spears. Seven years of fuel.

[10] They will not bother gathering wood from the fields or chopping it from the forests. The weapons will burn. They will plunder those who plundered them, strip those who stripped them. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[11] On that day I will give Gog a burial plot in Israel—the Valley of the Travelers, east of the sea. It will block the road. There they will bury Gog and all his hordes. They will call it the Valley of Hamon-Gog—the Valley of Gog's Mob.

[12] For seven months the house of Israel will be burying them, scrubbing the land clean.

[13] Every last person in the land will join the burial detail, and the day I display my glory will be a day they never forget. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[14] They will set apart men full-time—men who patrol the land, who bury the bodies the others missed, the ones still lying on the ground. After seven months they will still be searching.

[15] As the search teams sweep the land, anyone who spots a human bone will set up a marker beside it—then the burial crews come and carry it off to the Valley of Hamon-Gog.

[16] (There will even be a city there called Hamonah—the Mob-town.) That is how they will purge the land.

[17] And you, son of man—this is what the Lord GOD says. Call out to every kind of bird and every wild beast: Gather. Come in from every direction. Come to my sacrifice—the great sacrifice I am preparing for you on the mountains of Israel. You will gorge on flesh and drink blood.

[18] You will eat the flesh of warriors and drink the blood of princes of the earth—all of them like rams and lambs and goats and bulls, fattened beasts of Bashan.

[19] You will eat fat until you are sick of it and drink blood until you are drunk on it—at the sacrifice I have slaughtered for you.

[20] At my table you will glut yourselves on horses and riders, on warriors and every kind of fighting man. The declaration of the Lord GOD.

[21] I will put my glory on display among the nations. Every nation will see the judgment I bring down, the hand I lay on them.

[22] And from that day forward, the house of Israel will know—I am the LORD their God.

[23] The nations too will finally understand: Israel went into exile because of her own guilt. Because they betrayed me, I hid my face from them. I handed them over to their enemies, and they fell by the sword, every one of them.

[24] I dealt with them according to their filth and their rebellion, and I hid my face from them.

[25] So this is what the Lord GOD says: Now I bring Jacob home from captivity. I will have mercy on the whole house of Israel. I will burn with jealousy for my holy name.

[10] Fishermen will stand along its banks. From En-gedi to En-eglaim—nets spread to dry. Fish of every kind, like the fish of the Great Sea, beyond counting.

[11] But the marshes and swamps will not be healed. They are left for salt.

[12] And on both banks of the river, every kind of fruit tree will grow. Their leaves will not wither. Their fruit will not fail. Every month they bear, because the water flowing past them comes from the sanctuary. Their fruit will feed. Their leaves will heal."

[13] This is what the Lord GOD says: These are the borders by which you will divide the land as inheritance among the twelve tribes of Israel—Joseph gets two portions.

[14] You will inherit it equally, each tribe with the next. I swore with raised hand to give this land to your ancestors, and now it falls to you as inheritance.

[15] This is the boundary of the land: On the north side, from the Great Sea by way of Hethlon to Lebo-hamath, on to Zedad,

[16] Berothah, Sibraim (between the borders of Damascus and Hamath), and Hazer-hatticon, which lies on the border of Hauran.

[17] So the boundary runs from the sea to Hazar-enan, with Damascus to the north and Hamath beyond. That is the north side.

[18] On the east: between Hauran and Damascus, with the Jordan running between Gilead and the land of Israel, down to the eastern sea, as far as Tamar. That is the east side.

[19] On the south: from Tamar to the waters of Meribath-kadesh, then along the Brook of Egypt to the Great Sea. That is the south side.

[20] On the west: the Great Sea itself, the boundary running up to a point opposite Lebo-hamath. That is the west side.

[21] You will divide this land among yourselves, tribe by tribe.

[22] Cast lots to apportion it—as inheritance for yourselves and for the foreigners living among you who have had children among you. Treat them as native-born Israelites. They share the inheritance with you, tribe by tribe.

[23] Whatever tribe a foreigner has settled in—there give him his inheritance. So says the Lord GOD.

[1] Now here are the tribal names. Starting at the northern frontier — running along the Hethlon road to Lebo-hamath, on to Hazar-enan, with Damascus on the north flanked by Hamath — that stretch from the eastern border to the sea belongs to Dan. One portion.

[2] South of Dan, from east to west: Asher. One portion.

[3] South of Asher, east to west: Naphtali. One portion.

[4] South of Naphtali, east to west: Manasseh. One portion.

[5] South of Manasseh, east to west: Ephraim. One portion.

[6] South of Ephraim, east to west: Reuben. One portion.

[7] South of Reuben, east to west: Judah. One portion.

[8] Then comes the sacred reserve — the territory you will set apart. Twenty-five thousand cubits wide, running east to west, the same length as any tribal portion. The sanctuary will sit at the center of it.

[9] The plot you lift up and dedicate to the LORD: twenty-five thousand by ten thousand cubits.

[10] This holy ground belongs to the priests. Twenty-five thousand on the north, ten thousand on the west, ten thousand on the east, twenty-five thousand on the south — and the LORD's sanctuary stands in the middle of it.

[11] This is for the consecrated priests — the sons of Zadok. They kept their post when Israel wandered off, unlike the Levites who drifted with them.

[12] So out of the sacred reserve, this portion is theirs — the holiest of the holy — bordering Levite land.

[13] The Levites get their own stretch alongside the priests: twenty-five thousand by ten thousand. The full dimension — twenty-five thousand long, ten thousand wide.

[14] None of it can be sold. None of it can be traded or transferred. This is firstfruits land. It belongs to the LORD. Untouchable.

[15] The remaining strip — five thousand cubits wide by twenty-five thousand long — is common ground. For the city: housing, open land. The city sits at the center.

[16] City dimensions: forty-five hundred cubits on the north, forty-five hundred on the south, forty-five hundred on the east, forty-five hundred on the west.

[17] Around the city, open space: two hundred fifty cubits to the north, two hundred fifty south, two hundred fifty east, two hundred fifty west.

[18] What remains, running parallel to the sacred reserve — ten thousand to the east, ten thousand to the west — its produce feeds the workers of the city.

[19] The city's workers, drawn from every tribe of Israel, will farm it.

[20] The whole reserve — twenty-five thousand by twenty-five thousand — a perfect square. You lift it up as holy, with the city's property folded in.

[21] Whatever's left on either side of the sacred reserve and the city property — that belongs to the prince. East of the twenty-five thousand reaching to the eastern border, west of the twenty-five thousand reaching to the sea. Tribal portions to the north and south, the sacred reserve and the sanctuary at the center.

[22] So the Levite holdings and the city's land sit inside the prince's territory, between Judah's border and Benjamin's. The prince has the rest.

[23] Now for the remaining tribes. From east to west: Benjamin. One portion.

[24] South of Benjamin, east to west: Simeon. One portion.

[25] South of Simeon, east to west: Issachar. One portion.

[26] South of Issachar, east to west: Zebulun. One portion.

[27] South of Zebulun, east to west: Gad. One portion.

[28] Gad's southern edge runs from Tamar to the waters of Meribath-kadesh, on to the Wadi of Egypt, out to the Great Sea.

[29] This is the land you will parcel out by lot as inheritance to the tribes of Israel. These are their portions. So says the Lord GOD.

[30] Now the city's exits. North side: forty-five hundred cubits long —

[31] three gates named for the tribes: gate of Reuben, gate of Judah, gate of Levi.

[32] East side, forty-five hundred cubits, three gates: Joseph, Benjamin, Dan.

[33] South side, forty-five hundred cubits, three gates: Simeon, Issachar, Zebulun.

[34] West side, forty-five hundred cubits, three gates: Gad, Asher, Naphtali.

[35] The perimeter: eighteen thousand cubits. And from that day forward, the city's name will be this — *The LORD Is There*.

[1] In the first year of Cyrus, king of Persia, the LORD lit a fire under him — to make good on what he had spoken through Jeremiah. Cyrus issued a decree across his entire empire, and he put it in writing:

[2] "Cyrus, king of Persia, declares: The LORD, the God of heaven, has handed me every kingdom on earth — and he has charged me with building him a house in Jerusalem, in Judah.

[3] Anyone among you who belongs to his people — may his God go with him. Let him go up to Jerusalem in Judah and rebuild the house of the LORD, the God of Israel. He is the God who is in Jerusalem.

[70] So the priests, the Levites, some of the people, the singers, the gatekeepers, and the temple servants settled in their towns — and all Israel went home.

[1] When the seventh month arrived and the people of Israel were settled in their towns, they gathered as one in Jerusalem.

[2] Then Jeshua son of Jozadak rose up with his fellow priests, and Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel rose up with his kinsmen, and together they rebuilt the altar of the God of Israel — to offer burnt offerings on it, exactly as written in the Law of Moses, the man of God.

[3] They set the altar back on its foundation, even though they were afraid of the peoples around them, and they began offering burnt offerings on it to the LORD — morning and evening, every day.

[4] They kept the Feast of Booths the way it was written, with the proper daily quota of burnt offerings, each day's portion on its day,

[5] and after that the regular burnt offerings, the new moon sacrifices, and the offerings for all the LORD's appointed sacred times, plus every freewill offering anyone brought to the LORD.

[6] From the first day of the seventh month they began offering burnt offerings to the LORD — though the foundation of the LORD's temple had not yet been laid.

[7] So they paid out silver to the masons and carpenters, and food, drink, and oil to the Sidonians and Tyrians, to ship cedar logs down from Lebanon to the coast at Joppa — by authorization from Cyrus, king of Persia.

[8] In the second month of the second year after they arrived at the house of God in Jerusalem, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jozadak — along with the rest of their brothers, the priests and Levites, and everyone who had come back from exile to Jerusalem — got the work underway. They appointed Levites twenty years old and up to oversee the work on the LORD's house.

[9] So Jeshua with his sons and brothers, Kadmiel with his sons (descendants of Judah), and the sons of Henadad with their sons and brothers — all the Levites — stood together as one to supervise the workmen at the house of God.

[10] When the builders laid the foundation of the LORD's temple, the priests took their stations in their vestments with trumpets, and the Levites — the sons of Asaph — with cymbals, to praise the LORD in the order David king of Israel had laid down.

[11] They sang back and forth, praising and thanking the LORD: *He is good. His mercy toward Israel never ends.* And all the people erupted in a thunderous shout of praise to the LORD because the foundation of the LORD's house was laid.

[12] But many of the priests, Levites, and family heads — the old men who had seen the first temple — wept out loud when they watched the foundation of this house go down before their eyes. Others shouted for joy at the top of their lungs.

[13] No one could tell the sound of joy from the sound of weeping — the people were roaring, and the noise carried for miles.

[1] Word reached the enemies of Judah and Benjamin that the returned exiles were rebuilding a temple for the LORD, the God of Israel.

[2] So they showed up at Zerubbabel's door, and at the doors of the family heads, with a proposal: "Let us build with you. We seek your God just like you do. We've been sacrificing to him ever since Esarhaddon king of Assyria brought us here."

[3] Zerubbabel, Jeshua, and the rest of Israel's family heads shut it down: "You have no part with us in building a house for our God. We'll build it ourselves — for the LORD, the God of Israel — exactly as King Cyrus of Persia commanded."

[4] So the locals went to work — wearing down the people of Judah, rattling them, sabotaging the construction.

[5] They bribed officials to obstruct the project, and they kept it up the entire reign of Cyrus, king of Persia, all the way to the reign of Darius.

[6] Then under Xerxes — at the very start of his reign — they drafted formal charges against the inhabitants of Judah and Jerusalem.

[7] And again in the days of Artaxerxes: Bishlam, Mithredath, Tabeel, and their associates sent a letter to Artaxerxes king of Persia. The text was written in Aramaic and translated.

[8] Then Rehum the commanding officer and Shimshai the scribe wrote their own letter to King Artaxerxes against Jerusalem. It went like this:

[9] From Rehum the commander, Shimshai the scribe, and the rest of their colleagues — the judges, the officials, the administrators, the Persians, the men of Erech, the Babylonians, the Susians (that is, the Elamites),

[10] and all the other peoples whom the great and noble Ashurbanipal deported and resettled in the city of Samaria and across the province Beyond the River. And now —

[11] (This is a copy of the letter they sent him.)
To King Artaxerxes,
From your servants, the men of the province Beyond the River:

[12] Let the king know: the Jews who came up from your territory have arrived in Jerusalem. They are rebuilding that rebellious, wicked city. They're finishing the walls and repairing the foundations.

[13] Let the king know what this means: if this city is rebuilt and its walls completed, they will stop paying tribute, taxes, and tolls — and the royal revenue will take the hit.

[14] Since we eat the king's salt, it would be wrong to stand by and watch the king dishonored. That's why we're sending this report —

[15] so that a search can be made in the royal archives. You'll find in those records that this city has a long history of rebellion, of damage to kings and provinces, of fomenting uprisings from way back. That's why it was destroyed.

[16] We are warning the king: if this city is rebuilt and its walls completed, you will have nothing left in the province Beyond the River.

[17] The king sent his reply:
To Rehum the commander, Shimshai the scribe, and the rest of their colleagues residing in Samaria and elsewhere in the province Beyond the River — greetings.

[18] The letter you sent has been read aloud before me, clearly translated.

[19] I issued an order. A search was made. It's confirmed: this city has a long record of revolt against kings. Sedition and rebellion have been bred there.

[20] Powerful kings have ruled in Jerusalem and held sway over the whole province Beyond the River. Tribute, taxes, and tolls all flowed to them.

[21] Issue an order, then: stop these men. That city is not to be rebuilt until I say otherwise.

[22] And don't be careless about this. Why let the danger grow and damage the crown?

[23] The moment Artaxerxes' letter was read before Rehum, Shimshai the scribe, and their colleagues, they rushed to Jerusalem — to the Jews — and forced them to stop. By strength of arms.

[24] So the work on the house of God in Jerusalem came to a halt. And it stayed halted until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

[1] Then the prophets started speaking — Haggai and Zechariah son of Iddo — prophesying to the Jews in Judah and Jerusalem in the name of the God of Israel who was over them.

[2] So Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jozadak got up and started rebuilding the house of God in Jerusalem. The prophets of God were right there with them, backing them up.

[3] Right away, Tattenai, the governor of the province west of the Euphrates, and Shethar-bozenai, along with their associates, showed up and confronted them: "Who authorized you to rebuild this temple and finish this structure?"

[4] They also demanded the names of every man working on the project.

[5] But the eye of their God was on the elders of the Jews. The work was not stopped — not until a report could reach Darius and a written response come back.

[6] Here is the copy of the letter that Tattenai, governor of the region west of the Euphrates, and Shethar-bozenai and his fellow officials sent to King Darius.

[7] The report they sent read like this:

To King Darius:
All peace.

[8] Let it be known to the king that we traveled to the province of Judah, to the house of the great God. It is being rebuilt with massive stones, and timber is being set into the walls. The work is moving fast and prospering under their hands.

[9] We questioned the elders. We asked them: "Who authorized you to rebuild this temple and finish this structure?"

[10] We also asked for their names so we could write down the leaders for your records.

[11] This is the answer they gave us:

"We are servants of the God of heaven and earth. We are rebuilding the temple that was built here many years ago — a great king of Israel built it and finished it.

[12] But our ancestors provoked the God of heaven to fury, so he handed them over to Nebuchadnezzar the Chaldean, king of Babylon. He destroyed this temple and deported the people to Babylon.

[13] But in the first year of King Cyrus of Babylon, Cyrus himself issued a decree to rebuild this house of God.

[14] What is more, the gold and silver vessels of God's house — the ones Nebuchadnezzar had taken from the temple in Jerusalem and carried off to the temple in Babylon — King Cyrus took out of the Babylonian temple and handed them to a man named Sheshbazzar, whom he appointed governor.

[15] Cyrus told him: 'Take these vessels. Go. Put them back in the temple in Jerusalem. Let the house of God be rebuilt on its original site.'

[16] So this Sheshbazzar came and laid the foundations of the house of God in Jerusalem. From that day until now it has been under construction — and it is still not finished."
