[5] Into your hands I place my life — you've already bought me back, Lord, God of truth.

[6] I have no use for people who chase empty gods. I trust the Lord. [7] I'll dance in your steady love, because you saw what was crushing me — you knew the agony inside me — [8] and you didn't hand me over to the enemy. You set my feet down in wide-open country.

[9] Be gracious to me, Lord — I'm hemmed in. My eyes burn through with grief, and so does my body, so does my soul. [10] My life is being spent on sorrow, my years on groaning. My strength buckles under what I've done wrong. My bones are giving way. [11] To every enemy I'm a joke — and worse to my neighbors, a horror to anyone who knows me. People on the street see me coming and bolt. [12] I've slipped out of memory like a corpse. I'm a broken pot. [13] I hear them whispering — terror on every side — plotting together, scheming how to take my life.

[14] But I — I trust you, Lord. I said it: you are my God. [15] My times are in your hand. Rescue me from the grip of my enemies, from those hunting me down. [16] Let your face light up over your servant. Save me by your steady love. [17] Don't let me be shamed, Lord — I called out to you. Let the wicked be shamed instead, silenced in the grave. [18] Shut the mouths that lie, the arrogant tongues spitting contempt at the righteous.

[19] How vast is the goodness you've stored up for those who fear you, poured out for those who shelter in you — and the whole world watching. [20] You hide them in the cover of your presence, far from human plots; you tuck them away in your shelter, safe from the war of tongues.

[21] Blessed be the Lord — his steady love astonished me, like a city under siege that suddenly holds. [22] I had panicked. I'd said, "I'm cut off from your sight." But you heard me crying for help when I called.

[23] Love the Lord, all you who belong to him. He keeps the faithful — and he pays back, in full, the proud. [24] Be strong. Let your heart take courage, all of you waiting on the Lord.

[1] Lucky is the one whose rebellion is lifted off, whose failure is buried out of sight. [2] Lucky is the one the LORD refuses to charge with guilt — the one whose spirit holds no deceit.

[3] When I kept silent, my bones wasted away — I groaned through the day, every day. [4] Day and night your hand pressed down on me; my strength dried up like ground in summer heat. *Selah*

[5] Then I owned it. I stopped hiding what I'd done. I said, "I'll lay my rebellion before the LORD" — and you lifted the weight of my guilt clean off. *Selah*

[6] So let every faithful one pray to you while you can still be found. When the floodwaters rise, they will not reach them. [7] You are my hiding place. You guard me from the squeeze. You ring me with songs of rescue. *Selah*

[8] I'll teach you. I'll show you the road to walk. I'll keep my eye on you and guide you. [9] Don't be like a horse or a mule — mindless, needing bit and bridle just to come near you.

[10] The wicked have pain stacked on pain. But the one who trusts the LORD — steadfast love surrounds them. [11] Celebrate in the LORD, you who are right with him. Sing out loud, all of you whose hearts run straight.

[1] Sing out, you who walk with him—praise looks good on the upright.
[2] Strike the harp in thanks. Make music on the ten-stringed lyre.
[3] Write him a new song. Play with skill, play with fire.

[4] Because his word rings true and everything he does is solid.
[5] He's in love with justice and fairness; the earth is soaked through with his loyal love.

[6] One word from him and the heavens existed. One breath, and the whole star-strewn host.
[7] He scooped the seas into a jar, locked the deeps in his vaults.

[8] Let the whole earth hold its breath before him. Let everyone alive stand in awe.
[9] Because he spoke—and it was. He commanded—and it stood there.

[10] He shreds the plans of nations. He cancels what the peoples scheme.
[11] But his own plan stands forever. What he intends moves generation to generation.

[12] Lucky is the nation whose God is the LORD—the people he chose as his own inheritance.

[13] From the heavens he looks down. He sees every human face.
[14] From his throne-room he watches everyone who lives on earth—
[15] he who shaped every heart, he who reads everything they do.

[16] No king is saved by the size of his army. No warrior survives by sheer muscle.
[17] Counting on a horse for rescue is a fool's bet—all that power can't save you.

[18] But look—the LORD's eye is on those who revere him, on those who wait for his loyal love
[19] to pull them back from death, to keep them alive when the famine bites down.

[20] So we wait for the LORD. He's our help. He's our shield.
[21] Our hearts come alive in him because we've trusted his holy name.
[22] Let your loyal love rest on us, LORD—the way we've rested our hope in you.

[1] I will bless the LORD every moment of my life. His praise will never leave my mouth.

[2] My soul makes its boast in him — let the broken hear it and come alive.

[3] Make him great with me. Together, let us lift his name high.

[4] I went looking for the LORD, and he answered. He pulled me out from under every fear I carried.

[5] Look to him and your face catches light — shame will never darken it again.

[6] This nobody cried out, and the LORD heard. He saved me from every tight corner.

[7] The angel of the LORD pitches camp around those who fear him, and he gets them out alive.

[8] Taste it for yourself — see how good the LORD is. Blessed is the one who runs to him for shelter.

[9] Fear the LORD, you who belong to him — those who fear him want for nothing.

[10] Young lions go hungry and prowl in vain, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.

[11] Come here, children. Listen to me. I will teach you what it means to fear the LORD.

[12] Who is it that loves life? Who wants days worth living, days full of good?

[13] Then keep your tongue from evil, your lips from telling lies.

[14] Turn away from wrong. Do good. Hunt for peace and chase it down.

[15] The LORD's eyes are on the righteous, his ears tuned to every cry they make.

[16] But his face is set against those who do evil — to wipe their memory off the earth.

[17] The righteous cry out, the LORD hears, and he hauls them out of every disaster.

[18] The LORD stays close to the brokenhearted. He rescues those whose spirit lies in pieces.

[19] The righteous endure trouble after trouble — and the LORD delivers them out of every one.

[20] He guards their bones — not one of them will be broken.

[21] Evil kills the wicked. Those who hate the righteous will pay for it.

[22] The LORD redeems the life of his servants. Take refuge in him, and condemnation cannot touch you.

[1] Fight my fighters, LORD. Make war on those who make war on me.

[2] Strap on shield and armor. Rise up. Come help me.

[3] Pull out spear and javelin to block my pursuers. Tell my soul: I am your rescue.

[4] Let those hunting my life be shamed and humiliated. Let those plotting my ruin stumble back in disgrace.

[5] Make them chaff in the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them off.

[6] Make their road dark and slick, with the angel of the LORD chasing them down.

[7] They hid their net for me — no reason. They dug a pit for my life — no reason.

[8] So let ruin ambush them when they least expect it. Let the net they hid catch their own feet. Let them fall into the pit they dug.

[9] Then my soul will dance in the LORD, will celebrate his rescue.

[10] Every bone in me will say: LORD, who is like you? You snatch the weak from those too strong for them, the poor and needy from those who would strip them bare.

[11] Witnesses show up swearing lies, grilling me about things I know nothing about.

[12] They pay back my good with evil. My soul sits bereaved.

[13] But when they were sick, I wore sackcloth. I starved my body with fasting. My prayers kept returning to my own chest, unanswered.

[14] I walked around grieving for them like a friend, like a brother. I bent low in mourning like someone weeping for his mother.

[15] But when I stumbled, they threw a party. They gathered against me — strangers I never knew — ripping me apart and never stopping.

[16] Like godless mockers at a feast, they ground their teeth at me.

[17] Lord, how long will you watch this? Pull my soul back from their wrecking, my one life from these lions.

[18] I will thank you in the great assembly. Among a roaring crowd I will praise you.

[19] Do not let my treacherous enemies gloat over me. Do not let those who hate me without cause exchange winks behind my back.

[20] They do not speak peace. They cook up lies against quiet people in the land.

[21] They open their mouths wide at me, sneering: Ha! Ha! We saw it with our own eyes!

[22] You saw it too, LORD. Do not stay silent. Lord, do not stay far from me.

[23] Wake up. Rise to my defense. My God, my Lord — take up my case.

[24] Vindicate me, LORD my God, by your justice. Do not let them gloat over me.

[25] Do not let them say in their hearts: Yes! Just what we wanted. Do not let them say: We swallowed him whole.

[26] Let those who celebrate my ruin be shamed and humiliated together. Let those who tower over me be wrapped in shame and disgrace.

[27] But those who want to see me cleared — let them shout for joy, let them celebrate. Let them say always: The LORD is great — he delights in the welfare of his servant.

[28] Then my tongue will speak your justice, will praise you all day long.

[1] Deep inside the wicked, an oracle speaks — sin whispers to their heart, and they listen. There is no fear of God before their eyes.

[2] They flatter themselves so thoroughly they can't even see their own guilt, can't bring themselves to hate it.

[3] Every word from their mouth is poison and deceit. They've abandoned wisdom. They've abandoned doing good.

[4] They lie awake at night plotting trouble. They plant their feet on a road that leads nowhere good — and they refuse to call evil what it is.

[5] But your love, Lord — it stretches to the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches the clouds.

[6] Your justice towers like the great mountains. Your judgments run deeper than the sea. You rescue everything that breathes — humans, animals, all of it.

[7] How precious is your love, O God! Every creature finds shelter in the shadow of your wings.

[8] They feast on the abundance of your house. You let them drink from the river of your delights.

[9] With you is the fountain of life. In your light we see light.

[10] Keep pouring your love on those who know you, your righteousness on the honest of heart.

[11] Don't let the foot of the arrogant trample me. Don't let the hand of the wicked drive me from my place.

[12] Look — there they fall, the evildoers. Flung down. Unable to rise.

[1] Don't let the success of the wicked eat at you. Don't burn with envy when crooks prosper.
[2] They're grass — green this morning, brown by afternoon, gone by sundown.

[3] Trust God. Do good. Settle into the land and feed on its faithfulness.
[4] Delight yourself in him, and he'll plant the deepest desires of your heart and bring them to harvest.

[5] Hand him your road. Trust him. He'll act.
[6] He'll bring your integrity blazing into the open like sunrise, your vindication like the high noon sun.

[7] Be still before God. Wait for him. Don't agitate yourself over the one whose schemes pay off, the one whose evil plans succeed.
[8] Stop the simmering rage. Walk away from fury. Don't let it pull you in — it only leads to more evil.

[9] The wicked get cut down. Those who wait on God inherit the earth.
[10] Give it a little while — the wicked won't be there. You'll look for them in their old place and find nothing.
[11] But the humble inherit the land. They settle into a peace so deep it overflows.

[12] The wicked plot against the righteous and grind their teeth in hatred,
[13] but the Lord laughs at them. He sees their day coming.

[14] They've drawn their swords, strung their bows, aiming at the poor and the helpless, hunting the honest down.
[15] Their swords will pierce their own hearts. Their bows will splinter in their hands.

[16] Better the little a righteous person has than the bloated wealth of the wicked.
[17] The arms of the wicked will be broken. The Lord himself props up the righteous.

[18] God knows every day of the blameless. Their inheritance is forever.
[19] When disaster hits, they won't be ruined. When famine comes, they'll eat their fill.

[20] But the wicked perish. The Lord's enemies are like the glory of pastures — beautiful, brief, vanishing into smoke.

[21] The wicked borrow and never repay. The righteous give freely, openhanded.
[22] Those God blesses inherit the land. Those he curses are cut off.

[23] A good person's steps are firmed up by the Lord — he loves the road they're walking.
[24] Even when they stumble, they don't fall flat — the Lord catches their hand.

[25] I was young once. Now I'm old. And I have never seen the righteous abandoned, never seen their children begging bread.
[26] All day long they're generous, lending freely — and their children become a blessing.

[27] Turn from evil. Do good. You'll live in the land forever.
[28] The Lord loves justice. He never abandons his faithful ones — they're guarded for all time. But the line of the wicked gets cut.

[29] The righteous inherit the land and live on it forever.
[30] Wisdom flows from the righteous mouth. Justice shapes every word.
[31] The law of their God is written on their heart. Their feet don't slide.

[32] The wicked stalk the righteous, hunting for a chance to kill.
[33] The Lord won't leave them in those hands, won't let them be condemned when dragged to trial.

[34] Wait for the Lord. Stay on his road. He'll lift you up to inherit the land. You'll witness the wicked being cut down.

[35] I saw a wicked man — ruthless, towering, spreading himself like a native cedar.
[36] I passed by again and he was gone. I searched. He couldn't be found.

[37] Watch the man of integrity. Study the upright. There's a future for the person of peace.
[38] But sinners are wiped out together. The future of the wicked is annihilation.

[39] The rescue of the righteous comes from the Lord — he is their fortress in the day everything caves in.
[40] The Lord stands by them, pulls them clear. He delivers them from the wicked and saves them, because they took shelter in him.

[1] LORD — don't tear into me in your fury. Don't discipline me in the heat of your wrath.

[2] Your arrows have buried themselves in me. Your hand has come down hard.

[3] There's no health left in my body — your anger has seen to that. No peace in my bones — my sin has seen to that.

[4] My guilt has risen over my head like floodwater, a weight too heavy to carry.

[5] My wounds fester. They stink. And it's my own foolishness that opened them.

[6] I'm bent double, brought low, dragging myself through the day in mourning clothes.

[7] My back is on fire. There's nothing sound left in me.

[8] I'm spent. Crushed. My heart roars with a groan that won't quit.

[9] Lord, every longing I have is laid out before you. My sighing isn't hidden from you.

[10] My heart pounds wildly. My strength has drained away. Even the light in my eyes has gone dark.

[11] Friends and companions hang back from my wounds. My own family stands at a distance.

[12] Those hunting my life are setting their snares. Those wanting to ruin me speak destruction — plotting deceit all day long.

[13] But I'm like a deaf man — I hear nothing. Like a mute — I open no mouth.

[14] I've become a man who cannot hear, whose lips frame no reply.

[15] Because it's you, LORD, I'm waiting for. You will answer, my Lord, my God.

[16] I said: don't let them gloat over me — those who strut when my foot slips.

[17] I'm on the verge of falling. The pain never leaves me.

[18] So I confess my guilt. I'm sick over my sin.

[19] Meanwhile my enemies thrive — full of life, multiplying. Those who hate me for no reason keep stacking up.

[20] They pay me back evil for good. They oppose me for chasing what's right.

[21] Don't abandon me, LORD. My God — don't stay far off.

[22] Come quickly to help me, Lord — my salvation.

[1] I told myself: I'll watch my steps. I won't sin with my tongue. I'll keep a muzzle on my mouth while the wicked are standing in front of me.

[2] So I went silent — total silence, not a word, not even about what was good. And my pain only sharpened.

[3] My heart burned inside me. The longer I brooded, the hotter the fire — until finally I had to speak:

[4] Lord, show me how this ends. Show me the count of my days. Let me see how fragile I really am.

[5] Look — you've made my life a few handbreadths long. My whole existence is nothing to you. Every human being, even standing tall, is only a breath. *Selah*

[6] We drift through life like shadows. We churn ourselves up over nothing. We pile up wealth and don't even know who'll end up with it.

[7] So now — what am I waiting for, Lord? My hope is in you. Only you.

[8] Pull me out of every rebellion I've fallen into. Don't let the fool make a mockery of me.

[9] I've gone quiet. I won't open my mouth — because you are the one who did this.

[10] Lift this blow off me. The strike of your hand is destroying me.

[11] When you discipline a person for their guilt, you consume what they love like a moth eating cloth. Every human is only a breath. *Selah*

[12] Hear my prayer, Lord. Listen to my cry. Don't be deaf to my tears. I'm a stranger here, passing through, like every ancestor before me.

[13] Look away from me. Let me catch my breath before I'm gone — before I'm nothing at all.

[1] I waited. I waited for the LORD, and he bent down to me and heard my cry.

[2] He hauled me up out of the roaring pit, out of the sucking mud, and set my feet on rock — gave me ground that would hold.

[3] He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God. Many will see it, and they will be struck with awe, and they will trust the LORD.

[4] Blessed is the one who anchors their trust in the LORD — who doesn't turn toward the arrogant, doesn't drift after lies.

[5] LORD my God, the wonders you've done, the plans you've laid for us — no one can line them up beside you. If I tried to count them, to tell them all, they would outrun me. There are too many.

[6] You didn't want sacrifice and offering — you opened my ears instead. Burnt offerings and sin offerings — you never asked for them.

[7] So I said: Here I come. The scroll is written about me.

[8] Doing what you want, my God — that's my delight. Your instruction lives inside me.

[9] I've announced your righteousness in the great assembly. I haven't shut my lips — LORD, you know it.

[10] I haven't buried your righteousness in my heart. I've spoken your faithfulness, your saving power. I haven't hidden your steady love and your truth from the great assembly.

[11] So you, LORD — don't withhold your mercy from me. Let your steady love and your truth guard me always.

[12] Troubles have closed in around me — more than I can count. My sins have caught me, and I can't see my way out. They outnumber the hairs on my head, and my heart has failed me.

[13] LORD — be willing. Rescue me. LORD — hurry. Help me.

[14] Let those hunting my life be humiliated and disgraced together. Let those who delight in my ruin be driven back in shame.

[15] Let them be appalled at their own disgrace — those who jeer over me, "Aha! Aha!"

[16] But let everyone who seeks you celebrate and rejoice in you. Let those who love your salvation say it without ceasing: The LORD is great.

[17] As for me — I'm poor and needy, but the Lord is thinking of me. You are my help, my rescuer. My God — don't wait.

[1] Blessed is the one who pays attention to the weak. When trouble comes, the LORD pulls him out.

[2] The LORD guards his life, keeps him alive, calls him blessed in the land — and refuses to hand him over to the appetite of his enemies.

[3] When sickness flattens him to his bed, the LORD is there, nursing him back. The LORD remakes the whole sickbed into something else entirely.

[4] So I said: LORD, show me grace. Heal me. I've sinned against you.

[5] My enemies whisper venom about me: "When will he finally die? When does his name disappear?"

[6] One comes to visit and his mouth is empty — his heart is busy stockpiling slander. He walks out the door and starts talking.

[7] Together they hiss against me, every one of them, plotting the worst:

[8] "Some deadly thing has fastened on him. He's down. He's never getting up."

[9] Even the friend I trusted — the one who ate at my table — has lifted his heel against me.

[10] But you, LORD — show me grace. Lift me up. Let me give them what they have coming.

[11] This is how I know you delight in me: my enemy doesn't get the last word over me.

[12] You hold me steady because I'm honest, and you set me in your presence — forever.

[13] Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from forever to forever. Amen and amen.

[1] Like a deer gasping for streams of water, my soul gasps for you, God.

[2] My soul thirsts for God — for the living God. When can I come and stand before his face?

[3] Tears have been my food day and night, while they ask me, relentless: "Where is this God of yours?"

[4] I remember — and it breaks me open — how I used to move with the crowd, leading them in procession to the house of God, voices ringing with joy and thanksgiving, a festival roar.

[5] Why are you collapsing, my soul? Why this churning inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[6] My soul caves in on itself. So I remember you from the land of Jordan, from the peaks of Hermon, from the small hill called Mizar.

[7] Deep calls to deep in the thunder of your waterfalls. Every wave, every breaker you send — they all crash over me.

[8] By day the Lord commands his steadfast love, and by night his song stays with me — a prayer to the God of my life.

[9] I say to God, my rock: "Why have you forgotten me? Why must I walk in mourning while the enemy crushes me?"

[10] Their taunts cut to the bone as they hammer me all day: "Where is this God of yours?"

[11] Why are you collapsing, my soul? Why this churning inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[1] Judge me, God. Take up my case against a nation with no loyalty. From the deceitful, the unjust man — rescue me.

[2] You are the God where I take shelter. Why have you cast me off? Why do I walk in mourning, crushed beneath my enemy?

[3] Send out your light. Send out your truth. Let them lead me — let them bring me to your holy mountain, to the place where you dwell.

[4] Then I will come to the altar of God, to God my joy, my exultation. With the harp I will praise you — God, my God.

[5] Why are you sinking, my soul? Why this storm inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[1] God, we heard it with our own ears — our fathers told us the story, what you accomplished in their day, back in the ancient times.

[2] With your own hand you tore nations out by the roots and planted our fathers in their place. You crushed peoples and made our fathers spread.

[3] They didn't take the land by their swords. Their arms won them nothing. It was your right hand, your arm, the light of your face — because you loved them.

[4] You are my King, O God. Command victory for Jacob.

[5] Through you we drive back our enemies. In your name we trample those who rise against us.

[6] I don't trust my bow. My sword won't save me.

[7] You — you are the one who saves us from our enemies. You are the one who shames those who hate us.

[8] In God we boast all day long. We will praise your name forever.

[9] But now — you've rejected us. You've humiliated us. You no longer march out with our armies.

[10] You make us retreat from the enemy. Those who hate us help themselves to our spoil.

[11] You hand us over like sheep to be eaten. You scatter us among the nations.

[12] You sell your people for nothing — you didn't even haggle for the price.

[13] You make us the laughingstock of our neighbors, the mockery and scorn of everyone around us.

[14] You make us a proverb among the nations — heads shaking at the sight of us.

[15] My disgrace stares me in the face all day. Shame covers me —

[16] from the voice of the taunter, the slanderer, the face of the enemy, the avenger.

[17] All this has come on us — and we have not forgotten you. We have not betrayed your covenant.

[18] Our hearts haven't turned back. Our feet haven't strayed from your path.

[19] Yet you crushed us in the place where jackals prowl. You covered us with the shadow of death.

[20] If we had forgotten the name of our God, if we had reached out our hands to some foreign god —

[21] wouldn't God have uncovered it? He knows the secrets of the heart.

[22] No — for your sake we are being killed all day long. We are counted as sheep marked for slaughter.

[23] Wake up! Why are you sleeping, Lord? Rouse yourself! Don't reject us forever.

[24] Why are you hiding your face? Why do you forget our affliction, our oppression?

[25] We are crushed into the dust. Our bodies cling to the ground.

[26] Rise up — help us! Redeem us, because of your steadfast love.

[1] My heart is stirring with a beautiful theme; I'm reciting my verses for the king. My tongue is the pen of a master scribe.

[3] Strap your sword to your thigh, warrior — clothe yourself in splendor and majesty.

[11] The king is captivated by your beauty. He is your lord now — bow to him.

[12] The daughter of Tyre will come with a gift. The wealthy of the people will seek your favor.

[13] The king's daughter is glorious within her chamber — her gown woven with threads of gold.

[14] In embroidered robes she is led to the king. Her companions, the virgins behind her, are brought to you.

[15] With joy and gladness they enter the palace of the king.

[16] In place of your fathers will be your sons — you will make them princes over all the earth.

[17] I will make your name remembered through every generation. So the peoples will praise you forever and ever.

[11] The LORD of armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our stronghold. *Selah*

[3] He drove nations down beneath us, pressed peoples under our feet.

[4] He picked our inheritance for us—the pride of Jacob, the one he loved.

[5] God has ascended with a shout, the LORD with the blast of the trumpet.

[1] Great is the LORD, worthy of every shout of praise, in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.

[2] Beautiful in its rise, the joy of the whole earth — Mount Zion, the high northern slopes, the city of the great King.

[3] God himself walks her ramparts. He is the fortress within the fortress.

[1] Listen up, every nation. Pay attention, everyone who lives on this earth—

[1] The Mighty One, God, the LORD, has spoken. He summons the earth from where the sun rises to where it sets. [2] From Zion — beauty made perfect — God blazes forth. [4] He calls the heavens above and the earth below to witness the trial of his people: [5] "Gather my faithful ones to me — those who sealed a covenant with me by sacrifice." [7] "Listen, my people, and I will speak. Israel, I am bringing charges against you. I am God — your God. [8] My quarrel isn't about your sacrifices. Your burnt offerings stand before me always. [9] I don't need a bull from your barn or goats from your pens. [10] Every animal in the forest already belongs to me, and the cattle on a thousand hills. [11] I know every bird in the mountains; every creature in the field is mine. [12] If I were hungry, I wouldn't tell you — the world is mine, and everything in it. [13] Do I eat the meat of bulls? Do I drink the blood of goats? [14] Offer God your thanksgiving. Make good on your vows to the Most High. [15] Call on me when trouble comes — I will rescue you, and you will give me glory."

[16] But to the wicked God says: "What right do you have to recite my statutes or take my covenant on your lips? [17] You hate correction. You toss my words behind you. [18] You see a thief and run with him. You throw in your lot with adulterers. [19] Your mouth spills evil. Your tongue weaves deceit. [20] You sit there slandering your own brother — smearing your mother's son. [21] You did all this while I held my peace, and you assumed I was just like you. But now I'm calling you out. I'm laying it bare before your eyes.

[1] God — have mercy on me. Out of your steady love. Out of the depth of your tenderness, wipe away what I've done. [2] Wash me clean of this guilt. Scrub me down to the bone of my wrongdoing.

[15] Don't let the current swallow me. Don't let the deep close over my head. Don't let the pit seal its mouth on me.

[16] Answer me, LORD— your love is good. Turn toward me— your mercy is vast.

[17] Don't hide your face from your servant. I'm in trouble. Answer me quickly.

[18] Come close. Redeem me. Buy me back from my enemies.

[19] You see how they shame me, how they disgrace and humiliate me. Every one of my enemies stands in front of you.

[20] Their insults have broken my heart. I'm sick from it. I looked for sympathy— there was none. I looked for someone to comfort me— I found no one.

[21] They put poison in my food. When I was thirsty, they handed me vinegar.

[22] Let their banquet table become a trap. Let their feasts become a snare.

[23] Let their eyes go dark so they can't see. Let their backs bend permanently under the weight.

[24] Pour out your fury on them. Let your burning anger overtake them.

[25] Leave their camp empty. Let no one live in their tents.

[26] Because they pile on the one you've already struck. They gossip about the pain of those you've wounded.

[27] Add this to the charges against them. Don't let them walk into your righteousness.

[28] Erase them from the book of the living. Don't let their names be written among the righteous.

[29] But I am in pain, I am broken— let your rescue, God, lift me up high.

[30] I will sing praise to God's name. I will magnify him with thanksgiving.

[31] And this will please the LORD more than any bull, more than any sacrifice with horns and hooves.

[32] The humble will see it and celebrate. You who seek God— let your hearts come alive.

[33] Because the LORD listens to the poor. He doesn't dismiss his prisoners.

[34] Let heaven and earth praise him. Let the seas and everything swimming in them join in.

[35] Because God will rescue Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah. His people will live there, will own it.

[36] His servants' descendants will inherit it. Those who love his name will make their home there.

[1] God — rescue me. Now. Lord — get here. Hurry.

[2] Let the ones hunting my life be humiliated, sent reeling. Let the ones who relish my ruin stagger backward in disgrace.

[3] Let those who jeer "Ha! Ha!" turn on their heels, choking on their own scorn.

[4] But everyone searching for you — let them break into joy, let them celebrate you. Let the ones who love your rescue keep saying it forever: "God is huge. God is glorious."

[5] As for me — I'm broken, I'm empty. God, come fast. You're my help, you're my way out. Lord — don't make me wait.

[1] In you, LORD, I've taken cover — don't ever let me end up disgraced.

[2] You're the righteous one — rescue me, pull me out. Lean in and listen. Save me.

[3] Be the rock I keep running to, the fortress always open to me. You gave the word — save me. You're my cliff-face, my stronghold.

[4] My God, snatch me out of the grip of the wicked, out of the fist of the cruel and the corrupt.

[5] You're what I've been waiting for, Lord — my hope since I was young, my confidence from the start.

[6] I've leaned on you since the womb. You drew me out of my mother's body. You're the reason I sing.

[7] To a lot of people I look like a walking disaster — but you've been my refuge, solid and unshaken.

[8] My mouth is full of your praise — your splendor, all day long.

[9] Don't toss me aside when I'm old. Don't walk away when my strength runs out.

[10] My enemies are talking about me. The ones plotting my death are huddled together, scheming.

[11] "God has dropped him," they say. "Go after him. Grab him. Nobody's coming to save him."

[12] God — don't stay far off. My God — hurry. Help me.

[13] Let the ones hunting me crash and burn. Let the ones who want me ruined be buried in their own shame and disgrace.

[14] But me — I'll keep hoping. I'll pile praise on top of praise.

[15] My mouth will tell the story of your righteousness, your saving acts, all day long — though the count is beyond me.

[16] I'll come in the strength of the Lord GOD. I'll tell of your righteousness — yours alone.

[17] God, you taught me from the time I was a boy, and to this day I keep announcing your wonders.

[18] Even now — gray-haired, old — don't abandon me, God. Let me tell the next generation about your arm, the coming generation about your power.

[19] Your righteousness, God, reaches the sky. You've done staggering things. Who is there like you?

[20] You've shown me trouble — mountains of it, deep and bitter — but you'll bring me back to life. You'll haul me up from the depths of the earth.

[21] You'll lift me higher. You'll come around again and comfort me.

[22] So I'll thank you with the harp — your faithfulness, my God. I'll make music to you with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.

[23] My lips will erupt in song when I make music to you — and my soul, which you bought back, will sing too.

[24] All day my tongue will repeat your righteousness — because the ones who wanted me destroyed have been shamed and humiliated.

[1] God, give the king your judgment. Give the king's son your justice. [2] Let him govern your people with righteousness, your afflicted ones with justice. [3] Let the mountains carry peace to the people, and the hills, righteousness. [4] Let him defend the cause of the poor, rescue the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor.

[5] Let them fear you as long as the sun endures, as long as the moon, through every generation. [6] Let him come down like rain on a mown field, like showers soaking the earth. [7] In his days let righteousness bloom and peace flood the land — until the moon is no more.

[8] Let his rule stretch from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth. [9] Let desert tribes bow before him, his enemies lick the dust. [10] Let the kings of Tarshish and the distant coasts bring tribute. Let the kings of Sheba and Seba haul in their gifts. [11] Let every king fall down before him. Let every nation serve him.

[12] Because he rescues the needy when they cry — the afflicted, the one with no one to help. [13] He bends low over the weak and the poor. He saves the lives of those teetering at the edge. [14] He buys their lives back from violence and crushing weight. Their blood is precious in his sight.

[15] Long live the king! Let gold from Sheba be poured at his feet. Let prayer rise for him without ceasing, blessing surround him all day long. [16] Let grain flourish across the land, even on the mountain peaks. Let the harvest wave like the cedars of Lebanon. Let the cities teem with life like grass springing from the soil. [17] Let his name endure forever, outlast the sun. Let every nation find blessing in him. Let all peoples call him blessed.

[18] Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel — the only one who works wonders. [19] Blessed be his glorious name forever. Let his glory flood the whole earth. Amen and amen.

[20] Here end the prayers of David, son of Jesse.

[1] Yes — God is good to Israel, good to those whose hearts are clean.

[2] But me? My feet nearly gave way. I came that close to slipping.

[3] Because I envied the arrogant. I watched the wicked thrive and something in me broke.

[4] They don't struggle. No agony in their dying — their bodies stay sleek and strong.

[5] They skip the troubles the rest of us drown in. They don't get hit like ordinary people.

[6] So pride drapes them like a necklace. Violence fits them like a tailored coat.

[7] Their eyes bulge from rich living. Their hearts overflow with whatever they imagine.

[8] They sneer. They talk cruelty. From their high perch, they threaten everyone below.

[9] Their mouths set themselves against heaven, and their tongues swagger through the earth.

[10] So the crowd turns to them — drinks down their words like water and asks for more.

[11] They say, "How would God know? Does the Most High even notice?"

[12] Look at them — the wicked, untroubled, piling up wealth.

[13] So what was the point of keeping my heart pure? Of washing my hands clean of guilt?

[14] All day long I've been struck down. Every morning a fresh punishment.

[15] If I had said out loud what I was thinking, I would have betrayed your children.

[16] When I tried to make sense of it, the weight of it crushed me —

[17] until I walked into God's sanctuary. Then I saw how their story ends.

[18] You set them on slick ground. You're tipping them toward ruin.

[19] In an instant — gone. Swept away by terrors, every one.

[20] Like a dream when you wake — Lord, when you rise, you'll dismiss them like phantoms.

[21] When my heart turned bitter, when I was pierced through —

[22] I was the brute. The idiot. An animal standing before you, uncomprehending.

[23] And still — I am always with you. You've taken hold of my right hand.

[24] You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will gather me into glory.

[25] Who do I have in heaven but you? And on earth — I want nothing apart from you.

[26] My body fails. My heart fails. But God is the rock of my heart, my portion forever.

[27] Look — those who drift from you perish. You finish off everyone who plays you false.

[28] But me? Being close to God is the only good I know. I've made the Lord my refuge, so I can tell what he's done.

[1] God, why? Why have You thrown us out for good? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your own pasture?

[2] Remember the people You bought long ago — the tribe You redeemed as Your inheritance, Mount Zion, where You chose to live.

[3] Walk through these endless ruins. The enemy has wrecked everything in Your sanctuary.

[4] Your foes roared in the middle of Your meeting place. They planted their banners there as trophies.

[5] They looked like men swinging axes in a thick forest —

[6] hacking down every carved panel with hatchet and hammer.

[7] They torched Your sanctuary. They dragged the dwelling place of Your name to the ground, defiled it.

[8] They said in their hearts, "Let's crush them completely." They burned down every place where God was worshiped in the land.

[9] No signs anymore. No prophets left. No one among us who knows how long this lasts.

[10] How long, God? Will the enemy mock You forever? Will they spit on Your name without end?

[11] Why do You hold back Your hand — Your right hand? Pull it from Your robe. Finish them.

[12] Yet God is my king from ancient days, working salvation right here on the earth.

[13] You split the sea with Your strength. You crushed the heads of the sea-monsters in the water.

[14] You smashed Leviathan's skulls and served him up as food to the desert creatures.

[15] You cracked open springs and streams. You dried up rivers that never run dry.

[16] The day is Yours. The night is Yours. You set the moon and sun in place.

[17] You drew every border of the earth. Summer and winter — You made them both.

[18] Remember this, LORD: the enemy mocks You. A pack of fools sneers at Your name.

[19] Don't hand over the life of Your dove to the wild beasts. Don't forget the lives of Your afflicted — not forever.

[20] Look at Your covenant. The dark corners of the land are crawling with violence.

[21] Don't let the crushed walk away in shame. Let the poor and needy praise Your name.

[22] Rise up, God. Plead Your own case. Remember how fools mock You all day long.

[23] Don't tune out the voice of Your enemies. The uproar of those who defy You climbs higher and higher.

[1] We thank you, God, we thank you. Your name is close — and the stories of your wonders spill from every mouth.

[2] "When I choose the moment," you say, "I will judge with a straight edge.

[3] The earth may shake and everyone on it stagger, but I am the one holding its pillars steady."

[4] To the arrogant I say: stop your strutting. To the wicked: drop the raised horn.

[5] Stop hoisting your power to the sky. Stop talking with that stiff, defiant neck.

[6] Promotion does not come from the east or the west, not from the wilderness either —

[7] God is the judge. He brings one down and lifts another up.

[8] There is a cup in the Lord's hand, foaming with dark wine, mixed and ready. He pours it out, and every wicked soul on earth will drain it down to the dregs.

[9] But I — I will not stop telling it. I will sing to the God of Jacob.

[10] "I will shatter every horn the wicked raise," he says, "and the horns of the righteous will rise high."

[1] God is known in Judah. His name looms large in Israel.

[2] His tent is pitched in Salem, his lair in Zion.

[3] There he snapped the flaming arrows, the shield, the sword, the machinery of war.

[4] You blaze with light — more majestic than the everlasting peaks.

[5] The brave-hearted were stripped bare. They sank into their final sleep. Every warrior's hand went limp.

[6] One word from you, God of Jacob — and rider and horse lay stunned, dead in their tracks.

[7] You — you alone — are terrifying. Who can stand before you when your anger ignites?

[8] From heaven you handed down the verdict. The earth went silent, terrified, still —

[9] when God rose to render judgment, to rescue every broken soul on earth.

[10] Even human rage ends up praising you; what fury survives, you wear like a belt.

[11] Make your vows to the LORD your God — and keep them. Let every nation around him bring tribute to the One who should be feared.

[12] He clips the breath of princes. Kings of the earth tremble at his name.

[1] I cry out to God — full voice, no holding back. I cry out, and I'm waiting for him to hear me.

[2] When the day went dark on me, I went looking for the Lord. All night my hand stayed stretched out, refusing to drop. My soul refused every comfort offered.

[3] I think about God — and I groan. I try to pray — and my spirit goes faint.

[4] He won't let my eyes close. I'm so undone I can't even put it into words.

[5] So I start counting backward — through the old days, through years buried deep.

[6] At night I remember the song I used to sing. I sit with my own heart and interrogate it. My spirit digs and digs:

[7] Is the Lord done with me forever? Will he never look my way again?

[8] Has his unfailing love finally failed? Has the promise gone silent for good?

[9] Did God forget how to be gracious? Did anger lock the door on his mercy?

[10] And I said: this is what wounds me — the strong right hand of the Most High has changed.

[11] But I will remember. I will remember what the Lord has done. I will call back to mind your wonders from long ago.

[12] I'll think through every act of yours. I'll turn your works over in my hands like stones.

[13] God, your way is holy ground. What god is great the way our God is great?

[14] You're the God who works wonders. You flexed your strength in front of the nations and they saw it.

[15] With one arm you bought your people back — the children of Jacob and Joseph.

[16] The waters saw you, God. The waters saw you and writhed. The deep itself shook to its core.

[17] Clouds emptied themselves like buckets. The sky cracked open with thunder. Your arrows shot in every direction.

[18] Your thunder rolled like chariots. Lightning lit up the world. The earth trembled and bucked.

[19] Your path cut straight through the sea. Your road ran across deep water. And no one could find your footprints afterward.

[20] You led your people like a flock — Moses and Aaron, the shepherds at the front.

[1] Listen, my people, to what I'm teaching. Lean in close to the words from my mouth.

[2] I'm opening my mouth in story and saying — I'm cracking open riddles buried since the beginning,

[3] the things we've heard, the things we know, the things our fathers told us.

[4] We won't hide it from their children. We'll tell the next generation the praises of the LORD, his power, the wonders he worked.

[5] He set a testimony in Jacob, planted a Torah in Israel — commanded our fathers to teach it to their children,

[6] so the next generation would know, even the children not yet born, so they could rise and tell their own children —

[7] so they would put their hope in God and not forget what he's done, but keep his commands.

[8] So they wouldn't be like their fathers — a generation stubborn and defiant, hearts that wouldn't hold steady, spirits that wouldn't stay faithful to God.

[9] The men of Ephraim, armed, carrying bows — they turned and ran on the day of battle.

[10] They didn't keep God's covenant. They refused to walk in his Torah.

[11] They forgot what he'd done — the wonders he'd shown them.

[12] In front of their fathers he worked the impossible, in Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.

[13] He split the sea and walked them through. He stacked the waters up like walls.

[14] He led them by a cloud in the day, by a fire's glow all through the night.

[15] He cracked open rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink as deep as the deep itself.

[16] He pulled streams out of stone — water cascading down like rivers.

[17] But still they kept on sinning against him — defying the Most High in the desert.

[18] They tested God in their hearts, demanding the food they were craving.

[19] They spoke against God: "Can God set a table out here in the wilderness?

[20] Sure, he struck the rock and water gushed and torrents flooded — but can he hand us bread too? Can he get meat for his people?"

[21] The LORD heard. Fury erupted. Fire blazed against Jacob. Wrath rose against Israel —

[22] because they didn't believe in God. They didn't trust his power to save.

[23] So he commanded the skies above. He threw open the doors of heaven.

[24] He rained manna down on them to eat — gave them grain straight from heaven.

[25] Mortals ate the bread of angels. He sent them food until they were stuffed.

[26] He stirred up the east wind in the heavens, drove the south wind by his strength.

[27] He rained meat on them like dust, birds with wings like the sand of the sea.

[28] He dropped them right in the middle of camp, all around their tents.

[29] They ate and gorged themselves — he gave them exactly what they wanted.

[30] But before they'd even swallowed their craving — while the food was still in their mouths —

[31] the wrath of God rose up against them. He cut down their strongest men. He felled the choice young men of Israel.

[32] And still — after all this — they kept sinning. They didn't believe his wonders.

[33] So he ended their days in futility, their years in sudden terror.

[34] When he killed them, they'd come looking for him — turn back, search for God in earnest.

[35] They'd remember God was their rock, the Most High God their redeemer.

[36] But it was just lips. They flattered him with their mouths. They lied to him with their tongues.

[37] Their hearts weren't fixed on him. They weren't faithful to his covenant.

[38] But he — full of mercy — covered over their guilt and didn't destroy them. Again and again he held back his anger. He didn't unleash all his wrath.

[39] He remembered they were flesh — a breath that blows past and doesn't come back.

[40] How many times they defied him in the wilderness — wounded him out there in the wasteland.

[41] Over and over they tested God. They provoked the Holy One of Israel.

[42] They didn't remember his hand — the day he ransomed them from the enemy,

[43] when he set his signs against Egypt, his wonders in the fields of Zoan.

[44] He turned their rivers to blood — they couldn't drink from their own streams.

[45] He sent swarms of flies that devoured them, frogs that ruined them.

[46] He handed their crops to the locust, their labor to the swarm.

[47] He killed their vines with hail, their sycamores with frost.

[48] He gave their cattle over to the hail, their livestock to lightning bolts.

[49] He unleashed on them the heat of his anger — fury, indignation, calamity — a delegation of destroying angels.

[50] He cleared a path for his anger. He didn't spare them from death. He handed their lives over to plague.

[51] He struck down every firstborn in Egypt — the first fruits of strength in the tents of Ham.

[52] But his own people he led out like sheep, guided them through the wilderness like a flock.

[53] He led them safely — they weren't afraid — and the sea swallowed their enemies.

[54] He brought them to his holy border, to this mountain his right hand had taken.

[55] He drove out nations before them, measured them out an inheritance with a line, settled the tribes of Israel in the tents of the conquered.

[56] But they tested God Most High and defied him. They didn't keep his statutes.

[57] They turned back — traitors like their fathers — twisted like a warped bow.

[58] They provoked him with their high places. They made him jealous with their carved gods.

[59] God heard. Fury. He utterly rejected Israel.

[60] He walked out of his tent at Shiloh — the dwelling he'd pitched among humans.

[61] He let his strength go into captivity, his glory into the hand of the enemy.

[62] He handed his people over to the sword — was furious with his own inheritance.

[63] Fire consumed their young men. Their young women had no wedding songs.

[64] Their priests fell by the sword. Their widows couldn't even weep.

[65] Then the Lord woke up — like a man waking, like a warrior sobering from wine.

[66] He drove his enemies back, broken, and shamed them forever.

[67] He rejected the tent of Joseph. He didn't choose the tribe of Ephraim.

[68] He chose the tribe of Judah — Mount Zion, the one he loved.

[69] He built his sanctuary like the heights of heaven, like the earth he founded forever.

[70] He chose David, his servant — took him from the sheep pens,

[71] brought him in from following the nursing ewes to shepherd Jacob his people, Israel his inheritance.

[72] And David shepherded them with a heart of integrity. With skillful hands, he led them.

[1] God — the nations have stormed into what belongs to you. They desecrated your holy temple. They reduced Jerusalem to rubble.

[2] They left the corpses of your servants as carrion for the sky's vultures, the flesh of your faithful for the wild dogs.

[3] They spilled blood like water all around Jerusalem, and no one was left to bury the dead.

[4] We've become a punchline to our neighbors, the laughingstock of everyone watching from the edges.

[5] How long, LORD? Will you stay furious forever? Will your jealousy keep burning like fire?

[6] Pour out your wrath on the nations who don't acknowledge you, on the kingdoms that refuse to call on your name —

[7] because they devoured Jacob and laid waste to his homeland.

[8] Don't keep tallying our ancestors' sins against us. Hurry — let your mercy reach us. We're hollowed out.

[9] Help us, God of our rescue, for the glory of your name. Pull us out, wipe our sins clean — for your name's sake.

[10] Why should the nations get to sneer, "Where is their God?" Let us watch — let the nations watch — as you avenge the blood of your servants poured out.

[11] Let the groaning of the prisoner reach you. By the strength of your arm, preserve those marked for death.

[12] Pay back our neighbors sevenfold, right into their laps, for the contempt they hurled at you, Lord.

[13] And we — your people, the flock you shepherd — we will thank you forever. From generation to generation, we will tell your praise.

[1] Shepherd of Israel, hear us — you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit enthroned above the cherubim, blaze forth. [2] In front of Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, wake up your power. Come and save us.

[3] God, bring us back. Let your face break into light over us, and we will be saved.

[4] Lord God of armies, how long will your anger smolder while your people pray? [5] You've fed them tears for bread and made them drink tears by the bowlful. [6] You've turned us into the argument of our neighbors, and our enemies laugh themselves sick.

[7] God of armies, bring us back. Let your face break into light over us, and we will be saved.

[8] You pulled a vine out of Egypt. You drove out nations and planted it. [9] You cleared the ground for it; it sank deep roots and filled the land. [10] Its shadow draped the mountains, its branches outreached the mighty cedars. [11] It sent its tendrils to the Sea, its shoots all the way to the River.

[12] So why have you torn down its walls, leaving every passerby free to pick it clean? [13] Wild boars from the forest tear at it. Whatever moves in the field feeds on it.

[14] God of armies — turn back! Look down from heaven and see. Tend to this vine, [15] the stock your right hand planted, the son you raised up for yourself. [16] It's been burned with fire, hacked down — they perish at the rebuke of your face.

[17] Lay your hand on the man at your right, the son of man you made strong for yourself. [18] Then we won't turn away from you again. Bring us back to life, and we'll call on your name.

[19] Lord God of armies, bring us back. Let your face break into light over us, and we will be saved.

[1] Sing out to God, our strength! Roar with joy to the God of Jacob!

[2] Strike up the music. Pound the tambourine. Sweet harp, soft lyre — bring them all.

[3] Blow the ram's horn at the new moon, and again when the moon is full, on the day of our feast.

[8] Listen, my people, and I will warn you. Israel — if only you would hear me!

[9] Let no foreign god live among you. Bow to no stranger's deity.

[10] I am the LORD your God, the one who hauled you up out of Egypt. Open your mouth wide — I will fill it.

[11] But my people wouldn't listen. Israel refused me.

[12] So I let them go — let them follow their stubborn hearts, let them walk their own crooked paths.

[13] If only my people would listen to me! If only Israel would walk my roads!

[14] I'd crush their enemies in a heartbeat. I'd turn my hand against their oppressors.

[15] Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him — and their doom would last forever.

[16] But you — I'd feed you the finest wheat. I'd satisfy you with honey straight from the rock."

[1] God rises in the divine assembly. Among the gods, he renders judgment:

[2] "How long will you twist justice? How long will you tip the scales for the wicked?

[3] Defend the weak. Defend the orphan. Vindicate the afflicted and the destitute.

[4] Rescue the weak and the needy. Tear them from the grip of the wicked."

[5] They know nothing. They understand nothing. They wander in the dark while the foundations of the earth shudder beneath them.

[8] Rise, O God. Judge the earth. Every nation belongs to you.

[1] God — don't stay quiet. Don't hold your peace. Don't sit still, God.

[2] Look — your enemies are roaring. The ones who hate you are lifting their heads.

[13] My God — turn them into tumbleweed, into chaff scattering before the wind.

[14] Like fire devouring a forest, like flame setting mountains ablaze —

[15] chase them down with your storm. Terrify them with your hurricane.

[16] Fill their faces with shame, God, until they come looking for your name.

[17] Let them be humiliated and panicked forever. Let them die in disgrace —

[18] so they finally know that you alone — your name is the LORD — you alone are Most High over all the earth.

[1] How beautiful the place where you live, Lord of every army.

[2] My soul aches — actually aches — to step into your courts. My whole self, flesh and breath together, cries out for the living God.

[3] Even the sparrow finds a house. Even the swallow builds a nest where she can lay her young — right there at your altars, Lord of armies, my King, my God.

[4] How fortunate they are, the ones who live in your house. They never stop singing your praise. *Selah.*

[5] How fortunate the one whose strength is anchored in you, whose heart is already walking the road to Zion.

[6] Passing through the Valley of Tears, they turn it into a place of springs — the early rain blankets it in pools of blessing.

[7] They go from strength to strength, every one of them appearing before God in Zion.

[8] Lord, God of armies — hear my prayer. Listen, God of Jacob. *Selah.*

by tens of thousands in our fields.

[14] Our oxen will haul heavy loads.
No breach in the wall, no one carried off,
no scream rising in our streets.

[15] Blessed are the people who live like this.
Blessed are the people whose God is the LORD.

[1] I will lift you high, my God, my King. I will bless your name forever, and forever after that.

[2] Every day I will bless you. I will praise your name forever, and forever after that.

[3] The LORD is vast — beyond measure, beyond praise. His greatness has no floor, no ceiling, no edge to reach.

[4] One generation will hand your works to the next, will tell of your mighty acts.

[5] I will turn over in my mind the radiant splendor of your majesty, and the wonders you have done.

[6] They will speak of the terrifying force of your works, and I will recount your greatness.

[7] They will spill out the memory of your overflowing goodness and sing your justice with joy.

[8] The LORD is tender and full of mercy, slow to anger, abundant in steadfast love.

[9] The LORD is good to all. His compassion rests on everything he has made.

[10] All your works will give you thanks, LORD. Your faithful ones will bless you.

[11] They will speak of the glory of your kingdom and tell of your power,

[12] making your mighty acts known to the children of men, and the dazzling splendor of your reign.

[13] Your kingdom is a kingdom of all the ages. Your rule stretches across every generation that ever was or will be. The LORD is faithful in every word he speaks and gracious in everything he does.

[14] The LORD steadies all who are falling. He lifts every back that has been bent low.

[15] Every eye watches you in hope, and you give them their food in its proper time.

[16] You open your hand and satisfy the longing of every living thing.

[17] The LORD is just in all his ways and gracious in all his works.

[18] The LORD stands close to all who call on him — to all who call on him in truth.

[19] He fulfills the desire of those who fear him. He hears their cry and saves them.

[20] The LORD guards all who love him, but the wicked — every last one — he will destroy.

[21] My mouth will speak the praise of the LORD. Let every creature bless his holy name forever, and forever after that.

[1] Praise the LORD.

Praise the LORD, O my soul.

[2] I will praise the LORD as long as I draw breath. I will sing to my God with every heartbeat I have left.

[3] Don't stake your life on princes. Don't trust mortals to save you — they can't.

[4] One breath leaves them and they're back in the dirt. That very day, every plan they had dies with them.

[5] But blessed is the one whose help is the God of Jacob — whose hope rests on the LORD their God,

[6] the Maker of sky and earth and sea and everything in them, the One who keeps faith forever,

[7] who delivers justice to the crushed, who feeds the starving, who unlocks the prison door.

[8] The LORD gives sight to the blind. The LORD lifts up those bent double. The LORD loves the ones living right.

[9] The LORD watches over the foreigner, sustains the orphan and the widow — but he twists the path of the wicked into ruin.

[10] The LORD reigns forever. Your God, Zion, through every generation.

Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise the LORD. How good it is to sing to our God, how lovely, how fitting this song of praise.

[2] The LORD is rebuilding Jerusalem, gathering Israel's exiles home.

[3] He binds up the broken-hearted and dresses every wound.

[4] He counts the stars, every last one, and calls each by name.

[5] Our Lord is vast. His power, staggering. His understanding has no edge, no end.

[6] He lifts the humble off the ground and drives the wicked into the dirt.

[7] Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving. Strike up the harp for our God.

[8] He drapes the sky with clouds, sends rain to the earth, makes grass leap up on the hills.

[9] He feeds the cattle. He hears the raven chicks crying and answers.

[10] He doesn't thrill at the warhorse's strength or the runner's powerful legs.

[11] What pleases the LORD is this: those who revere him, those who stake everything on his unfailing love.

[12] Sing his praise, Jerusalem. Glorify your God, Zion.

[13] He has reinforced the bars of your gates and blessed the children inside your walls.

[14] He keeps your borders peaceful and fills you with the finest wheat.

[15] He sends his command to the earth — his word races out at full speed.

[16] He scatters snow like wool and spreads frost like ash.

[17] He hurls down hail like crumbs of bread — who can stand his cold?

[18] He speaks again, and it all melts. He stirs the wind, and waters flow.

[19] He gave his word to Jacob, his statutes and laws to Israel.

[20] He has done this for no other nation. They don't know his commands. Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise the LORD!
Praise him from the heavens —
praise him in the heights!

[2] Praise him, all his messengers.
Praise him, all his armies of light.

[3] Praise him, sun and moon.
Praise him, every shining star.

[4] Praise him, highest heavens,
and you waters above the sky.

[5] Let them all praise the name of the LORD —
he spoke, and they leapt into being.

[6] He set them in their places forever,
fixed them by a decree that will not break.

[7] Praise the LORD from the earth —
sea monsters and every ocean deep,

[8] fire and hail, snow and mist,
storm wind doing what he says,

[9] mountains and every hill,
fruit trees and every cedar,

[10] wild beasts and all the cattle,
crawling things and birds in flight,

[11] kings of the earth and every nation,
princes and every judge on the planet,

[12] young men and young women too,
old ones and children together —

[13] let them praise the name of the LORD,
for his name alone stands above all.
His splendor blazes over earth and sky.

[14] He has lifted up a horn for his people —
the song of every one of his faithful,
the children of Israel, the people pressed close to his heart.

Praise the LORD!

[1] Sing to the LORD a song never sung before — let his praise rise in the gathering of the faithful. [2] Let Israel rejoice in the One who made them. Let Zion's children erupt in joy over their King. [3] Let them praise his name with dancing, strike up music on tambourine and harp. [4] The LORD takes delight in his people. He crowns the humble with rescue. [5] Let the faithful celebrate this glory — let them shout for joy even from their beds. [6] Praises of God in their throats, a double-edged sword in their hands — [7] to bring reckoning on the nations, to call the peoples to account, [8] to bind their kings in chains, their nobles in iron shackles, [9] to carry out the sentence already written against them. This is the honor given to all his faithful ones. Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise God in his holy place. Praise him in the vault of his power.

[2] Praise him for the deeds that thunder. Praise him for greatness without measure.

[3] Praise him with the blast of the ram's horn. Praise him with harp and lyre.

[4] Praise him with tambourine and dance. Praise him with strings and pipe.

[5] Praise him with cymbals that ring. Praise him with cymbals that crash.

[6] Let everything that breathes — praise the Lord. Praise the Lord.

[1] This is the unveiling of Jesus the Anointed, given to him by God to show his servants what is about to break loose. He sent his messenger to deliver it to his servant John, [2] who has testified to everything he saw — the word of God, the witness of Jesus the Anointed, all of it. [3] Whoever reads these words out loud — blessed. Whoever hears them and holds onto what is written here — blessed. The time is right on top of us.

[4] John, to the seven assemblies in the province of Asia:

Grace to you and peace from the One who is, who was, and who is coming — and from the seven Spirits before his throne — [5] and from Jesus the Anointed, the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, the ruler of every king on earth.

To the One who loves us and tore us free from our sins with his own blood, [6] who made us a kingdom, priests serving his God and Father — to him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.

[7] Look up — he is coming on the clouds. Every eye will see him, even the ones who ran him through. Every tribe on earth will beat their chests and wail. Yes. Amen.

[8] "I am the Alpha and the Omega," says the Lord God — the One who is, who was, and who is coming. The Almighty.

[9] I, John — your brother, sharing with you in the pressure and the kingdom and the patient endurance that come with Jesus — I was on the island called Patmos, exiled there for the word of God and the witness of Jesus. [10] On the Lord's day the Spirit took hold of me, and behind me I heard a voice like a war trumpet [11] saying: "Write down what you see in a scroll and send it to the seven assemblies — Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea."

[12] I turned to see the voice that was speaking to me, and when I turned I saw seven golden lampstands, [13] and standing among the lampstands one like a son of man — robed to his feet, a gold sash across his chest. [14] His head and his hair white as wool, white as snow. His eyes — fire. [15] His feet like bronze glowing in a furnace. His voice like the roar of deep water. [16] In his right hand, seven stars. From his mouth, a sharp double-edged sword. His face — the sun blazing at full strength.

[17] When I saw him, I dropped at his feet like a corpse. He laid his right hand on me and said:

"Stop being afraid. I am the First and the Last. [18] I am the Living One. I was dead — and look, I am alive forever and ever. And I hold the keys to death and the grave.

[19] "So write down what you have seen, what is happening now, and what is about to happen after this. [20] Here is the secret of the seven stars you saw in my right hand, and of the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are the messengers of the seven assemblies, and the seven lampstands are the assemblies themselves."

[1] To the messenger of the church in Ephesus, write this:

These are the words of the One who holds seven stars in his right hand, who walks among seven golden lampstands:

[2] I know what you've done. I know your labor, your endurance. I know you cannot stomach the corrupt — that you tested those who called themselves apostles and exposed them as frauds. [3] You've held the line. You've borne up under pressure for my name and refused to quit.

[4] But I have this against you: you've abandoned the love you had at the start. [5] Remember how high you were. Turn back. Do again what you did then. If you don't, I'm coming for you — and your lampstand comes off its stand.

[6] One thing in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans do. So do I.

[7] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: To the one who conquers, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life that grows in the paradise of God.

[8] To the messenger of the church in Smyrna, write this:

These are the words of the First and the Last, the One who died and came back:

[9] I know your suffering. I know your poverty — though you are rich. I know the slander leveled against you by those who call themselves Jews and are nothing of the kind. They are a synagogue of Satan.

[10] Don't be afraid of what's coming. The devil is about to throw some of you in prison to test you, and you'll suffer for ten days. Stay faithful — even if it kills you — and I will hand you the crown of life.

[11] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: The one who conquers walks away untouched by the second death.

[12] To the messenger of the church in Pergamum, write this:

These are the words of the One whose mouth wields a sharp two-edged sword:

[13] I know where you live — right where Satan's throne sits. And still you cling to my name. Even when Antipas, my faithful witness, was executed in your city — Satan's own backyard — you did not deny me.

[14] But I have a few things against you. Some of you cling to the teaching of Balaam, who showed Balak how to trip up Israel — luring them into idol-feasts and sexual sin. [15] You also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans. [16] Turn back. Now. Or I'm coming, and I'll fight them with the sword from my mouth.

[17] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: To the one who conquers, I will give the hidden manna. I will give a white stone, and on it a new name — known to no one except the one who receives it.

[18] To the messenger of the church in Thyatira, write this:

These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes blaze like fire, whose feet shine like burnished bronze:

[19] I know what you've done — your love, your faith, your service, your endurance. And your latest works exceed the first.

[20] But I have this against you: you tolerate that woman Jezebel — the one calling herself a prophet — who teaches my servants and seduces them into sexual sin and idol-feasts. [21] I gave her time to turn. She refuses. [22] So watch: I am throwing her onto a sickbed. And those who sleep with her — unless they repent of what she's done — I'll throw into deep suffering. [23] Her children I will strike dead. Then every church will know: I am the One who searches hearts and minds, and I will give back to each of you according to what you've done.

[24] But to the rest of you in Thyatira — those who haven't swallowed this teaching, who haven't dabbled in what they call "the deep things of Satan" — I lay no other burden on you. [25] Only hold on to what you have until I come.

[26] To the one who conquers and keeps doing my work to the end, I will give authority over the nations — [27] he will shepherd them with an iron rod, shattering them like clay pots — [28] the same authority I received from my Father. And I will give him the morning star.

[29] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches.

[1] To the messenger of the assembly in Sardis, write:

These are the words of the One who holds the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars: I know what you've done. You have a reputation for being alive. You're dead.

[2] Wake up. Strengthen whatever's left before it dies too — because I have not found your works complete in the sight of my God.

[3] Remember what you received, what you heard. Hold onto it. Repent. If you won't wake up, I'll come like a thief, and you won't know the hour I arrive at your door.

[4] But a few names in Sardis haven't soiled their clothes. They will walk with me in white, because they are worthy.

[5] The one who conquers will be dressed in white. I will never erase that name from the book of life. I will speak that name out loud before my Father and his angels.

[6] If you have ears, hear what the Spirit is saying to the assemblies.

[7] To the messenger of the assembly in Philadelphia, write:

These are the words of the Holy One, the True One, the One who holds the key of David — what he opens no one shuts, what he shuts no one opens:

[8] I know what you've done. Look — I've set an open door in front of you that no one can close. You have only a little strength, but you've kept my word and you have not denied my name.

[9] Watch what I do with the synagogue of Satan — those who claim to be Jews but are lying. I will make them come and bow at your feet and finally see that I have loved you.

[10] Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you through the hour of testing that's coming on the whole world to expose everyone who lives on the earth.

[11] I'm coming soon. Hold tight to what you have. Don't let anyone strip you of your crown.

[12] The one who conquers — I'll make a pillar in the temple of my God. They'll never leave it. I'll write on them the name of my God, and the name of my God's city, the new Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from my God, and my own new name.

[13] If you have ears, hear what the Spirit is saying to the assemblies.

[14] To the messenger of the assembly in Laodicea, write:

These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the origin of God's creation:

[15] I know what you've done. You're neither cold nor hot. I wish you were one or the other.

[16] But because you're lukewarm — neither hot nor cold — I am about to spit you out of my mouth.

[17] You keep saying, "I'm rich, I've made it, I need nothing." You have no idea that you are the wretched one, the pitiful one, poor and blind and naked.

[18] So here's my counsel: buy from me gold refined in fire so you can actually be rich, and white clothes to cover the shame of your nakedness, and salve for your eyes so you can finally see.

[19] The ones I love — I confront them. I discipline them. So burn with seriousness. Repent.

[20] Look — I'm standing at the door. I'm knocking. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in, and we will eat together — them with me, me with them.

[21] The one who conquers — I'll give them a seat with me on my throne, just as I conquered and sat down with my Father on his throne.

[22] If you have ears, hear what the Spirit is saying to the assemblies.

[1] After this I looked, and there it was—a door standing open in heaven. And the voice I had heard before, the one like a trumpet blast, called to me: "Come up here. I'll show you what has to happen next."

[2] Instantly I was in the Spirit. And there before me stood a throne in heaven, with Someone seated on it.

[3] The One on the throne blazed like jasper and carnelian, and a rainbow circled the throne, glowing like emerald.

[4] Around the throne stood twenty-four other thrones, and on them sat twenty-four elders robed in white, crowns of gold on their heads.

[5] From the throne came lightning, rumbling, thunder. Seven torches of fire burned in front of it—the seven Spirits of God.

[6] And before the throne stretched something like a sea of glass, clear as crystal. In the center, around the throne, were four living creatures, covered with eyes—front and back.

[7] The first like a lion. The second like an ox. The third with the face of a man. The fourth like an eagle in flight.

[8] Each of the four had six wings, and they were studded with eyes, inside and out. Day and night they never stop saying:

"Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty,
who was, and is, and is coming."

[9] And whenever the living creatures give glory and honor and thanks to the One seated on the throne—the One who lives forever and ever—

[10] the twenty-four elders fall down before Him. They worship the One who lives forever. They cast their crowns before the throne and cry out:

[11] "You are worthy, our Lord and God,
to receive glory and honor and power.
For You created all things—
by Your will they exist,
by Your will they were made."

[1] Then I saw it—in the right hand of the One on the throne—a scroll, written front and back, sealed shut with seven seals.

[2] And a mighty angel was calling out, voice ringing across everything: "Who is worthy to break the seals and open the scroll?"

[3] Silence. No one in heaven, no one on earth, no one beneath the earth could open it. No one could even look inside.

[4] I wept. I wept hard, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to see what it held.

[5] Then one of the elders said to me, "Stop weeping. Look—the Lion from Judah's tribe, the Root of David, has triumphed. He can open the scroll and break its seven seals."

[6] So I looked. And there, at the center of the throne, surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders—a Lamb. Standing. Slaughtered. With seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent into all the earth.

[7] He stepped forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the One on the throne.

[8] The moment He took it, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell facedown before the Lamb. Each one held a harp and a golden bowl full of incense—the prayers of the holy ones, rising.

[9] And they sang a new song:

"You are worthy to take the scroll
 and break its seals,
because You were slaughtered,
and with Your blood You bought for God
people from every tribe and tongue,
every nation and race—

[10] You made them a kingdom and priests for our God,
and they will reign over the earth."

[11] Then I looked, and I heard the voices of countless angels surrounding the throne and the living creatures and the elders—thousands upon thousands, ten thousand times ten thousand—

[12] thundering together:

"Worthy is the Lamb who was slaughtered—
worthy to receive power and wealth
and wisdom and strength,
honor and glory and blessing!"

[13] And then every creature—in heaven, on earth, under the earth, in the sea, everything that breathes—I heard them all, together:

"To the One on the throne and to the Lamb:
blessing and honor and glory and power
forever and ever!"

[14] And the four living creatures said, "Amen." And the elders fell down and worshiped.

[1] I watched as the Lamb broke the first of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures speak with a voice like thunder: "Come."

[2] I looked, and there — a white horse. Its rider held a bow, was handed a crown, and rode out conquering, bent on conquest.

[3] He broke the second seal, and I heard the second living creature say: "Come."

[4] Out came another horse, this one fiery red. Its rider was given authority to strip peace from the earth, to make people slaughter each other. A great sword was placed in his hand.

[5] He broke the third seal, and I heard the third living creature say: "Come." I looked, and there — a black horse. Its rider held a pair of scales.

[6] And from somewhere among the four living creatures I heard a voice: "A day's wages for a loaf of bread. A day's wages for three loaves of barley. But the oil and the wine — leave them alone."

[7] He broke the fourth seal, and I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say: "Come."

[8] I looked, and there — a sickly pale horse. Its rider was named Death, and Hades trailed close behind him. They were given authority over a quarter of the earth — to kill by sword, by famine, by plague, by the wild beasts of the land.

[9] He broke the fifth seal, and I saw beneath the altar the souls of those who had been slaughtered for the word of God and for the testimony they had held to.

[10] They cried out with a great voice: "How long, Sovereign Lord — holy, true — until you judge? Until you avenge our blood on the people of the earth?"

[11] Each was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until the full number of their fellow servants and brothers and sisters — those marked for death just as they had been — was complete.

[12] Then I watched him break the sixth seal. A massive earthquake shook the earth. The sun went black like sackcloth made of hair. The whole moon turned the color of blood.

[13] The stars of heaven dropped to the earth like a fig tree shaking off unripe figs in a hard wind.

[14] The sky peeled back like a scroll rolling up. Every mountain and island was wrenched from its place.
